Ayahs

الٓر ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۱)
Aif, Lam, Ra. These are the signs of the Manifest Book. (1)
إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَٰنًا عَرَبِيًّا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۲)
Indeed We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran so that you may exercise your reason. (2)

Key Points

Various views have been expressed regarding the disconnected letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah), including: 1. The Qur'an, this Divine miracle, is composed of these very letters of the alphabet, which are available to everyone. 2. It is the name of the Surah that begins with those letters. 3. It is a type of Divine oath or swearing. 4. They are secrets and codes between Allah and the Prophet. However, considering that out of the 29 Surahs that begin with the disconnected letters, in twenty-four cases, the discussion immediately following them is about the Qur'an and its miraculous nature, perhaps the best interpretation is the first view.

Regardless of the language in which the Qur'an was revealed, others would have had to become familiar with that language. However, the revelation of the Qur'an in the Arabic language has advantages, including: A: The Arabic language possesses such a vastness of vocabulary and firmness of grammatical rules that is not found in other languages. B: According to narrations, the language of the people of Paradise is Arabic. C: The people of the region where the Qur'an was revealed were Arabic speakers, and it was impossible for their heavenly book to be in another language.

Allah has used the term "revelation" (Nuzul) for both the Qur'an and rain. We mention the similarities between the two: A: Both descend from the sky. "و انزلنا من السماء ماءً" (3) B: Both are pure and purifying. "ینزّل علیكم من السماء ماءً لیطهّركم" (4), "ربّنا وابعث فیهم رسولا... یعلّمهم الكتاب و الحكمة و یزكیّهم" (5) C: Both are means of life. "دعاكم لِما یحییكم" (6), "لنحیى به بلدة میتا" (7) D: Both are blessed and sources of blessing. "و هذا كتاب انزلناه مبارك" (8), "و نزّلنا من السماء ماء مباركاً" (9) E: The Qur'an, like rain, was revealed drop by drop and Ayah by Ayah (gradual revelation).

Perhaps the emphasis on the Qur'an being Arabic is a refutation of those who claimed that a non-Arab taught the Qur'an to the Prophet. "...انّهم یقولون انّما یعلّمه بشر لسان الّذى یلحدون الیه اعجمى و هذا لسان عربىّ مبین" (10)

3) Al-Mu’minun, 18. 4) Al-Anfal, 11. 5) Al-Baqarah, 129. 6) Al-Anfal, 24. 7) Al-Furqan, 49. 8) Al-An’am 92. 9) Qaf 9. 10) An-Nahl, 103.

Messages

  1. The Qur'an is composed of these very letters of the alphabet (Alif, Lam, Ra, ...). If you can, bring forth a book like the Qur'an using these same letters. "الر"

  2. The Qur'an possesses a very high status. "تلك"

  3. The Qur'an, at the outset, informs the addressee and the reader about the firmness of its verses, and then proceeds to state other matters. "آیات الكتاب المبین"*

  4. The revelation of the Qur'an in the Arabic language on the one hand, and the command to reflect upon it on the other, indicates that Muslims must become familiar with the Arabic language. "قرآناً عربیّاً لعلّكم تعقلون"

  5. The Qur'an is not only for recitation and seeking blessing (tabarruk), but it is a means for human intellect and growth. "لعلّكم تعقلون"

  6. Writings must be clear and illuminating, purposeful and guiding. "الكتاب المبین... لعلّكم تعقلون"*

  7. To achieve felicity, the mere revelation of the Qur'an is not enough; rather, one must also use intellect regarding it. "انزلناه... لعلّكم تعقلون"*

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Yusuf Surah Yusuf is one of the Makkan Surahs and contains one hundred and eleven verses. The name of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) is mentioned 27 times in the Qur'an, 25 of which are in this very Surah. The verses of this Surah are interconnected and, in several attractive and concise sections, present the story of Yusuf's life from childhood until his attainment of the position of treasurer of Egypt, his chastity and purity, the neutralization of various plots against him, and manifestations of Divine power. The story of Prophet Yusuf عليه السلام is mentioned only in this Surah of the Qur'an, whereas the stories of other prophets are narrated in multiple Surahs. (1) The story of Prophet Yusuf is also narrated in the Torah, the Book of Genesis, from chapter 37 to 50, but in comparison with what is presented in the Qur'an, the authenticity of the Qur'an and the distortion of the Torah become clearly evident. Among literary works, the story of Yusuf and Zulaikha holds a distinguished place, including the epic poem Yusuf and Zulaikha by Nizami Ganjavi and Yusuf and Zulaikha attributed to Ferdowsi. In the story of Yusuf عليه السلام, the Qur'an focuses more on his own personality as he navigates the turmoil of events, whereas in the stories of other prophets, it mostly refers to the fate of the opponents, their obstinacy, and their destruction. In some narrations, the teaching of Surah Yusuf to women and girls has been prohibited, but according to some scholars, these narrations do not have reliable documentation. (2) Furthermore, the basis for this prohibition is the presentation of love by Zulaikha, the wife of the Aziz of Egypt, which, in the Qur'anic narrative, contains no negative point.

1) Tafsir Hada'iq. 2) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْقَصَصِ بِمَآ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ وَإِن كُنتَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمِنَ ٱلْغَـٰفِلِينَ (۳)
We will recount to you the best of narratives in what We have revealed to you of this Quran, and indeed prior to it you were among those who are unaware [of it]. (3)

Key Points

Qasas (قصص) means both story and the narration of a story. Storytelling and narrative play a significant role in human education. This is because a story is the tangible embodiment of a nation's life and the practical experience of a people. History is the mirror of nations, and the more familiar we are with the history and lives of our predecessors, the more it is as if we have lived the lifespan of those people. Imam Ali عليه السلام, in Letter 31 of Nahj al-Balaghah addressed to his son Imam Hassan عليه السلام, has a statement where He says: My son! I have studied the lives of the past generations and am so aware of them that it is as if I have lived with them and have lived as long as they did.

Perhaps one of the reasons for the impact of stories and narratives on humans is their inherent inclination towards them. Usually, historical books and narrative works have enjoyed special popularity throughout the history of human culture and have been understandable and accessible to most people, whereas only a small group pursued rational and argumentative discussions.

In narrations, the entire Qur'an is referred to as Ahsan al-Qasas (the Best of Stories), and there is no contradiction in the Qur'an being Ahsan al-Qasas among the heavenly books, and this specific Surah being Ahsan al-Qasas among the Surahs of the Qur'an. (11)

Differences between Quranic stories and other stories: 1. The Storyteller is Allah. «نحن نقصّ» 2. It is purposeful. «نقصّ علیك من انباء الرّسل مانثبّت به فؤادك» (We relate to you the accounts of the Messengers to strengthen your heart thereby) (12) 3. It is truth, not imagination. «نحن نقصّ علیك نبأهم بالحقّ» (We relate to you their story in truth) (13) 4. It is based on knowledge, not conjecture. «فلنقصنّ علیهم بعلمٍ» (We shall certainly relate to them with knowledge) (14) 5. It is a means of reflection, not stupefaction. «فاقصص القصص لعلّهم یتفكّرون» (So relate the stories, perhaps they may reflect) (15) 6. It is a means of lesson-taking ('ibrah), not entertainment or amusement. «لقد كان فى قصصهم عبرة» (There was certainly a lesson in their stories) (16)

The story of Prophet Joseph is Ahsan al-Qasas (the Best of Stories) because: 1. It is the most authentic of stories. «بما اوحینا» (by Our revelation) 2. This story addresses the struggle against the self (Jihad al-Nafs), which is the greatest struggle. 3. The hero of the story is a youth who possesses all human perfections (patience, faith, piety, chastity, trustworthiness, wisdom, forgiveness, and benevolence). 4. All characters in the story achieve a good outcome. For example, Joseph attains governance, the brothers repent, the father regains his sight, the famine-stricken country is saved, and sorrow and jealousy turn into reunion and affection. 5. In this story, a collection of opposites are presented side-by-side: separation and reunion, sorrow and joy, famine and abundance, loyalty and treachery, owner and owned, well and palace, poverty and wealth, slavery and sovereignty, blindness and sight, chastity and false accusation.

Not only are the divine stories Ahsan (best), but all of Allah's works are Ahsan. This is because: 1. He is the Best of Creators. «احسن الخالقین» (17) 2. He possesses the Best Book. «نزّل احسن الحدیث» (18) 3. He possesses the Best Religion. «و من احسن دیناً ممّن اسلم وجهه لله» (19) 4. He gives the Best Reward. «لیجزیهم اللَّه احسن ما عملوا» (20)

And in return for these best things, Allah has demanded the best action from humanity. «لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملاً» (21)

Ghaflah (heedlessness/unawareness) is presented in the Qur'an in three meanings: A: Bad Ghaflah, such as the Ayah: «و انّ كثیراً من النّاس عن آیاتنا لغافلون» (And indeed, many among mankind are heedless of Our signs.) (22) B: Good Ghaflah, such as the Ayah: «انّ الّذین یرمون المحصنات الغافلات المؤمنات لعنوا فى الدنیا و الآخرة» (Indeed, those who accuse chaste, unaware [of indecency], believing women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter.) (23) C: Natural Ghaflah, meaning lack of information, similar to the Ayah under discussion: «و ان كنت من قبله لمن الغافلین» (and indeed you were before it among the unaware), where Allah, to preserve the dignity and respect of the Prophet, does not say: «كنت من قبله لمن الجاهلین» (you were before it among the ignorant).

11) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq. 12) Hud, 120. 13) Al-Kahf, 13. 14) Al-A'raf, 7. 15) Al-A'raf, 176. 16) Yusuf, 111. 17) Al-Mu'minun, 14. 18) Al-Zumar, 23. 19) Al-Nisa, 125. 20) Al-Nur, 38. 21) Hud, 7. 22) Yunus, 92. 23) Al-Nur, 23.

Messages

1- In the stories of the Qur'an, the Storyteller is Allah. «نحن نقصّ»

2- The Storyteller (and its author) must be reliable and trustworthy. «نحن نقصّ»*

3- Psychologically and educationally, preparation must be made for better impact. (Before starting the story, He prepares the ground by saying, 'I am going to tell a story, and the best story at that.') «نحن نقصّ علیك احسن القصص»*

4- The best words are entrusted to the best individuals. «نحن نقصّ... احسن القصص»*

5- To provide role models for others, we must select and introduce the best ones. «احسن... »

6- The Qur'an recounts stories with the best and most beautiful expression. «احسن القصص»

7- The best story is that which is based on revelation. «احسن القصص بما اوحینا»

8- The unlettered Prophet, prior to the revelation, was unaware and unfamiliar with past history. «...لمن الغافلین»

9- The Qur'an removes heedlessness and lack of knowledge. «من قبله لمن الغافلین»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَ يُوسُفُ لِأَبِيهِ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّى رَأَيْتُ أَحَدَ عَشَرَ كَوْكَبًا وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِى سَـٰجِدِينَ (٤)
When Joseph said to his father, ‘Father! I saw eleven planets, and the sun and the moon: I saw them prostrating themselves before me,’ (4)

Key Points

24) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 25) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 441. 26) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 11, p. 299. 27) Al-Anfal, 43. 28) Al-Fath, 27. 29) As-Saffat, 10.

Messages

1- Children must trust the wisdom and compassion of their father and consult with him regarding life matters. «قال یوسف لابیه»*

2- We should not call the father by his name. «یا اَبت»

3- Parents are the best reference for solving the problems of their children. «یا اَبت»

4- Fathers and children, when addressing each other, should use words that signify intimacy, mercy, and compassion. «یا اَبت»*

5- Parents should pay attention to the dreams of their children. «یا اَبت»

6- Prophet Yusuf initially did not know dream interpretation, and thus sought help from his father to interpret his vision. «یا اَبت»

7- Sometimes visions and dreams are one of the ways to receive truths. «انّى رأیت»

8- In the culture of dreams, objects become symbols of realities. (For example, the sun symbolizes the father, the moon symbolizes the mother, and the stars symbolize the brothers.) «رأیت احد عشر كوكباً...»

9- Seeing the moon, the sun, and the stars (simultaneously) is among the wonders of Yusuf's dream. «كوكباً و الشمس و القمر»*

10- The dreams of the friends of Allah are real. The word «رأیتُ» (I saw) is repeated in this verse to emphasize that he certainly saw it, lest the incident be thought imaginary.

11- Yusuf عليه السلام saw in his dream that the sun, the moon, and the stars were prostrating to him, just like conscious beings. (The phrase «رأیتهم» is used for conscious beings)*

12- Chosen individuals reach a status where they become the object of prostration for other humans. «رأیتهم لى ساجدین»*

13- Sometimes a teenager possesses a talent that compels elders to show humility. «ساجدین »

14- Starting a story with a vision and ending it with its interpretation is one of the best methods for writing a story or screenplay. «احسن القصص - رأیتهم لى ساجدین»*

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰبُنَىَّ لَا تَقْصُصْ رُءْيَاكَ عَلَىٰٓ إِخْوَتِكَ فَيَكِيدُوا۟ لَكَ كَيْدًا ۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (٥)
he said, ‘My son, do not recount your dream to your brothers, lest they should devise schemes against you. Satan is indeed man’s manifest enemy. (5)

Key Points

One of the principles of life is keeping secrets (confidentiality). If Muslims had acted upon the content of this Ayah, all our manuscripts, scientific works, artistic artifacts, and antiques would not have ended up in foreign museums, and they would not have become aware of our resources, capabilities, and interests under the guise of experts, diplomats, and tourists. Furthermore, due to simplicity (naivety) or betrayal, our secrets would not have been placed in the hands of those who are constantly plotting and scheming against us. Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) told his dream to his father away from the sight of his brothers, which itself is a sign of his sharp intelligence.

Messages

  1. Address children kindly. «یا بنىّ»*
  2. Parents must have an intimate relationship with their children so that the children can share their thoughts with them. «یا بنىَّ»*
  3. We must warn children about the danger of envy (jealousy) in the home and society. «یا بنىّ لا تقصص...»*
  4. Teach children confidentiality from childhood and within the family. «یا بنىّ لا تقصص»*
  5. Knowledge and information must be classified, separating confidential from non-confidential. «لا تقصص»
  6. Do not tell every word to everyone. «لا تقصص»
  7. Concealing virtues to prevent envy is a necessary action. «لا تقصص»*
  8. Upon recognizing the talent of one child, do not discuss it with the other children. «لا تقصص»*
  9. Caution must be exercised when introducing sharp-witted and brilliant individuals. «لا تقصص»*
  10. Prophet Ya’qub (Jacob) also possessed the knowledge and gift of dream interpretation. «لا تقصص رؤیاك»*
  11. Sometimes the devil is close at hand. «اخوتك»*
  12. Prevention is better than cure. (Not telling the dream to the brothers is a form of prevention against provoking envy.) «لا تقصص رؤیاك على اخوتك فیكیدوا»*
  13. Even in the families of the Prophets, anti-ethical issues such as envy and plotting arise among the children. «یا بنىّ لا تقصص... فیكیدوا...»
  14. Do not ignite the grounds for envy. «لا تقصص... فیكیدوا»
  15. Envy causes rights, even the right of kinship, to be disregarded. «لا تقصص... فیكیدوا»*
  16. Yusuf’s brothers, علیه السلام, acted as they did even though they had no knowledge of his dream or his future status. What would they have done if they had known?! «لا تقصص... فیكیدوا»*
  17. Foresight and future planning are valuable traits. «لا تقصص... فیكیدوا»*
  18. Revealing secrets and certain news can prove very costly. «لا تقصص... فیكیدوا»*
  19. Some secrets are so important that their disclosure endangers the life of an individual or individuals. «فیكیدوا لك كیداً»*
  20. In important and sensitive situations, the danger must be warned against before it occurs. «فیكیدوا لك كیداً»*
  21. Prophet Ya’qub was certain regarding the plotting of the brothers against Yusuf. «فیكیدوا لك كیداً»*
  22. In predicting important matters, sometimes expressing suspicion or revealing character flaws is permissible. «فَیَكیدوا لَك كَیدا»
  23. It is necessary for parents to be aware of the temperaments of their children toward one another so that they can exercise complete management. «فَیَكیدُوا لَك كَیداً»
  24. Plotting and scheming are satanic acts. «فیكیدوا...انَّ الشَیْطان»
  25. Satan gains dominance over us by exploiting internal predispositions. (The brothers' envy provided the ground for the manifestation of Satan's enmity toward man.) «فیكیدوا ... انّ الشیطان للانسان عدو مبین»
  26. Satan is the enemy of man, even if he is the son of a Prophet. «انّ الشیطان للانسان عدوّ مبین»*
  27. Introduce Satan to children from childhood and explain his role. «انّ الشیطان للانسان عدوّ مبین»*

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰٓ ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَآ أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰٓ أَبَوَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (٦)
That is how your Lord will choose you, and teach you the interpretation of dreams, and complete His blessing upon you and upon the house of Jacob, just as He completed it earlier for your fathers, Abraham and Isaac. Your Lord is indeed all-knowing and all-wise.’ (6)

Key Points

The interpretation (ta'wil) of a dream means explaining its inner reality (bāṭin) and the manner of its realization. "Aḥādīth" is the plural of "ḥadīth," meaning the narration of an event. Since a person narrates his vision (ru'yā) to others, the dream itself has also been called ḥadīth. In this Ayah, Prophet Ya'qūb interprets the dream of his son Yūsuf for him and informs him of his future. Although the apparent meaning of the Ayah is that the speaker of the phrase "و كذلك یجتبیك" (and thus will your Lord choose you) is either Allah or Prophet Ya'qūb (whose knowledge comes from Allah, making it a correct prophecy), there is no objection, especially considering that Yūsuf was not a prophet at that time, to Him addressing Yūsuf directly with the phrase "یجتبیك".

Messages

1- Prophets are the chosen ones of Allah. «یَجتبیك»

2- Because Yūsuf عليه السلام was chosen and... He was afflicted with various hardships so that the essence of his being might be revealed through those difficulties. «یجتبیك» (البلاء للولاء...)*

3- The worth of a person is not determined by age; it is possible for someone to be younger in age but higher in character and value. (Just as Yūsuf was younger than his brothers) «یجتبیك ربّك»*

4- The friends of Allah (Awliyā') can see the future of individuals through a single dream. «یَجتبیك رَبّك ویُعَلِّمك»

5- Important sciences and fundamental, key knowledge should not be taught to everyone; first, worthy individuals must be selected, and then the knowledge should be placed at their disposal. «یجتبیك ربّك و یعلّمك»*

6- Knowledge is the first Divine gift to His chosen ones. «یجتبیك ربّك و یُعلّمك»

7- Prophets are the direct students of Allah. «یعلّمك»

8- Dream interpretation is among the matters that Allah grants to man. «یُعلّمُك من تأویل الاحادیث»

9- Among the ways of connecting with the Unseen (Ghayb) is the interpretation of visions and dreams. «یعلّمك من تأویل الاحادیث»*

10- The knowledge taught to Yūsuf عليه السلام was also limited. (The particle "min" [من] in the phrase «من تأویل الاحادث» means a portion of the science of dream interpretation)*

11- The station of prophethood (Nubuwwah) and governance (Ḥukūmah) are the culmination of blessings. «و یتّم نعمته»

12- In the culture of the Quran, ancestors are considered equivalent to fathers. «اَبَویك من قبل ابراهیم و اسحاق»

13- The knowledge, guidance, and merit of an individual are a Divine blessing and gift not only for himself but also for his family and relatives. «علیك و على آل یعقوب»*

14- The completion of the blessing upon the family of Ya'qūb indicates the good ultimate end (āqibat bi-khayrī) of Yūsuf's brothers. «یتمّ نعمته علیك و على آل یعقوب»*

15- The selection of prophets is based on Divine knowledge and wisdom. «یجتبیك ... علیمٌ حكیمٌ»

Ayah

۞ لَّقَدْ كَانَ فِى يُوسُفَ وَإِخْوَتِهِۦٓ ءَايَـٰتٌ لِّلسَّآئِلِينَ (۷)
In Joseph and his brothers there are certainly signs for the seekers. (7)

Key Points

In the story of the life of Prophet Joseph (Yusuf), many signs and tokens of Allah's power are evident, each of which serves as a source of lesson and admonition for those who research and seek (the truth); among them are: 1. The mysterious and symbolic dream of Prophet Joseph. 2. The knowledge of dream interpretation. 3. Jacob's (Ya'qub's) recognition and awareness of his son's future. 4. Being in the well and remaining unharmed. 5. Prophet Jacob's عليه السلام becoming blind and regaining his sight. 6. The bottom of the well and the height of status/power. 7. Going to prison and attaining governance. 8. Being pure and hearing accusations of impurity. 9. Separation and reunion. 10. Slavery and kingship. 11. Imprisonment to escape sin. 12. Magnanimity and swift forgiveness of the erring brothers.

Alongside these signs, there are questions that can be raised, the answer to each of which illuminates the path of life: 1. How does envy lead a person to fratricide (killing a brother)? 2. How do ten people become unanimous and complicit in a single act of treachery? 3. How does Joseph magnanimously forgo punishing his treacherous brothers? 4. How does a person, through the remembrance of Allah, prefer prison over the pleasure of sin?

This Surah was revealed at a time when the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was under severe economic and social siege. This story served as a source of comfort for the Prophet, [implying]: O Prophet! If some of your relatives do not believe, do not be distressed; Joseph's brothers threw him into the well!

Messages

  1. Before narrating the story, prepare the listener to hear it and take a lesson from it. «لقد كان فى یوسف...»
  2. Envy shatters the boundaries of family and kinship affections as well. «لقد كان فى یوسف و اخوته»
  3. The story is one, but the points and lessons derived from it are numerous. «آیات»
  4. Inquiry and posing questions are among the ways to reach the truth. «آیات للسائلین»*
  5. Unless we are thirsty to hear and eager to learn, we will not fully benefit from the lessons of the Quran. «لِلسّائلین »
  6. The stories of the Quran provide answers to people's life questions. «للسائلین»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالُوا۟ لَيُوسُفُ وَأَخُوهُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىٰٓ أَبِينَا مِنَّا وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ إِنَّ أَبَانَا لَفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۸)
When they said, ‘Surely Joseph and his brother are dearer to our father than [the rest of] us, though we are a hardy band. Our father is indeed in manifest error.’ (8)

Key Points

‘Usbah’ refers to a united and strong group because, through unity and solidarity, they support one another like the ‘aṣāb (nerves/sinews) of a body. Two of the sons of Prophet Ya‘qūb (Jacob), (Yūsuf and Benyamin), were from one mother, and the rest were from another mother. The father's affection for Yūsuf, whether due to his young age or the perfections He possessed, caused the brothers' jealousy. In addition to jealousy, by saying: «و نحن عصبة» (and we are a strong group), they showed that they had become afflicted with arrogance and pride, and as a result of this pride and envy, they accused their father of error and deviation in showing affection to his children. Some people in society, instead of elevating themselves, bring down the elders. Since they are not beloved themselves, they try to break those who are beloved. There is a difference between discrimination (tab‘īḍ) and distinction (tafāwut). Discrimination is giving preference without reason. But distinction is preference based on merit. For example, a teacher's grades show distinction, but this distinction is wise, not oppressive. The affection of Prophet Ya‘qūb for Yūsuf was wise, not oppressive, but Yūsuf's brothers considered this affection baseless and said: «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین» (Indeed, our father is in manifest error). Sometimes excessive affection causes trouble; Ya‘qūb loved Yūsuf very much, and this very fact led to the brothers' jealousy and the throwing of Yūsuf into the well. Just as Zulaykhā’s affection for Yūsuf resulted in Yūsuf being imprisoned. Therefore, when the jailer, who had become captivated by Yūsuf’s character, told him: I love you. Yūsuf said: I fear that this friendship might also bring about a calamity.(30)

30) Tafsīr al-Sittīn al-Jāmi‘.

Messages

1- Jealousy causes adults to forget their age and status and harbor malice toward children. (Considering that Yūsuf was younger, the older ones envied him) «لیوسف... احبّ الى ابینا»

2- If children feel discrimination, the fire of jealousy flares up among them. «احبّ الى ابینا منّا»

3- The love and desire to be beloved exist in the nature of every human being. People suffer from a lack of attention and affection toward them. «احبّ الى ابینا»

4- Prophet Ya‘qūb had affection for all his children. «احبّ الى ابینا منّا» (The brothers were saying: Yūsuf is more beloved, meaning we are also beloved).

5- Force and power do not bring affection. (The brothers had power, but they were not beloved) «لیوسف... احبّ... و نحن عصبة»

6- Being together leads to a feeling of strength and power. «نحن عصبة»

7- Groupism, if lacking proper and God-pleasing leadership, accelerates the factors of downfall. («و نحن عصبة» is a sign of cohesion, but cohesion that is unrestrained and without proper leadership).

8- To commit error and sin, a person first justifies himself and theorizes his action. (The brothers considered themselves united and strong, «و نحن عصبة», and their father deviated, «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال», and with this reasoning, they justified their jealousy).

9- Young people typically consider themselves all-knowing and give less importance to the opinions of their fathers. «نحن عصبة انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین»

10- The feeling of power and strength blinds the intellect. «نحن عصبة انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین»

11- Incorrect standards lead to incorrect results. (If the standard is only power and number, the result is attributing deviation to the minority.) «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین»

12- Selfish people attribute the causes of failure to others instead of seeking them within themselves. «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین» (Instead of saying, "We are jealous," they said, "Our father is deviated").

13- Jealousy breaks the boundary of prophethood and fatherhood (prophecy and paternity), and the sons attribute deviation and injustice to the Prophet who is their father. «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین»

14- Human heedlessness may reach a point where, despite their own deviation and error, they deem others to be in error. (Yūsuf’s brothers, instead of considering themselves jealous and conspiratorial, considered their father deviated) «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین»

Ayah

ٱقْتُلُوا۟ يُوسُفَ أَوِ ٱطْرَحُوهُ أَرْضًا يَخْلُ لَكُمْ وَجْهُ أَبِيكُمْ وَتَكُونُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ قَوْمًا صَـٰلِحِينَ (۹)
‘Kill Joseph or cast him away into some [distant] land, so that your father’s attention may be exclusively towards you, and thereafter you may become a righteous lot.’ (9)

Key Points

Man has four states when encountering blessings: envy (hasad), stinginess (bukhl), altruism (ithar), and desirable emulation (ghibta). If he says to himself: Since we do not possess a certain blessing, others should not possess it either—that is envy. If he says: Only we should benefit from this blessing, but others should not—that is stinginess. If he says: Let others benefit from the blessing, even if it means we are deprived—that is altruism. If he says: Now that others benefit from the blessing, I wish we could also benefit—that is desirable emulation. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Sometimes I show affection to some of my children and seat them on my knees, even though they do not deserve all this affection, lest they envy my other children and the story of Joseph be repeated.(31)

31) Tafsir-e Namuneh, quoting Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 78.

Messages

1- Dangerous thought leads man to dangerous action. «لیوسف... احبّ... اقتلوا»

2- The feeling of discrimination in affection by children drives them to the point of fratricide. (Although the father's intense affection for Joseph was not without reason, but due to his perfections, the brothers felt discriminated against and imagined the affection was baseless, and this very feeling compelled them to conspire.) «احبّ الى ابینا... اقتلوا»*

3- From the perspective of the short-sighted, the physical elimination of the rival is the best solution. «اقتلوا یوسف»*

4- Envy drives man to fratricide. «اقتلوا یوسف»

5- Sinners tempt one another to commit sin and gain strength from conspiring together. (The brothers commanded one another to kill and cast him away.) «اقتلوا... او اطرحوه»*

6- Man desires popularity, and the lack of affection is the source of the greatest dangers and deviations. «یخل لكم وجه ابیكم»

7- The envious imagine that: out of sight, out of mind. «اقتلوا یوسف... یخل لكم وجه ابیكم» (The brothers thought that if the father did not see Joseph, Joseph would leave his thoughts, and all his attention would be focused on us.)*

8- Although the Quran introduces the path to gaining popularity as faith and righteous deeds; «ان الذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات سیجعل لهم الرّحمن ودّا»,(32) Satan, however, portrays the path to becoming beloved as fratricide. «اقتلوا... یخل لكم وجه ابیكم »

9- The envious person imagines that by destroying others, the blessings will become his. «اقتلوا ... یخل لكم وجه ابیكم »

10- Satan opens the door to sin today by promising repentance in the future. «و تكونوا من بعده قوماً صالحین »

11- Knowledge and awareness are not always factors that prevent deviation. Although the brothers knew that killing or exiling Joseph was wrong; «تكونوا من بعده قوماً صالحین», they still proceeded with the action.

12- Speaking of repentance before committing a sin is deceiving the conscience and opening the path to sin. (The brothers were telling each other: Destroy Joseph, and afterward, through repentance, we will become righteous people.) «تكونوا من بعده قوماً صالحین»*32) Maryam, 96.

Ayah

قَالَ قَآئِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ لَا تَقْتُلُوا۟ يُوسُفَ وَأَلْقُوهُ فِى غَيَـٰبَتِ ٱلْجُبِّ يَلْتَقِطْهُ بَعْضُ ٱلسَّيَّارَةِ إِن كُنتُمْ فَـٰعِلِينَ (۱٠)
One of them said, ‘Do not kill Joseph, but cast him into the recess of a well so that some caravan may pick him up, if you are to do [anything].’ (10)

Key Points

Jubb (جُبّ) means a well that is not lined with stone. Ghayābah (غَیابت) also refers to niches placed in the wall of the well, near the water, which are not visible if one looks down from the top of the well. Enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy ‘anil-Munkar) has blessings that become clear in the future. The prohibition "لاتقتلوا" (Do not kill) saved Joseph, and in later years, he saved the kingdom from famine. Just as Asiyah, the wife of Pharaoh, saved the life of Moses through the prohibition "لاتقتلوا" (Do not kill), and in later years, he saved the Children of Israel from the evil of Pharaoh. This is a clear example of the Divine promise where He says: «و مَن احیاها فكانّما اَحْیا الناس جمیعاً» (33) Whoever saves one life, it is as if he has saved the lives of all mankind.

33) مائده 32.

Messages

1- Sometimes the forbidding of evil by a single individual can change the opinion of the group and influence it. (One person said: Do not kill, but he changed the group's opinion.) «قال قائل»

2- The name of the speaker is not important; the content of the speech is important. «قال قائل...»

3- We should not be intimidated by the majority or conform to the crowd. (It was one person, but he did not conform to the crowd.) «قال قائل لاتقتلوا»

4- If it is not possible to completely stop an evil act, it must be reduced as much as possible. «لا تقتلوا ... والقوه »

5- Consultation is helpful even in undesirable matters, let alone in good deeds. (They consulted about killing their brother.) «لاتقتلوا...»

6- To combat corruption, sometimes it is necessary to repel the greater evil with the lesser evil. (Do not kill him; throw him into the well.) «لاتقتلوا یوسف و القوه فى غیابت الجبّ»

7- Alongside forbidding evil, we should offer an alternative. (If we say, "Do not do this," then we should say what they should do instead.) «لاتقتلوا... القوه فى غیابت الجبّ»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا مَا لَكَ لَا تَأْمَ۫نَّا عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَنَـٰصِحُونَ (۱۱)
They said, ‘Father! Why is it that you do not trust us with Joseph? We are indeed his well-wishers. (11)

Messages

1- Those who are more worthless (or hollow) make greater claims and engage in more propaganda. «انّا له لناصحون»

2- One cannot trust every brother, even. (It seems Ya'qub had repeatedly prevented Yusuf from going with his brothers, which is why the brothers criticize him by saying, «ما لك لا تأمنّا» [What is wrong with you that you do not trust us?].)

3- Do not be deceived by every slogan, and beware of names that lack substance (names that do not match reality). (The traitor calls himself a sincere advisor.) «لناصحون»

4- To dispel suspicion, the enemy offers you every kind of assurance. «انّا له لناصحون»

5- The traitor places the blame upon others. «مالك»

6- From the very first day, mankind has been deceived in the name of benevolence. Satan, too, said to deceive Adam and Eve: I am indeed a sincere advisor to you both. «و قاسمهما انّى لَكما لمن الناصحین»(34) «انّا له لناصحون»

7- Envy compels a person toward sins such as lying and deceit, even against his most beloved relatives. «انّا له لناصحون» 34) A'raf 21.

Ayah

أَرْسِلْهُ مَعَنَا غَدًا يَرْتَعْ وَيَلْعَبْ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَـٰفِظُونَ (۱۲)
Let him go with us tomorrow so that he may eat lots of fruits and play, and we will indeed take [good] care of him.’ (12)

Key Points

Man needs recreation and sport, and as observed in this Ayah, the strongest logic that managed to make Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) submit to his sons' request was that Yusuf (Joseph) needed recreation. It is stated in narrations: A believer must allocate time for recreation and enjoying lawful pleasures so that, through it, he may succeed in performing other tasks.(35) Not only yesterday, but today and even in the future, under the guise of sport and play, they will separate the youth from their main goal and keep them in heedlessness. They take games seriously so that serious matters are considered mere play. Arrogance and conspirators not only misuse sport but also pursue their sinister goals under any other desirable and acceptable name. They dispatch the most dangerous spies to countries under the name of diplomats. Under the name of military advisors, they conspire and gain access to military secrets. Under the name of human rights, they support their mercenaries. Under the name of medicine, they send weapons to their mercenaries. Under the name of economic experts, they keep weak countries vulnerable. Under the name of pesticide spraying, they destroy gardens and farms, and even under the name of Islamic scholar, they misrepresent Islam.

35) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 390.

Messages

1- A child's recreation must be with the father's permission. «اَرْسِلْه»

2- The youth need recreation and sport. «یرتع و یلعب»

3- The brothers misused a permissible and logical means for deception. «اَرْسِله... یرتع و یلعب»

4- If fathers and caring educators have appropriate planning for the leisure time, recreation, and play of children and adolescents, others cannot misuse this opportunity. (Yusuf's brothers exploited Yusuf's need for sport) «یرتع و یلعب»*

5- We must be careful during children's play and recreation so that they are not harmed. «لحافظون»*

6- Sometimes excessive insistence on a matter indicates a plot or conspiracy. (In the previous Ayah, «انا له لناصحون», and in this Ayah, «انا له لحافظون»)*

Ayah

قَالَ إِنِّى لَيَحْزُنُنِىٓ أَن تَذْهَبُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَأَخَافُ أَن يَأْكُلَهُ ٱلذِّئْبُ وَأَنتُمْ عَنْهُ غَـٰفِلُونَ (۱۳)
He said, ‘It really upsets me that you should take him away, and I fear the wolf may eat him while you are oblivious of him.’ (13)

Messages

1- Separation and parting are sorrowful, even for the Prophets. «لیحزننى»*

2- The divine test comes from the very area about which individuals are sensitive. (Jacob was sensitive regarding Joseph, and Joseph’s separation became the means of his trial.) «لیحزننى... أخاف»*

3- Do not expose secrets (or: Do not reveal faults). «اخاف ان یاكله الذئب»

(The father was aware of his sons' jealousy, which is why he told Joseph: Do not recount the dream you saw to your brothers. But here, he does not mention their jealousy; rather, he uses the excuse of the danger of the wolf and their negligence regarding Joseph.)

4- Grant your child independence.

(A father's love for his child and defending him against potential danger are two principles, but the child's independence is another principle. Jacob sent Joseph along with his other brothers. This is because a youth must gradually gain independence and separate from his father, choose friends for himself, think, and stand on his own feet, even if it comes at the cost of enduring difficulties and sorrow.)

5- We should not disclose our sensitivities to everyone, otherwise they might be used against the person himself. «أخاف أن یأكله الذئب»*

6- Negligence leads to harm and vulnerability. «یأكله الذئب و انتم عنه غافلون»*

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ لَئِنْ أَكَلَهُ ٱلذِّئْبُ وَنَحْنُ عُصْبَةٌ إِنَّآ إِذًا لَّخَـٰسِرُونَ (۱٤)
They said, ‘Should the wolf eat him while we are a hardy band, then we will indeed be losers!’ (14)

Messages

1- Sometimes elders, based on experience and awareness, sense danger, but young people are proud of their own strength and take the danger lightly. «ونحن عصبة» (The father was worried, but the sons were proud of their strength.)

2- Being strong is not a proof of being trustworthy. (Yusuf’s brothers were strong, «نحن عصبة», but they were not trustworthy)*

3- The brothers did not express concern that the wolf might tear Yusuf apart, but they reacted strongly against the possibility that their own strength and power might be compromised. «ونحن عصبة انّا اذاً لخاسرون»*

4- If someone accepts a responsibility and does not perform it well, they put their capital, character, honor, and conscience at risk and will be among the losers. «لخاسرون»

5- Deception and the expression of false emotions are among the schemes of hypocritical and two-faced individuals. (The brothers said in the presence of their father:) «انا اذاً لخاسرون»

6- Some people, in order to achieve their sinister goals, are even willing to disregard their own honor and character. «انّا اذاً لخاسرون»*

Ayah

فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَأَجْمَعُوٓا۟ أَن يَجْعَلُوهُ فِى غَيَـٰبَتِ ٱلْجُبِّ ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِ لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُم بِأَمْرِهِمْ هَـٰذَا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱٥)
So when they took him away and conspired to put him into the recess of a well, We revealed to him, ‘[A day will come when] you will surely inform them about this affair of theirs while they are not aware [of your identity].’ (15)

Key Points

Since Allah willed that Joseph should become a ruler, He had to pass through certain stages; He became a slave so that He might have mercy on slaves. He fell into the well and the prison so that He might have mercy on prisoners. Just as Allah says to His Prophet: You were poor and an orphan so that you would not drive away the poor and the orphan. «ألم یجدك یتیماً... فامّا الیتیم فلا تقهر...»(36)

36) Ad-Duha, 6 to 11.

Messages

1- The consensus and agreement of a group of people is not a sign of truthfulness (or righteousness). «اجمعوا ان یجعلوه فى غیابت الجبّ»

2- Sometimes there is a gap between the plan and the action. (The brothers' plan was to throw Joseph into the well, «القوه», but in action, they placed Joseph in the well. «یجعلوه»)

3- Revelation to Joseph عليه السلام began when he was separated from his family and subjected to the unkindness of his brothers. «فلمّا ذهبوا به... اوحینا الیه»*

4- Divine aid comes to the friends of Allah (Awliya') at critical moments. «فى غیابت الجُبّ و اوحینا الیه »

5- The best means of comfort for Joseph inside the well was Allah's inspiration regarding a bright future. «اوحینا الیه»

6- Joseph possessed the worthiness to receive Divine revelation in his youth. «اوحینا الیه»

7- Allah tests His friends (Awliya') with hardships and guides them. «اوحینا الیه»*

8- Awareness leads to tranquility. (What caused Joseph's tranquility in the well was the Divine revelation.) «اوحینا الیه لتنبّئنّهم بأمرهم هذا»*

9- Hope is the best asset for continuing life. (We revealed to Joseph that in the future, he would be saved from the well and would shame his brothers for their deed.) «اوحینا الیه لتنبّئنّهم بأمرهم هذا»*

10- When the end of vices is disgrace, a wise person must restrain his soul. «لتنبّئنّهم بامرهم...»*

11- Refer to evil deeds through allusion and implication. «بامرهم»*

Ayah

وَجَآءُوٓ أَبَاهُمْ عِشَآءً يَبْكُونَ (۱٦)
In the evening, they came weeping to their father. (16)

Key Points

In the Quran, there are four types of crying and tears: 1. Tears of joy: A group of Christians shed tears upon hearing the verses of the Quran. «ترى اعینهم تفیض من الدمْع ممّا عرفوا من الحقّ»(37) 2. Tears of grief and regret: Devoted Muslims would cry when they heard from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله that there were no resources available for them to go to the front lines. «تفیض من الدمع حزناً الا یجدوا ما ینفقون»(38) 3. Tears of fear: When the Divine verses were recited to the saints (Awliya), they would fall down in prostration, weeping. «خرّوا سُجدا وبُكیّاً»(39), «و یخِرّون للأذقان یبكون ویزیدهم خشوعاً»(40) 4. Fake and fabricated tears: This very verse, where Joseph's brothers came weeping to Jacob, claiming that the wolf had devoured Joseph. «یبكون»

37) Ma'idah, 83. 38) Tawbah, 92. 39) Maryam, 58. 40) Isra', 109.

Messages

1- Conspirators do not neglect the role of emotions and timing. «عشاءً»

2- Crying is not always a sign of sincerity. Do not trust every instance of weeping. «یبكون»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَآ إِنَّا ذَهَبْنَا نَسْتَبِقُ وَتَرَكْنَا يُوسُفَ عِندَ مَتَـٰعِنَا فَأَكَلَهُ ٱلذِّئْبُ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ لَّنَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۷)
They said, ‘Father! We had gone racing and left Joseph with our things, whereat the wolf ate him. But you will not believe us even if we spoke truly.’ (17)

Messages

1- Lying brings forth lies. To justify their mistake, the brothers told three consecutive lies: We went racing, we left Yusuf near the belongings, the wolf ate him. «نستبق، تركنا، فأكله الذئب»

2- A liar is forgetful. Despite the fact that the brothers took Yusuf for play, in their report to their father, they claimed he was guarding their belongings. «تركنا یوسف عند متاعنا»*

3- Racing in games has a long history. «نستبق»

4- A betrayer is cowardly, and a liar fears exposure. «ما أنت بمؤمن لنا و لو كنّا صادقین»

5- A liar insists that people consider him truthful. «و ما انت بمؤمن لنا و لو كنّا صادقین»*

Ayah

وَجَآءُو عَلَىٰ قَمِيصِهِۦ بِدَمٍ كَذِبٍ ۚ قَالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنفُسُكُمْ أَمْرًا ۖ فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ (۱۸)
And they produced sham blood on his shirt. He said, ‘No, your souls have made a matter seem decorous to you. Yet patience is graceful, and Allah is my resort against what you allege.’ (18)

Key Points

| Question: Patience regarding Divine decrees is beautiful, but what beauty is there in patience regarding an injustice committed against an oppressed child, such that Ya'qub (Jacob) says: "فصبر جمیل" (beautiful patience)? Answer: Firstly, Ya'qub knew through revelation that Yusuf (Joseph) was alive. Secondly, if Ya'qub had made any move that would increase their suspicion and doubt, the brothers would have gone to the well and eliminated Yusuf. Thirdly, one should not do anything that completely closes the door of repentance, even for the oppressors.

Messages

1- Be wary of creating false atmospheres/propaganda. «بدمٍ كذب»

2- Do not be deceived by feigned victimhood. (Ya'qub was not deceived by the blood-stained shirt and the tears; rather, he said: Woe to your souls/selves.) «بل سولت لكم انفسكم»

3- Satan and the self (nafs) make sin appear beautiful to man and justify its commission. «سوّلت لكم انفسكم»

4- Ya'qub عليه السلام knew that Yusuf had not been eaten by a wolf; therefore, he did not demand bones or remains of the body from the brothers. «بل سوّلت لكم انفسكم»*

5- Events have two faces: affliction and hardship, «بدم كذب» (false blood); patience and beauty. «فصبر جمیل»*

6- The Divine Prophets show the most beautiful reaction in the face of difficult events. «فصبر جمیل»*

7- In the face of events, besides internal patience and strength, one must seek assistance from Divine aid. «فصبر جمیل واللَّه المستعان»

8- One must seek assistance from Allah for patience. «فصبر جمیل و اللّه المستعان»*

9- The best type of patience is that in which Allah is not forgotten, despite the heart burning and tears flowing. «واللَّه المستعان»

10- Enduring the conspiracy of children against their own brother is difficult and painful; therefore, one must seek assistance from Allah to bear it. «و اللّه المستعان»*

11- By using the phrase «و اللّه المُستعان على ما تصفون» (and Allah is the One Whose help is sought against what you describe) instead of «على ما فعلتم» (against what you did), Prophet Ya'qub made the brothers understand that their claim was unbelievable. *

Ayah

وَجَآءَتْ سَيَّارَةٌ فَأَرْسَلُوا۟ وَارِدَهُمْ فَأَدْلَىٰ دَلْوَهُۥ ۖ قَالَ يَـٰبُشْرَىٰ هَـٰذَا غُلَـٰمٌ ۚ وَأَسَرُّوهُ بِضَـٰعَةً ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۹)
There came a caravan, and they sent their water-drawer, who let down his bucket. ‘Good news!’ he said. ‘This is a young boy!’ So they hid him as [a piece of] merchandise, and Allah knew best what they were doing. (19)

Key Points

Allah does not abandon His sincere servants and rescues them during hardships and difficulties. He saved Noah on the water, Jonah under the water, and Joseph near the water. Just as He saved Abraham from the fire, Moses in the middle of the sea, Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله inside the cave, and Ali علیه السلام in the bed where he slept in place of the Prophet. By Divine Will, the rope of the well became the means for Joseph to ascend from the bottom of the well to the throne and the palace. So, look what can be achieved with the Rope of Allah (Habl Allah)!? «و اعتصموا بحبل اللّه...»(41)

41) Aal-i-Imran, 103.

Messages

1- Sometimes one's own people throw a person into a well, but Allah rescues that person through strangers. «جائت سیارة»

2- Division of labor is one of the principles of management and collective life. («واردهم» means the person responsible for the water.)

3- Some groups view humans as mere merchandise. «بضاعة»

4- Concealing the truth is possible before people, but what do we do with Allah, Who is aware of everything? «اسرّوه... واللَّه علیم»

Ayah

وَشَرَوْهُ بِثَمَنٍۭ بَخْسٍ دَرَٰهِمَ مَعْدُودَةٍ وَكَانُوا۟ فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلزَّٰهِدِينَ (۲٠)
And they sold him for a cheap price, a few dirhams, for they set small store by him. (20)

Key Points

Whoever sells the Joseph of his existence cheaply will regret it. A person's lifespan, youth, honor, independence, and purity are each a Joseph that we must be careful not to sell cheaply.

Messages

  1. Wealth that is easily acquired is easily lost. «و شَروه بثمن بخس»

  2. The system of slavery and slave trade has a long history. «و شَروه»

  3. The lack of desire for Joseph at one stage benefited his future. «و شَرَوه بثمن بَخس...»*

  4. Whoever does not know the value of something loses it cheaply. «بِثَمن بخس» (The travelers did not recognize Joseph's value.)

  5. Valuable individuals will eventually have their worth revealed, even if they are treated coldly for a period. (If today they sell Joseph as a slave, one day he will become a ruler.) «شروه بثمن بخس...»*

  6. The history of money dates back thousands of years. «دراهم»

  7. Ignorant and heedless men sold Joseph for a low price, but aware and loving women described him as a noble angel. «شروه بثمن بخس - اِن هذا الاّ ملك كریم»

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «رُبّ امرئة أفقه من رجل»(42) How often is a woman more discerning than a man. (42) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 306.

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِى ٱشْتَرَىٰهُ مِن مِّصْرَ لِٱمْرَأَتِهِۦٓ أَكْرِمِى مَثْوَىٰهُ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَنفَعَنَآ أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُۥ وَلَدًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلِنُعَلِّمَهُۥ مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَالِبٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرِهِۦ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۲۱)
The man from Egypt who had bought him said to his wife, ‘Give him an honourable place [in the household]. Maybe he will be useful to us, or we may adopt him as a son.’ Thus We established Joseph in the land and that We might teach him the interpretation of dreams. Allah has [full] command of His affairs, but most people do not know. (21)

Key Points

The phrase «عسى أن ینفعنا او نتّخذه ولداً» (Perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son) has been used in the Quran in two instances: One is concerning Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) when his ark was taken from the water; Pharaoh's wife told him: Do not kill this infant, perhaps he will benefit us in the future. (43) The other is here, where the Aziz (Minister) of Egypt tells his wife: Treat this slave with respect, perhaps he will be useful to us in the future. Yes, by the Divine Will, the love for an unknown infant and an unknown slave settles so deeply in the hearts of the rulers of Egypt that it prepares the ground for their future governance.

(43) Al-Qasas, 9.

Messages

1- Nobility was evident in the countenance of Yusuf (Joseph), to the extent that the Aziz of Egypt recommended him to his wife. «اكرمى مثواه»

2- The wife plays a pivotal role in the home. «اكرمى مثواه»*

3- Invite your associates and acquaintances to treat people kindly. «اكرمى مثواه»*

4- Be forward-looking in your dealings. «اشتراه... عسى ان ینفعنا»*

5- By showing respect to people, one can expect help and assistance from them. «اكرمى... ینفعنا»

6- Hearts are in the hand of Allah. The affection for Yusuf settled in the heart of the buyer. «عسى ان ینفعنا او نتّخذه ولداً»

7- Adopt important decisions gradually, after evaluation and testing. (First, we treat Yusuf as a helper in the house, «عسى أن ینفعنا», then gradually we adopt him as a son) «او نتّخذه ولداً»*

8- Adoption has historical precedent. «نتّخذه ولداً»

9- The Aziz of Egypt was childless. «نتّخذه ولداً»*

10- An enemy becomes a source of good if Allah wills it. (His brothers threw him into the well, and today he is established in the palace) «كذلك مكنّا لیوسف»*

11- Do not underestimate subordinates; they may well be the powerful figures and rulers of the future. «مكنّا لیوسف فى الارض»*

12- Knowledge and learning are prerequisites for accepting responsibility. «مكّنّا... لنعلمه»

13- The end of bitterness is sweetness. «شروه بثمن بخسٍ - مكّنّا لیوسف»

14- The dominant Will of Allah elevated Yusuf from the well to a high status. «مكّنّا لیوسف»

15- What we perceive as an accident is, in reality, a Divine design for the fulfillment of His Will. «واللّه غالب على امره»

16- How often are there unpleasant events whose true nature is good. (Yusuf apparently fell into a well, but in reality, the plan was something else) «كذلك مكنّا لیوسف... اكثر النّاس لا یعلمون»*

17- People see the surface of events, but they are unaware of the Divine goals and plans. «لایعلمون»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُۥٓ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۲۲)
When he came of age, We gave him judgement and [sacred] knowledge, and thus do We reward the virtuous. (22)

Key Points

«اشدّ» (Ashudd) is derived from «شدّ» (Shadd), meaning "a firm knot," referring to physical and spiritual strength. This word is sometimes used in the Quran to mean "puberty" (بلوغ), as stated in Ayah 34 of Surah Al-Isra: «حتّى یبلغ اَشدّه» (meaning: do not approach the orphan's property until he reaches the age of maturity). Sometimes the intent of «اشدّ» is the age of forty. Similar to Ayah 15 of Surah Al-Ahqaf, which states: «بلغ اشدّه و بلغ اربعین سنة». And sometimes it refers to the age before old age, like Ayah 67 of Surah Ghafir: «ثم یخرجكم طفلا ثم لتبلغوا اشدّكم ثم لتكونوا شیوخا»

Messages

1- Knowledge and wisdom require capacity and readiness. «بلغ اشدّه»

2- Divine promotions occur without delay and at their proper time. «لمّا بلغ... آتیناه»*

3- Wisdom (Hikmah) is distinct from knowledge ('Ilm). (Knowledge is information, but wisdom is insight and discernment that guides man to the truth.) «آتیناه حكماً و علماً»*

4- The knowledge of the Prophets is not acquired (it is divinely granted). «آتیناه حكماً و علماً»

5- The presence of knowledge and wisdom side by side is extremely valuable and effective. «حكماً و علماً»*

6- State the reason for appointments and dismissals to the people. «آتیناه حكماً... كذلك نجزى المحسنین»*

7- We must perform good deeds (Ihsan) to be worthy of receiving Allah's special reward. «نجزى المحسنین»

8- The doers of good (the Muhsinin) are successful even in this world. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

9- Not everyone who possesses intellectual and physical ability is included in Divine favor; being a doer of good (Muhsin) is also necessary. «نجزى المحسنین»

Ayah

وَرَٰوَدَتْهُ ٱلَّتِى هُوَ فِى بَيْتِهَا عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ وَغَلَّقَتِ ٱلْأَبْوَٰبَ وَقَالَتْ هَيْتَ لَكَ ۚ قَالَ مَعَاذَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ رَبِّىٓ أَحْسَنَ مَثْوَاىَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۲۳)
The woman in whose house he was, solicited him. She closed the doors and said, ‘Come!!’ He said, ‘God forbid! Indeed He is my Lord; He has given me a good abode. Indeed the wrongdoers are not felicitous.’ (23)

Key Points

Regarding the interpretation of «معاذ اللّه انّه ربّى احسن مثواى» (Refuge is in Allah! Indeed, He is my Lord, Who has made my residence excellent), two possibilities have been suggested: A: Allah is my Lord, Who has honored my station, and I seek refuge in Him. B: The Aziz (Governor) of Egypt is my master, and I am at his table, and he told you concerning me: «اكرمى مَثواه» (Treat his stay honorably), and I will not betray him. Both possibilities have supporters who rely on evidence. But in our view, the first possibility is better. This is because Joseph did not commit sin due to Divine piety (Taqwa), not because he would say: Since I am in the house of the Aziz of Egypt and he has a right over me, I will not assault or attempt evil with his wife! Because the value of this action is less than observing piety. Of course, in several places in this Surah, «ربّك» (your lord/master), which refers to the 'Aziz of Egypt, is seen, but wherever «ربّى» (my Lord) is used in this Surah, the intention is Allah. Furthermore, it is far from the dignity of Joseph to humiliate himself so much as to call the Aziz of Egypt «ربّى». The friends of Allah seek refuge in Allah and achieve results: Moses sought refuge in Allah from the evil of Pharaoh's trials: «انّى عذت بربّى و ربّكم من كل متكبّر لایؤمن بیوم الحساب» (44) The mother of Mary says: I place Mary and her progeny under Your protection: «انّى اعیذها بك و ذرّیتها من الشیطان الرجیم» (45) And in this Ayah, Joseph seeks refuge in Allah: «قال معاذ اللّه», and Allah commands the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to seek refuge in Him: «قل اعوذ بربّ...» Instead of saying: «اعوذباللَّه» (I seek refuge in Allah), he immediately turned to seeking refuge in Allah and said: «معاذاللَّه» (Refuge is in Allah), so as not to emphasize his own act of seeking refuge. In reality, he did not attribute any value to himself.

44) Ghafir, 27. 45) Aal-i-Imran, 36.

Messages

  1. Great sins begin with softness and interaction. «راودته»
  2. A man being pure is not enough, because sometimes women harass men. «و راودته»
  3. The power of lust is such that it enslaves even the wife of a king to her servant. «و راودته الّتى»
  4. We should try not to mention the name of the transgressor and refer to her by allusion. «الّتى»
  5. Do not leave young men alone in houses where non-mahram women reside. Because the door to interaction may open. «و راودته الّتى هو فى بیتها»
  6. Love usually develops gradually as a result of interaction. Joseph's permanent presence in the house gradually caused love. «فى بیتها»
  7. Address issues related to moral corruption indirectly and politely. «راودته... غلّقت»*
  8. The presence of a non-mahram man and woman in a closed environment prepares the ground for sin. «غلّقت الابواب و قالت هیت لك»
  9. The ugliness of adultery (Zina) has been accepted throughout history, and for this reason, Zulaikha firmly closed all the doors. «غلّقت الابواب»
  10. All doors are closed, but the door of seeking refuge in Allah is open. «غلّقت الابواب... معاذ اللَّه»
  11. The situations for Divine testing are varied; sometimes in a well, and sometimes in a palace. «راودته... قال معاذ اللّه»*
  12. Piety (Taqwa) and human will can overcome the grounds for deviation and error. «معاذاللَّه»
  13. Attention to Allah is a deterrent against sin and slip-ups. «معاذ اللّه»
  14. If our leader or superior commands a sin, we must not obey him. «هیت لك قال معاذ اللَّه» (One must not disobey Allah for the sake of obeying people. «لا طاعة لمخلوق فى معصیة الخالق» (46))
  15. When facing the danger of sin, one must seek refuge in Allah. «قال معاذ اللّه»*
  16. Even in a governmental palace, one can be pure, chaste, truthful, and trustworthy. «قال معاذ اللّه»*
  17. The danger of the sexual instinct is so great that Allah must come to the rescue to save one from it. «معاذ اللّه»*
  18. The lustful person fears apparent disgrace (Zulaikha closed the doors firmly), but the God-worshipper fears Allah. «معاذ اللّه»*
  19. Committing sin (adultery/fornication) is incompatible with human dignity. «معاذ اللّه»*
  20. Remembering Allah spontaneously plays an important role in abandoning sin. «معاذ اللّه»*
  21. The first action against sin is the remembrance of Allah and seeking refuge in Him. «معاذ اللّه»*
  22. The best type of piety is that we do not sin because of the grace, love, and right of Allah, not out of fear of disgrace in this world or fire in the Hereafter. «معاذ اللَّه انّه ربّى احسن مثواى»
  23. Remembering Divine favors is one of the factors for abandoning sin. «انّه ربّى احسن مثواى»*
  24. Remembering the consequence of sin prevents its commission. «لایفلح الظالمون»
  25. Encouraging adultery/fornication or creating the grounds for sin for pure youth is oppression against oneself, one's spouse, society, and individuals. «لایفلح الظالمون»
  26. A moment of sin can distance a person from eternal salvation. «لایفلح الظالمون»
  27. Committing sin is ingratitude and denial of blessings. «لایفلح الظالمون»
  28. In every action, one must consider the outcome. «لایفلح الظالمون»*
  29. Joseph عليه السلام was thinking of salvation (Falah) in the midst of the conflict with Zulaikha. «لایفلح الظلمون»*
  30. A person who knows his rank and status does not sell himself for a small and fleeting price. «لایفلح الظلمون»*
  31. If oppression enters through one door, salvation (Falah) exits through another. «لایفلح الظلمون»*

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ هَمَّتْ بِهِۦ ۖ وَهَمَّ بِهَا لَوْلَآ أَن رَّءَا بُرْهَـٰنَ رَبِّهِۦ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنَصْرِفَ عَنْهُ ٱلسُّوٓءَ وَٱلْفَحْشَآءَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۲٤)
She certainly made for him; and he would have made for her [too] had he not beheld the proof of his Lord. So it was, that We might turn away from him all evil and indecency. He was indeed one of Our dedicated servants. (24)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The "Proof of the Lord" (Burhān Rabb) was the light of knowledge, certainty, and wisdom, which Allah mentioned in the previous verses: «و آتیناه حكماً و علماً» (And We gave him wisdom and knowledge). (47) And what has been mentioned in some narrations—that the intent of the proof is seeing the face of his father or Gabriel—does not have a strong chain of transmission.

The Qur'an has repeatedly spoken about the resolve and decision of the enemies of Allah to plot against the friends of Allah, but Allah has foiled their schemes. On the return from the Battle of Tabuk, the hypocrites wanted to cause the Prophet's camel to bolt, thereby assassinating the Prophet, but they failed; «و همّوا بما لم ینالوا» (and they resolved upon what they could not attain). (48) Or they decided to mislead the Prophet; «لهمّت طائفة منهم ان یضلّوك» (A faction among them resolved to mislead you). (49) Or they decided to commit aggression, but they failed. «اذهمّ قوم ان یبسطوا الیكم ایدیهم فكفّ ایدیهم عنكم» (when a people intended to extend their hands against you, but He restrained their hands from you). (50)

Yusuf (Joseph) was infallible (ma'sum) and chaste, based on the statements of all those who had a relationship with him in some way, and we present some examples of this: 1. Allah said: «لنصرف عنه السوء والفحشاء انّه من عبادنا المخلصین» (We helped Yusuf with the proof) so that We might avert evil and immorality from him, for he was one of Our chosen servants. 2. Yusuf said: «ربّ السّجن احبّ الىّ مما یدعوننى الیه» (My Lord! Prison is more beloved to me than that to which they invite me). In another place, he said: «انّى لم اخنه بالغیب» (Indeed, I did not betray him in his absence). 3. Zulaikha said: «لقد راودته عن نفسه فاستعصم» (Certainly, I sought to seduce him, but he remained chaste/infallible). 4. The Aziz (Minister) of Egypt said: «یوسف اعرض عن هذا و استغفرى لذنبك » (O Yusuf, disregard this, and [he told Zulaikha] seek forgiveness for your sin). 5. The witness who testified and said: If his shirt is torn from the back, it is clear that Yusuf is chaste. «ان كان قمیصه...» (If his shirt...). 6. The women of Egypt who said: «ما علمنا علیه من سوء» (We know of no evil concerning him). 7. Iblis (Satan), who promised to mislead everyone, said: «الاّ عبادك منهم المخلصین» (Except Your chosen servants among them). I cannot overcome the chosen ones, and this verse calls Yusuf Mukhlas (chosen/sincere).

46) Bihar, Vol. 10, p. 227. 47) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar. 48) Tawbah, 74. 49) Nisa, 113. 50) Ma'idah, 11.

Messages

1- If there is no Divine aid, anyone's foot will slip. «همّ بها لو لا أن رَآ برهان ربّه»

2- In every situation, the possibility of seeing the Proof of the Lord exists. «همّت به... رَآ برهان ربّه»*

3- Prophets are like other humans in their instincts, but due to their faith in the presence of God, they do not commit sin. «همّ بها لولا أن رَآ برهان ربّه»

4- Negligence of Divine remembrance is the ground for committing sin, and attention to it is the factor that preserves one from sin. «همّ بها لولا أن رَآ برهان ربّه»*

5- Becoming sincere (Mukhlas) causes a person to be preserved from sin. «لنصرف عند السوء... انّه من عبادنا المخلصین»*

6- Sincere worship is the key to success in avoiding sin. «لنصرف عنه السوء... انّه من عبادنا المخلصین»*

7- Allah protects His sincere servants. «لنصرف عنه... انّه من عبادنا المخلصین»

8- Evil and immorality cannot coexist with sincerity (Ikhlas). «لنصرف عند السوء... المخلصین»*

9- Becoming sincere is not exclusive to Yusuf عليه السلام; one can approach the station of the sincere (Mukhlasīn) by following his path. «انّه من عبادنا المخلصین»*

Ayah

وَٱسْتَبَقَا ٱلْبَابَ وَقَدَّتْ قَمِيصَهُۥ مِن دُبُرٍ وَأَلْفَيَا سَيِّدَهَا لَدَا ٱلْبَابِ ۚ قَالَتْ مَا جَزَآءُ مَنْ أَرَادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوٓءًا إِلَّآ أَن يُسْجَنَ أَوْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۲٥)
They raced to the door, and she tore his shirt from behind, and they ran into her husband at the door. She said, ‘What is to be the requital of him who has evil intentions for your wife except imprisonment or a painful punishment?’ (25)

Key Points

«استباق» (Istibāq) means two or more people racing or trying to outpace one another. Joseph fled toward the door to escape sin, and Zulaikha was running after him. It is as if they were racing toward the door. «قدّ» (Qadd) means the tearing of cloth lengthwise. «لفاء» (Lafā) refers to finding something suddenly.

Messages

1- Merely saying Ma'ādh Allāh (I seek refuge in Allah) is not enough; one must flee from sin. «استبقا»

2- Sometimes the outward appearance of an action is the same, but the goals are different. (One runs so as not to be tainted by sin; the other runs to cause defilement.) «استبقا»

3- Migration (Hegira) and fleeing from sin are necessary. «استبقا الباب»

4- The excuse that the door is closed is not sufficient reason to surrender to sin; one must move toward closed doors, perhaps they will open. «استبقا الباب»

5- The criminal usually leaves traces behind. «قدّت قمیصه من دُبُر»*

6- Sometimes one must unexpectedly inspect homes and workplaces. «الفَیا»*

7- Sometimes the accuser is the criminal himself. «قالت ما جزاء...»

8- The sinner, in order to exonerate himself, appeals to the emotions and sentiments of his relatives. «باهلك»

9- Those in power usually accuse others if they themselves are guilty. «مَن اراد باهلك سوء»*

10- The criminal, in order to exonerate himself, levels accusations against others. «اراد باهلك سوء»

11- How often is a true statement used to pursue falsehood. (Punishment for someone who intends evil against a married woman is a true principle, but which individual intended evil must be investigated.) «اراد باهلك سوءً الاّ ان یسجن»*

12- Zulaikha was not a lover, but rather lustful; because a true lover is willing to sacrifice his life for the beloved, not accuse him and cast him into prison. «اراد باهلك سوء...»*

13- Imprisonment and the jailing of criminals have historical precedent. «یسجن»

14- Declaring the punishment was a sign of the power held by the wife of the Azīz. «یسجن او عذاب الیم»

15- Lustful passion sometimes turns the lover into a killer. «ان یسجن او عذاب الیم»

16- Zulaikha possessed influence within the governmental apparatus and was able to dictate policy. «الاّ ان یسجن او عذاب الیم»*

Ayahs

قَالَ هِىَ رَٰوَدَتْنِى عَن نَّفْسِى ۚ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِدٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِهَآ إِن كَانَ قَمِيصُهُۥ قُدَّ مِن قُبُلٍ فَصَدَقَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۲٦)
He said, ‘It was she who solicited me.’ A witness of her own household testified [thus]: ‘If his shirt is torn from the front, she tells the truth and he lies. (26)
وَإِن كَانَ قَمِيصُهُۥ قُدَّ مِن دُبُرٍ فَكَذَبَتْ وَهُوَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۲۷)
But if his shirt is torn from behind, then she lies and he tells the truth.’ (27)

Key Points

In some narrations, the witness has been identified as a child who spoke in the cradle, like Jesus عليه السلام, but since it lacks a strong chain of transmission (sanad), it cannot be relied upon. However, it is better to consider the witness as one of the advisors of the Aziz (Minister), who was related to his wife and possessed intelligence and shrewdness, and thus, like the Aziz of Egypt, became a witness to this event. Otherwise, if the witness had been present for the original incident, it would make no sense for him to testify using a conditional sentence and say: "اِن كان..." (If it was...). Joseph (Yusuf), initially said nothing, and perhaps if the wife of the Aziz had not spoken and made an accusation, Joseph would not have been willing to expose her and say: "هى راودتنى" (She sought to seduce me). Sometimes some pure individuals have been subjected to accusations: Among women, there was none purer than Mary (Maryam), yet they accused her of impurity. Among men, no one was found as pure as Joseph, yet they also falsely accused him of adultery. But Allah proved their purity in the best possible way in both cases. In the story of Joseph, his shirt plays a crucial role: Here, the tearing of the shirt from the back became proof of his innocence and revealed the crime of the Aziz's wife. And in another place, the shirt not being torn led to the discovery of his brothers' crime. Because after throwing Joseph into the well, when the brothers stained his shirt with blood and showed it to their father, saying: "A wolf ate Joseph," the father asked: "Then why is his shirt not torn?!" Also, at the end of the story, Joseph's shirt became the means by which his father's eyes regained sight.

Messages

1- The accused must defend himself and identify the main culprit. Prophet Joseph عليه السلام gave an appropriate response to Zulaikha against the statement, "ما جزاء مَن اراد باهلك سوءً" (What is the recompense for him who intended evil against your wife?), by saying, "هِىَ راودتنى" (She sought to seduce me).*

2- One who is in the position of judgment must hear the complaint of the plaintiff and the defense of the accused, examine the evidence, and then give an opinion. (In this incident, the wife said that Joseph intended evil, "اراد باهلك سوء"; Joseph rejected the accusation and said, "هى راودتنى"; the witness also presented the sign of truth and falsehood through the tearing of the shirt from the front or the back).*

3- Defending the innocent is obligatory, and silence is not always commendable. "شهد شاهد" (A witness testified).

4- Helping to clarify the truth is a praiseworthy act. "و شهد شاهد" (And a witness testified).*

5- Allah supports individuals through means that are entirely unexpected. "شهد شاهد من اهلها" (A witness from her family testified).

6- Where Allah wills, the relatives of the criminal testify against him. "شهد شاهد من اهلها" (A witness from her family testified).

7- In testimony and judgment, observing the status, lineage, position, or kinship of the accused is not permissible. "شهد شاهد من اهلها" (A witness from her family testified).

8- The testimony of relatives against one another leads to greater certainty. "شاهد من اهلها" (A witness from her family).*

9- In judgment, attention should be paid to examining evidence and documents more than to the statements of the plaintiff and the accused. "قالت ما جزاءُ... قال هى راودتنى... شهد شاهد... ان كان قمیصه..." (She said, "What is the recompense..." He said, "She sought to seduce me..." A witness testified... "If his shirt was...")*

10- In judgment, every crime requires an expert. "شهد شاهد... ان كان قمیصه..." (A witness testified... If his shirt was...)*

11- In criminology, the criminal is identified and issues are discovered based on subtle remaining traces. "ان كان قمیصه..." (If his shirt was...).

12- A judge can rule based on circumstantial evidence (Qara'in). "ان كان قمیصه قُدّ من قُبُل فصدقت و هو من الكاذبین" (If his shirt was torn from the front, then she has told the truth, and he is of the liars).

13- Employing criminological methods is necessary for diagnosing the crime and the criminal. "ان كان قمیصه قدّ من دبر..." (If his shirt was torn from the back...).

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَءَا قَمِيصَهُۥ قُدَّ مِن دُبُرٍ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ مِن كَيْدِكُنَّ ۖ إِنَّ كَيْدَكُنَّ عَظِيمٌ (۲۸)
So when he saw that his shirt was torn from behind, he said, ‘This is [a case] of you women’s guile! Your guile is indeed great! (28)

Key Points

Although the Quran considers the plot (or cunning) of Satan weak: «انّ كید الشیطان كان ضعیفا»(51), in this Ayah, the plot of women is considered great (or mighty). According to Tafsir Safi, this is because the temptation (or whispering) of Satan is absent and stealthy, but the temptation of a woman is accompanied by kindness, affection, presence, and permanence. Sometimes Allah accomplishes great deeds through small means. For example, He brought about the downfall of Abraha's army with the Ababil birds, the preservation of the life of the Prophet of Islam with a spider's web, the teaching of burying the dead with a crow, the proof of the purity of Maryam علیها السلام with the speech of the infant, the purity of Yusuf (Joseph) with the tearing of the shirt, the faith of a nation with the journey of the hoopoe (Hudhud), and the discovery of the Companions of the Cave (Ashab al-Kahf) with a sample coin.

51) An-Nisa, 76.

Messages

1- Judgment based on evidence and free from affections and grudges is the requirement of justice. «فلمّا رأى قمیصه... قال انّه من كیدكنّ»*

2- Logical reasoning makes every person obedient and submissive. (The Aziz of Egypt became obedient to the witness's statement) «شهد شاهد... قال انّه من كیدكنّ»*

3- The signs and evidence at the crime scene must be carefully observed and examined. «قُدّ من دُبر»*

4- When you reach a definitive conclusion, do not hesitate in announcing the verdict. «فلمّا رأى... قال»*

5- The Aziz of Egypt possessed relative justice and fairness in dealing with matters. (Because he did not accuse anyone without inquiry and response, and thereafter he gave the right to Yusuf). «فلمّا رأى... انّه من كیدكنّ»*

6- The truth does not remain hidden, and the criminal will one day be exposed. «انّه من كیدكنّ»

7- We must accept the word of truth, even if it is bitter and detrimental. (The Aziz accepted that his wife was the culprit) «انّه من كیدكنّ»*

8- Fear the cunning of impure women, for their plot is dangerous. «انّ كیدكنّ عظیم»

9- Sometimes, cunning and plotting, no matter how great, are discoverable and exposed. «فلمّا رأى... قال انّه من كیدكنّ انّ كیدكنّ عظیم»

Ayah

يُوسُفُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَـٰذَا ۚ وَٱسْتَغْفِرِى لِذَنۢبِكِ ۖ إِنَّكِ كُنتِ مِنَ ٱلْخَاطِـِٔينَ (۲۹)
Joseph, let this matter alone, and you, woman, plead for forgiveness for your sin, for you have indeed been erring.’ (29)

Messages

1- The Aziz of Egypt wanted the matter to remain hidden, but the people of the world became aware of the incident throughout all centuries, so that Joseph's purity would be proven. «یوسف اعرض عن هذا»

2- The wicked deeds of individuals should not be exposed. «اعرض عن هذا»*

3- Due to his position, the Aziz of Egypt asked Joseph to overlook the incident. «اعرض عن هذا»*

4- The Aziz of Egypt, like other palace dwellers, showed leniency regarding the issue of honor and jealousy (ghayrah), contented himself with seeking forgiveness (istighfar), and seriously refrained from reprimanding his wife! «و استغفرى»

5- Non-divine leaders lack the power to deal decisively with their own transgressing relatives. «و استغفرى»

6- A woman's attempt to establish a relationship with someone other than her husband is an improper and illegitimate act. «واستغفرى لذنبك»

7- Unrestrained sexual relations and lust were considered reprehensible, even among those who did not adhere to divine religions. «استغفرى لذنبك»*

Ayah

۞ وَقَالَ نِسْوَةٌ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ ٱمْرَأَتُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ تُرَٰوِدُ فَتَىٰهَا عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۖ قَدْ شَغَفَهَا حُبًّا ۖ إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰهَا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳٠)
Some of the townswomen said, ‘The chieftain’s wife has solicited her slave boy! He has captivated her love. Indeed we see her to be in manifest error.’ (30)

Key Points

Shaghāf (شغاف) refers to the complexity at the top of the heart or the thin membrane covering the heart, which envelops it like a sheath. The phrase «شَغَفها حُبّاً» means that Yusuf's affection became intertwined with Zulaikha's heart, and the love became intense. (52) Two groups said regarding Yusuf: excessive affection for him is a sign of clear error (or misguidance): One group was his brothers, who, due to their father's love for Yusuf, called their father misguided and said: «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین» (Indeed, our father is in manifest error). The other group was the women of Egypt, who, due to Zulaikha's intense affection for Yusuf, called Zulaikha misguided and said: «انّا لنراها فى ضلال مبین» (Indeed, we see her in manifest error). Everyone desired Yusuf for himself; Ya'qub (Jacob) considered him his son; «یا بُنىّ» (O my son). The caravan considered him their commodity; «شروه بثمن بخس» (They sold him for a meager price). The Aziz (Minister) of Egypt considered him his adopted son; «نتّخذه ولدا» (We will take him as a son). Zulaikha considered him her beloved; «شغفها حبّاً» (She was passionately in love with him). The prisoners considered him the interpreter of their dreams; «نبّئنا بتأویله» (Inform us of its interpretation). But Allah considered him His chosen one and His Messenger; «یجتبیك ربّك» (Your Lord will choose you). And what remained for Yusuf was this very station of prophethood (or messengership). «واللّه غالب على امره» (And Allah is predominant over His affair).

52) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- News concerning the families of officials spreads quickly, so they must be careful of their behavior. «قال نسوة... اِمرأت العزیز» (Some women said... the wife of the Aziz).

2- Closing doors to commit sin does not prevent disgrace. «قال نسوة... اِمرأت العزیز تراود» (Some women said... the wife of the Aziz is soliciting).

3- The errors of family members are attributed to the man and head of the household. «امرئة العزیز تُراود فتاها»*

Ayah

فَلَمَّا سَمِعَتْ بِمَكْرِهِنَّ أَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَعْتَدَتْ لَهُنَّ مُتَّكَـًٔا وَءَاتَتْ كُلَّ وَٰحِدَةٍ مِّنْهُنَّ سِكِّينًا وَقَالَتِ ٱخْرُجْ عَلَيْهِنَّ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَيْنَهُۥٓ أَكْبَرْنَهُۥ وَقَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ وَقُلْنَ حَـٰشَ لِلَّهِ مَا هَـٰذَا بَشَرًا إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا مَلَكٌ كَرِيمٌ (۳۱)
When she heard of their machinations, she sent for them and arranged a repast, and gave each of them a knife, and said [to Joseph], ‘Come out before them.’ So when they saw him, they marvelled at him and cut their hands [absent-mindedly], and they said, ‘Good heavens! This is not a human being! This is but a noble angel!’ (31)

Key Points

Qatta'na (قطّعن) is derived from the root qata', meaning "to cut." Since it is in the Taf'il form (باب تفعیل), it also conveys the meaning that when the women present at the gathering saw Joseph, they were so stunned that they cut multiple places on their hands, slicing them into pieces. Hasha (حاشا) means to be on the margin or aside. It was customary that whenever they wished to declare a person free from a fault, they would first declare Allah free from imperfection (tanzeeh), and then that person. (53) The wife of the Aziz (Minister) of Egypt was a politically astute woman, and by hosting a banquet, she was able to expose the intentions of her rivals and take them by surprise.

53) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

  1. Sometimes the goal of recounting the affairs of others is not sympathy, but rather jealousy, conspiracy, and plotting against them. Bi-makrihinna (بمكرهنّ)
  2. Perhaps the women of Egypt, by spreading the news of Zulaikha's love for Joseph عليه السلام, intended to see Joseph themselves. Bi-makrihinna (بمكرهنّ)*
  3. Sometimes, cunning must be answered with cunning. (The women plotted by revealing the secret of the Aziz's wife, and she responded to their plot with a banquet.) Arsalat ilayhinna (ارسلت الیهنّ)
  4. To control the negative atmosphere created against her, Zulaikha proceeded to justify her actions to the key individuals involved in the rumor-mongering and news dissemination. (It is clear that the invitees were not all the women of the region.) Falammā sami‘at bi-makrihinna arsalat ilayhinna (فلمّا سمعت بمكرهنّ ارسلت الیهنّ)*
  5. Sometimes, the offender, in order to clear herself, expands the offense and makes it appear normal. Falammā sami‘at bi-makrihinna arsalat ilayhinna (فلمّا سمعت بمكرهنّ ارسلت الیهنّ)*
  6. Providing separate service for each guest is a commendable and valuable practice. Ātat min wāḥidatin minhunna sikkīnan (آتت من واحدة منهنّ سكّیناً)*
  7. Obedience to the master and owner is necessary as long as it does not involve disobedience (to Allah). Qālat ukhruj ‘alayhinna falammā ra’aynahu (قالت اخرج علیهنّ فلمّا رأینه)*
