  8. Hearing is never like seeing. Falammā ra’aynahu akbarnahu (فلمّا رأینه اكبرنه)*
  9. Man naturally shows humility in the face of greatness and nobility. Akbarnahu (اكبرنه)
  10. Zulaikha wanted to make the women of Egypt understand: You saw Joseph for one moment and lost control; what about me, who has Him in my house day and night?! Falammā ra’aynahu akbarnahu wa qaṭṭa‘na aydiyahunna (فلمّا رأینه اكبرنه و قطّعن ایدیهنّ)*
  11. Do not rush to criticize; perhaps if you were in her place, you would have become like her. Qaṭṭa‘na aydiyahunna (قطّعن ایدیهنّ). (When the critics saw Joseph, they all became afflicted just like the wife of the Aziz of Egypt.)
  12. A person makes claims until they are exposed to a test; but sometimes, when tested, they unconsciously reveal their true nature. Falammā ra’aynahu akbarnahu wa qaṭṭa‘na aydiyahunna (فلمّا رأینه اكبرنه و قطّعن ایدیهنّ)*
  13. When love arrives, a person does not feel the cutting of their own hand. Qaṭṭa‘na aydiyahunna (قطّعن ایدیهنّ). (If you have heard that when Hadrat Ali عليه السلام was praying, an arrow was pulled from his foot and he did not notice, do not be surprised. For if superficial worldly love leads to the cutting of hands, what will spiritual and profound love for true Beauty achieve!)
  14. Joseph was unparalleled in beauty and, at the same time, a model of chastity for everyone. Falammā ra’aynahu akbarnahu... qulna ḥāsha lillāh (فلمّا رأینه اكبرنه... قلن حاش للّه)*
  15. The beauty of Joseph عليه السلام caused him trouble, but his knowledge and piety led to his salvation. (Indeed, spiritual beauty is more important than physical beauty.)*
  16. The people of Egypt at that time believed in Allah and the angels. Ḥāsha lillāh... malakun karīm (حاش للّه... ملك كریم)

Ayah

قَالَتْ فَذَٰلِكُنَّ ٱلَّذِى لُمْتُنَّنِى فِيهِ ۖ وَلَقَدْ رَٰوَدتُّهُۥ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ فَٱسْتَعْصَمَ ۖ وَلَئِن لَّمْ يَفْعَلْ مَآ ءَامُرُهُۥ لَيُسْجَنَنَّ وَلَيَكُونًا مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰغِرِينَ (۳۲)
She said, ‘He is the one on whose account you blamed me. Certainly, I did solicit him, but he was continent, and if he does not do what I bid him, surely he shall be imprisoned and humbled.’ (32)

Key Points

Social and psychological conditions influence the type of reaction individuals exhibit. The wife of the Aziz (Minister), when she feared the exposure of her wicked deed, closed the doors, «غلقّت الابواب». But when she saw the women of Egypt supporting and agreeing with her, she openly said: «أنا راودته» (I solicited him). Similarly, in society, when sensitivity toward the ugliness of sin disappears, sin becomes easy. Perhaps it is to prevent this very thing that we read in the Kumayl Supplication: «اللّهم اغفر لى الذنوب الّتى تَهتك العِصَم» (O Allah, forgive me the sins which tear the veils of modesty). Because sin is initially difficult for a person to commit, but as soon as the veil of modesty falls, it becomes easy.

Messages

1- Do not blame others, lest you become afflicted yourselves. «فذلكنّ الّذى لمتنّنى فیه»

2- Sinful love leads to disgrace. «لقد راودته»

3- A liar is disgraced. The one who yesterday said: Joseph intended evil; «اراد باهلك سوء» today says: «لقد راودته» (I intended to seek gratification from him).

4- The sincerity and chastity of the pure cause the disgrace of the impure. «و لقد راودته عن نفسه»*

5- Sometimes even the enemy testifies to the purity of the opposing person. «فاستعصم» (The conscience of the criminal sometimes awakens.)

6- Purity is a prerequisite for prophethood. «فاستعصم ...»

7- How many pure individuals go to prison because of the self-indulgent. «فاستعصم... لیسجننّ»

8- Sinners use every means to achieve their goals. «و لئن لم یفعل ما آمره لیسجننّ»*

9- If power is not accompanied by faith and piety, it is used for selfish desires. «ما آمره لیسجننّ»*

10- Zulaikha had influence within the governmental apparatus of Egypt. «لیسجننّ»*

11- Abuse of power is the weapon of tyrants. «لیسجننّ»

12- Threatening imprisonment and humiliation is the weapon and method of tyrants. «لیسجننّ،... الصاغرین»

13- Selfish desire is so powerful that it continues its path even despite disgrace. «راودته... و لئن لم یفعل ما آمره...»*

14- A defeated lover turns into an enemy. «قالت... لیسجنّن و لیكوناً من الصاغرین»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ ٱلسِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِىٓ إِلَيْهِ ۖ وَإِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّى كَيْدَهُنَّ أَصْبُ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (۳۳)
He said, ‘My Lord! The prison is dearer to me than to what they invite me. If You do not turn away their schemes from me, I will incline towards them and become one of the senseless.’ (33)

Key Points

Joseph was a man of complete integrity. Once, he was sacrificed due to his brothers' envy, yet he bore no malice. Another time, he became the object of Zulaikha's desire, but he did not commit sin. A third time, when he gained power, he did not take revenge on his brothers. A fourth time, as soon as he saw the country in danger, instead of asking to return to his homeland, he requested the management of economic affairs and the salvation of the country. Everyone has something beloved; for Joseph, chastity was more beloved than prison. For one group, the world is more beloved; «الّذین یستحبّون الحیاة الدنیا»(54) but for the believer, Allah is more beloved. «والّذین آمنوا اشدّ حبّاً للّه»(55)

54) Ibrahim, 3. 55) Baqarah, 165.

Messages

1- Attention to the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah is among the etiquette of supplication (Du'a). «ربّ»

2- The friends of Allah (Awliya') consider the hardship of an honorable life better than comfort found in sin. «ربّ السجن احبّ الىّ»

3- Not every freedom is a virtue, and not every prison is a fault. «ربّ السّجن احبّ»

4- By seeking help from Allah, a person can distance himself from sin in any circumstance. «ربّ السجن احبّ» (Migration from an environment of sin is necessary.)

5- Suffering and hardship cannot be a justification for committing sin. «ربّ السجن احبّ...»

6- Supplication (Du'a), prayer, and seeking help from Allah is one of the ways to remain protected from sin and sexual deviations. «ربّ السجن احبّ...»

7- A person's character depends on his soul, not his body. If the soul is free, prison is paradise, and if the soul is under pressure, a palace becomes a prison. «السجن احبّ»

8- Separating the environment to protect individuals from sin is a worthy action. (Joseph desired separation, even if it meant going to prison).*

9- The satisfaction of Allah takes precedence over the satisfaction of people. «ربّ السجن احبّ الىّ ممّا یدعوننى الیه»*

10- No one remains protected without the grace of Allah. «و الاّ تصرف عنّى...» In critical situations, the only path to salvation is reliance upon Allah.

11- Divine tests become harder moment by moment. (Joseph was previously afflicted by one woman; now He is afflicted by several women.) «كیدهنّ، الیهنّ»

12- Satisfying people at the cost of displeasing Allah is ignorance (Jahl). «اكن من الجاهلین»

13- Sin causes the removal of God-given knowledge and divine blessings. (In previous verses, we read: «آتیناه حكماً و علماً» and here Joseph says:) «اكن من الجاهلین»

14- Ignorance (Jahl) is not merely illiteracy; choosing momentary pleasure and overlooking the satisfaction of Allah is pure ignorance. «اكن من الجاهلین»

15- Sin is an ignorant act. «اكن من الجاهلین»*

16- Failing to act upon one's knowledge places a person among the ignorant. «اكن من الجاهلین»*

Ayah

فَٱسْتَجَابَ لَهُۥ رَبُّهُۥ فَصَرَفَ عَنْهُ كَيْدَهُنَّ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۳٤)
So his Lord answered him and turned away their stratagems from him. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (34)

Messages

1- Men of Allah are those whose supplications are answered. «فاستجاب له»

2- Sometimes severe difficulties and hardships pave the way for the emergence of Divine aid and the path to salvation. «ربّ السجن... فاستجاب»*

3- Purity and chastity provide the means for the acceptance of supplication. «ربّ السجن احبّ... فاستجاب»*

4- A sincere request from Allah leads to His acceptance and favor. «ربّ... فاستجاب»*

5- Yusuf’s supplication to remain safe from the cunning of the women of Egypt was immediately answered. (The letter fā’ in «فاستجاب» symbolizes speed.)*

6- Whoever seeks refuge in Allah remains protected. «فاستجاب له ربّه فصرف عنه كيدهنّ»

7- Sometimes the enemy's action ends up benefiting us. (The enemy threw Yusuf into prison, which was the means of Yusuf’s deliverance from Zulaikha.) «فصرف عنه كيدهنّ»*

8- The acceptance of supplication is proof of Allah’s Hearing, Seeing, and Knowledge. «فاستجاب... هو السمیع العلیم»

9- Those whom people consult must first listen carefully, and then proceed to resolve the issue using their knowledge; just as Allah acts in this manner when answering supplication. «فاستجاب... هو السمیع العلیم»*

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُۥ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (۳٥)
Then it appeared to them, after they had seen all the signs [of his innocence], that they should confine him for some time. (35)

Messages

1- One fool throws a stone into a well, and a hundred wise men cannot retrieve it. One woman became infatuated, and numerous men and state officials could not devise a solution for this scandal. «بدا لهم من بعد ما رأوا»

2- Dwelling in palaces is usually accompanied by recklessness and impudence. «من بعد ما رأوا الایات لیسجننّه» Despite all these proofs of Yusuf's purity, he is still condemned to prison.

3- Beauty does not always bring happiness; it also brings trouble. «ثمّ بدا لهم... لیسجننّه»

4- Usually, in the courts and palaces of tyrants, trials are conducted in absentia and are merely ceremonial, leading to the condemnation of the innocent. «لیسجننّه» (However, this is not the case in all instances.)

5- In a corrupt society, pleasure-seekers are free while the pious are imprisoned. «لیسجننّه»*

6- Erring individuals, especially the powerful, rarely accept responsibility for their actions and seek to sacrifice others. «لیسجننّه»*

7- In tyrannical systems, the most innocent is the most oppressed. «لیسجننّه»*

8- Being pure and remaining pure has a cost. «لیسجننّه»*

9- Preventing the scandal of the family of the Aziz of Egypt and putting an end to the rumors was the goal of the rulers of Egypt in imprisoning Yusuf. «لیسجننّه حتّى حین»*

10- When the judicial system makes decisions according to the wishes of politicians and influential individuals, the duration of the imprisonment is also unspecified. «حتّى حین»*

Ayah

وَدَخَلَ مَعَهُ ٱلسِّجْنَ فَتَيَانِ ۖ قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَآ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰنِىٓ أَعْصِرُ خَمْرًا ۖ وَقَالَ ٱلْـَٔاخَرُ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰنِىٓ أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِى خُبْزًا تَأْكُلُ ٱلطَّيْرُ مِنْهُ ۖ نَبِّئْنَا بِتَأْوِيلِهِۦٓ ۖ إِنَّا نَرَىٰكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۳٦)
There entered the prison two youths along with him. One of them said, ‘I dreamt that I am pressing grapes.’ The other said, ‘I dreamt that I am carrying bread on my head from which the birds are eating.’ ‘Inform us of its interpretation,’ [they said], ‘for indeed we see you to be a virtuous man.’ (36)

Key Points

We read in a Hadith: The reason why the prisoners called Yusuf a doer of good was that he attended to the sick people in the prison, helped the needy, and made space for others. (56)

56) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn - Mizan al-Hikmah (Sijn).

Messages

1- Yusuf's prison was public (or shared). «معه السِجن»

2- We should not take dreams lightly; secrets are hidden in some of them. «أرانى أعصر خمراً» (It is possible for ordinary people to also see important dreams.)

3- A person can be fruitful everywhere, even in prison. «نبّئنا بتأویله»*

4- Doers of good perceive things with purity of heart that others do not find. «نبّئنا بتأویله انّا نراك من المحسنين»*

5- If people gain trust in someone, they share all their secrets with him. «انّا نراك من المحسنين»

6- Virtuous people influence others even while in prison. «انّا نراك من المحسنين»

7- Even criminals and sinners acknowledge a worthy status for the doers of good. «انّا نراك من المحسنين»

8- Doers of good are respected and are a refuge for people everywhere, even in prison. «انّا نراك من المحسنين»*

9- The benevolence of Yusuf عليه السلام was his first step in attraction and propagation (Da'wah). (Kindness and service to the prisoners caused hearts to be attracted and earned him the title of Muhsin (doer of good) from the prisoners.) *

10- One can be a Muhsin (doer of good) even without financial means or freedom. (Yusuf had neither wealth nor freedom in prison.) «انّا نراك من المحسنين»*

11- First prove your benevolence, then engage in propagating the religion. (Yusuf, who was known as a doer of good in the eyes of the people, engaged in propagation and guidance and invited them to monotheism before interpreting the dreams of his prison companions.)*

12- Providing correct knowledge is an instance of Ihsan (benevolence). (Inform us of the secrets of the dream we have seen.) «انّا نراك من المحسنين»*

Ayah

قَالَ لَا يَأْتِيكُمَا طَعَامٌ تُرْزَقَانِهِۦٓ إِلَّا نَبَّأْتُكُمَا بِتَأْوِيلِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمَا مِمَّا عَلَّمَنِى رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنِّى تَرَكْتُ مِلَّةَ قَوْمٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۳۷)
He said, ‘Before the meals you are served come to you I will inform you of its interpretation. That is among things my Lord has taught me. Indeed, I renounce the creed of the people who have no faith in Allah and who [also] disbelieve in the Hereafter. (37)

Key Points

Regarding the translation of the first part of the Ayah, there is also the possibility that the meaning of the Ayah is this: I know from Allah what food will be brought to you. Therefore, I can interpret your dream. This means that in addition to interpreting dreams, Joseph (Yusuf) also gave news about other things. For example, like Prophet Jesus (Isa) عليه السلام who gave news about the food stored in homes or what they were eating.

Question: Why did Prophet Joseph عليه السلام not interpret their dreams immediately, but postponed it until another time and an hour later?

Answer: Fakhr Razi, in his Tafsir Kabir, has stated several reasons for this: 1. He wanted to keep them waiting so that he could preach and guide them a little, perhaps the person who was to be executed would embrace faith and leave this world with a good end. 2. He wanted to gain their trust by describing the type of food that had not yet arrived. 3. He wanted to make them thirstier so they would listen better. 4. Since the interpretation of one of their dreams involved execution, he slightly delayed it so that the person would not collapse (or lose heart).

Messages

1- Sometimes, for greater impact, it is necessary for a person to present his scientific power and perfections to others. «قال... نبّأتكما بتأویله»

2- We should use our knowledge to benefit others. «نبّأتكما... ممّا علّمنى ربّى»*

3- We should consider our information and knowledge to be from Allah. «ممّا علّمنى ربّى»

4- The goal of education is also nurturing (spiritual development). «علّمنى ربّى»

5- Allah is Wise and does not open a door for someone without reason. «علّمنى ربّى» because I; «تركتُ ملّة قوم لایؤمنون»

6- Whoever flees from the darkness of disbelief reaches the light of knowledge. «علّمنى ربّى انّى تركت» (The reason for my knowledge is the abandonment of disbelief.)

7- Make the best use of opportunities. «نبّاتكما بتأویله... انّى تركتُ ملّة» (Joseph began his cultural and ideological work before interpreting the dream.)

8- The foundation of faith is Tabarri (disassociation) and Tawalli (allegiance). In this Ayah, disassociation from the disbelievers is mentioned, «انّى تركت», and in the next Ayah, following the guardianship (Wilayah) of the Divine Saints is mentioned. «واتّبعت»

9- One must avoid directly attributing deviation to individuals and should preach using an indirect method. (Joseph did not say: You abandon disbelief, but rather said: I abandoned the path of disbelief.) «انّى تركتُ ملّة قوم لایؤمنون»*

10- In all religions, the belief in Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) have been side by side. «قوم لایؤمنون باللَّه و هم بالاخرة هم كافرون»

Ayah

وَٱتَّبَعْتُ مِلَّةَ ءَابَآءِىٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۚ مَا كَانَ لَنَآ أَن نُّشْرِكَ بِٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْنَا وَعَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (۳۸)
I follow the creed of my fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. It is not for us to ascribe any partner to Allah. That is by virtue of Allah’s grace upon us and upon all mankind, but most people do not give thanks. (38)

Key Points

Family lineage, just as it is effective in shaping the personality of individuals, also influences people's acceptance. Therefore, Prophet Joseph (Yusuf), in introducing himself, relies on his fathers who are Divine Prophets, both to present his noble lineage and to demonstrate the sanctity of his call. This is the same method that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used in introducing himself, saying: "I am the unlettered Prophet whose name and description are mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel." Sayyid al-Shuhada (Master of Martyrs) Husayn ibn Ali علیهما السلام in Karbala, and Imam Sajjad علیه السلام before the people of Sham (Syria), also introduced themselves in this manner: "I am the son of Fatimah al-Zahra."

The term Millah (ملّة) in the Quran is used to mean religion or creed. The characteristics of the Millah of Abraham are outlined as follows: "Strive hard for Allah as is His due; be constant in prayer, give Zakat, and hold fast to Allah. There is no hardship or constraint in the religion; submit to Allah. This is the Millah of your father Abraham." (57)

57) Hajj, 78.

Messages

1- Reaching the truth depends on recognizing falsehood and abandoning it. «تركتُ ملّة قوم لا یؤمنون و اتّبعتُ ملّة...»

2- A person's grandfather is considered equivalent to his father, and the term Ab (أب - father) is also applied to him. «ملّة آبائى ابراهیم و اسحاق ویعقوب»

3- Prophets must come from pure lineages. «آبائى ابراهیم و...»

4- Divine Prophets share a single goal. «ملّة آبائى ابراهیم واسحاق و...»

5- Alongside negative paths, we must also show the positive path. «تركتُ ملّة... واتّبعتُ ملّة...»

6- Taking pride in one's fathers and following them is commendable and permissible, provided they followed the path of truth and Monotheism (Tawhid). «واتّبعت ملّة آبائى...»*

7- The fathers of the Prophets علیهم السلام were not polytheists. «ما كان لنا ان نشرك»*

8- Avoiding polytheism (Shirk) and fighting against it is the foundation and basis of Divine religions. «ما كان لنا ان نشرك»*

9- Polytheism, in all its dimensions (Essence, Attributes, and worship), is detestable. «من شى ء»

10- Avoiding polytheism and inclining toward Monotheism requires Divine success (Tawfiq). «ذلك من فضل اللَّه»

11- The teachings of the Prophets are a Divine blessing and deserve gratitude and thanks. «ذلك من فضل اللّه علینا... و لكنّ اكثر الناس لایشكرون»*

12- Prophethood and guidance are Divine success and grace for everyone. «علینا و على النّاس»

13- The majority is not the criterion for correct recognition. «اكثر النّاس لا یشكرون»

14- Turning away from the path of the Prophets is the greatest ingratitude for blessings. «لایشكرون»

Ayah

يَـٰصَـٰحِبَىِ ٱلسِّجْنِ ءَأَرْبَابٌ مُّتَفَرِّقُونَ خَيْرٌ أَمِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ (۳۹)
O my prison mates! Are different masters better, or Allah, the One, the All-paramount? (39)

Key Points

Humans are of three categories: A group is moldable, like water and air, which have no shape of their own and take the form of whatever container they are placed in. A group is impenetrable and resistant, like iron and steel, which stand firm against external pressure. But a group consists of Imams (leaders) and guides who bring others into the color of truth (Haqq). Joseph (Yusuf) is an example of the third category of humans, who, even in prison, transforms a polytheist (mushrik) into a monotheist (muwahhid). In the Quran, the method of comparison and questioning is used in various places. We point to some examples of this concerning Allah:

  1. «قل هل من شركائكم من یبدأ الخلق ثمّ یعیده »(58); Is there any among your partners (of Allah) who originates creation and then repeats it?
  2. «قل هل من شركائكم من یهدى الى الحقّ»(59); Is there any among your partners (of Allah) who guides to the truth (Haqq)?
  3. «قل أغیر اللَّه اَبغى ربّاً و هو ربّ كلّ شى ء»(60); Shall I seek a Lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of everything?
  4. «آللَّه خیرٌ امّا یشركون»(61); Is Allah better, or that which they associate (with Him)?

58) Yunus, 34. 59) Yunus, 35. 60) An'am, 164. 61) Naml, 59.

Messages

1- Address people with affection and emotion. «یا صاحبى»

2- Man is responsible even towards his companions. «یا صاحبى السجن»*

3- Utilize sensitive times and places for propagation (Tabligh). «یا صاحبى السجن ءارباب متفرّقون...» (As soon as Joseph saw that they needed him to interpret their dreams, he seized the opportunity and propagated the message.)

4- Questioning and comparison is one of the methods of guidance (Irshad) and direction. «ءارباب متفرّقون خیر...»

5- Belief in Monotheism (Tawhid) is the foundation of faith (Iman). (Joseph made Tawhid the starting point of his invitation.) «ءارباب متفرّقون خیر...»*

6- Multiple gods are a factor of division and dispersion, while the One God is a factor of unity. «ءارباب متفرّقون خیر أم اللّه...»*

7- Human nature (Fitrah) is averse to division and fragmentation, and Joseph utilized this very point to prove Monotheism (Tawhid). «ءارباب متفرّقون خیر أم اللّه الواحد...»*

8- Unity and oneness are factors of power and dominance. «الواحد القهّار»*

Ayah

مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَسْمَآءً سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ إِنِ ٱلْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۚ أَمَرَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِيَّاهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْقَيِّمُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٤٠)
You do not worship besides Him but [mere] names that you and your fathers have coined, for which Allah has not sent down any authority. Sovereignty belongs only to Allah. He has commanded you to worship none except Him. That is the upright religion, but most people do not know. (40)

Messages

1- Deities other than Him are not real; rather, they are products of the imagination of the polytheists and their ancestors. «ماتعبدون... الاّ اسماء سمّیتموها انتم و اباؤكم»

2- Many powers, organizations, institutions, seminars, resolutions, meetings, supports, condemnations, and other titles and appellations are names without substance (empty names) and modern idols of our time, which humanity follows instead of Allah. «ما تعبدون ... اسماء سمّیتموها»

3- History and antiquity are not proof of righteousness (or legitimacy). «انتم و آبائكم ما انزل اللّه بها من سلطان»*

4- Human beliefs must rely on rational or transmitted (textual) evidence and proof. «من سلطان»

5- Do not submit to any non-Divine command, because the right to command belongs solely to Allah. «ان الحكم الاّ للّه»

6- Sincere worship (Tawhid/Monotheism) is the straight and enduring path. «الاّ تعبدوا الاّ ایّاه ذلك الدّین القیّم»*

7- Every law except the Divine Law is unstable. «ذلك الدّین القیّم»

8- One should not rely on anything except a firm and stable belief. «ذلك الدّین القیّم»*

9- Most people are ignorant of the stability of Allah's religion. «ذلك الدین القیّم و لكن اكثر النّاس لایعلمون» (This refers either to Jahil Basit [simple ignorance], where one is aware of his own ignorance, or Jahil Murakkab [compound ignorance], where one imagines he knows but in reality does not).

10- Ignorance and unawareness pave the way for the emergence of polytheism (Shirk). «لا یعلمون»

Ayah

يَـٰصَـٰحِبَىِ ٱلسِّجْنِ أَمَّآ أَحَدُكُمَا فَيَسْقِى رَبَّهُۥ خَمْرًا ۖ وَأَمَّا ٱلْـَٔاخَرُ فَيُصْلَبُ فَتَأْكُلُ ٱلطَّيْرُ مِن رَّأْسِهِۦ ۚ قُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ ٱلَّذِى فِيهِ تَسْتَفْتِيَانِ (٤۱)
O my prison mates! As for one of you, he will serve wine to his master, and as for the other, he will be crucified and vultures will eat from his head. The matter about which you inquire has been decided.’ (41)

Key Points

The term Rabb («ربّ») is also applied to a ruler, an owner, and a master. For example, Rabb ad-Dar («ربّ الدار») means the owner of the house. Thus, the phrase «فیسقى ربّه خمراً» means he gives wine to his master.

Messages

  1. Respect the dignity of individuals, even if they do not follow your line of thought. «یا صاحبى»
  2. When responding to those who seek guidance, the order (of precedence) must be observed. «امّا احدكما...» (The first interpretation is for the one who narrated his dream earlier.)
  3. Some dreams, even if they come from a non-monotheist, can have an important interpretation. «فیسقى ربّه خمراً»
  4. The interpreter may state the interpretation of the dream, even if it is unpleasant. («فیصلب» means he will be crucified/hanged).*
  5. Joseph's interpretation of the dream is not a prediction or a guess, but rather certain news from Allah. «قُضى الامر»

Ayah

وَقَالَ لِلَّذِى ظَنَّ أَنَّهُۥ نَاجٍ مِّنْهُمَا ٱذْكُرْنِى عِندَ رَبِّكَ فَأَنسَىٰهُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ ذِكْرَ رَبِّهِۦ فَلَبِثَ فِى ٱلسِّجْنِ بِضْعَ سِنِينَ (٤۲)
Then he said to the one whom he knew would be delivered from among the two: ‘Mention me to your master.’ But Satan caused him to forget mentioning [it] to his master. So he remained in the prison for several years. (42)

Key Points

The term ẓann (ظنّ) is also used in the meaning of belief (i'tiqād) and knowledge ('ilm). This is because, in the previous Ayah, Joseph explicitly and decisively informed them of the release of one and the execution of the other. Therefore, ẓann here does not mean conjecture accompanied by doubt and uncertainty.

Biḍ' (بضع) refers to a number less than ten, and most commentators have stated the duration of Prophet Joseph's imprisonment was seven years.

In some interpretations, the phrase فانساه الشیطان (Fānsāhu ash-Shayṭān) has been translated as: "Satan removed the remembrance of the Lord from Joseph's mind, and instead of seeking help from Allah, he turned his attention to the king's cupbearer." This was considered a tark al-awlā (leaving the preferable) for Joseph, and thus he remained in prison for several more years.

However, the author of Al-Mīzān writes: Such narrations contradict the Quran, because the Quran considers Joseph among the sincere ones (al-mukhlaṣīn), and Satan has no influence over the sincere ones. Furthermore, two Ayahs later it states: قال الّذى نجا منهما و ادّكر بعد امّة (The one who was saved from the two said, and remembered after a long time). The forgetful person remembered Joseph after a long period, which indicates that the forgetfulness was related to the cupbearer, not Joseph.

Messages

  1. Prophets also solve their problems through conventional means, and this does not contradict Monotheism (Tawḥīd) or reliance (Tawakkul). اُذكرنى عند ربّك
  2. Not every request is a bribe. اُذكرنى عند ربّك Joseph did not ask for wages or a bribe for interpreting the dream; rather, he said: Convey my oppression to the king.
  3. To prove your innocence and purity, utilize every sound method to convey your complaint to the ears of the authorities. اُذكرنى عند ربّك
  4. Dwelling in palaces, comfort, and luxury sets the stage for forgetting the pain and suffering of the afflicted and the destitute. ناج... فانساه الشیطان
  5. People usually forget old friends after attaining position, status, and comfort. فانساه الشیطان ذكر ربّه
  6. Joseph's release from prison and the clearing of his name were incompatible with Satan's goals; therefore, he plotted (a scheme). فانساه الشیطان

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلِكُ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ سَبْعَ بَقَرَٰتٍ سِمَانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجَافٌ وَسَبْعَ سُنۢبُلَـٰتٍ خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يَابِسَـٰتٍ ۖ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَأُ أَفْتُونِى فِى رُءْيَـٰىَ إِن كُنتُمْ لِلرُّءْيَا تَعْبُرُونَ (٤۳)
[One day] the king said, ‘I saw [in a dream] seven fat cows being devoured by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and [seven] others [that were] dry. O courtiers, give me your opinion about my dream, if you can interpret dreams.’ (43)

Key Points

So far in this Surah, four dreams have been mentioned: the dream of Joseph himself, the dream of the two prison companions, and the dream of the King of Egypt. The first dream caused trouble for him, but the interpretation of the others' dreams led to his honor (or elevation). It is mentioned in the Torah: The King dreamt once that thin cows were eating fat cows, and another time, he saw green ears of grain beside dried ears of grain. (62) There is discussion and disagreement regarding whether the 'Aziz (Minister) of Egypt is the same person as the King of Egypt, or if they are two different people. We will pass over this, as it plays no role in the current discussion. It is stated in the book Rawdat al-Kafi that dreaming is of three types: sometimes it is divine glad tidings, sometimes it is the creation of fear by Satan, and sometimes it is confused and meaningless dreams. (63)

62) Tafsir al-Mizan. 63) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Allah saves a nation from famine through the dream of an oppressive king (provided that Joseph is the interpreter). «قال الملك انّى أرى »

2- Objects and animals have specific symbols and representations in the world of dreams. (For example, a thin cow symbolizes famine, and a fat cow symbolizes abundance.) «سبع بقرات سمان یأكلن سبع عجاف»*

3- Rulers and powerful individuals feel threatened by the slightest unpleasant memory, fearing that power might be taken from them. «قال الملك انّى أرى... أفتونى فى رؤیاى»

4- Rulers need consultation from experts in various matters. «یا ایّها الملأ افتونى»*

5- For dream interpretation, one must refer to those skilled in it, and should not pay attention to just anyone's interpretation. «أفتونى .... ان كنتم للرؤیا تعبرون»

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَضْغَـٰثُ أَحْلَـٰمٍ ۖ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِتَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحْلَـٰمِ بِعَـٰلِمِينَ (٤٤)
They said, ‘[These are] confused nightmares, and we do not know the interpretation of nightmares.’ (44)

Key Points

«اضغاث» (Aḍghāth) is the plural of «ضَغث» (ḍaghth), meaning mixing, and «ضِغْث» (ḍighth) means a mixed bundle of wood. «احلام» (Aḥlām) is the plural of «حلم» (ḥulm), meaning a confused dream. «اضغاث احلام» (Aḍghāthu Aḥlām) means scattered and confused dreams from which the interpreter cannot find a clue.

Messages

1- Do not justify your ignorance and lack of knowledge. (Since the nobles did not know the correct interpretation of the dream, they said: The king's dream is confused.) «قالوا اضغاث احلام»

2- Many dreams that appear scattered are, in fact, interpretable. «قالوا اضغاث احلام...»*

3- Work must be entrusted to the expert. (The expert interprets, but the non-expert says the dream is confused and uninterpretable.) «ما نحن بتأویل الاحلام بعالمین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِى نَجَا مِنْهُمَا وَٱدَّكَرَ بَعْدَ أُمَّةٍ أَنَا۠ أُنَبِّئُكُم بِتَأْوِيلِهِۦ فَأَرْسِلُونِ (٤٥)
Said the one of the two who had been delivered, remembering [Joseph] after a long time: ‘I will inform you of its interpretation; so let me go [to meet Joseph in the prison].’ (45)

Key Points

| Although Ummah (اُمّة) generally means a gathering of people, here it refers to a gathering of days (a period of time). (64)

64) Tafsir Kabir and Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Good deeds show their effect sooner or later. «ادّكر بعد اُمّة»

2- We should not only think of friends when we are in need. (As soon as they needed the interpretation of the dream, they remembered Joseph.) «وادّكر بعد امّة»*

3- Achieving status and position usually causes people to forget the past. «وادّكر بعد امّة»*

4- Whoever guides others to a task is as if he himself has performed that task. «اَنا انبّئكم»

5- Joseph’s fellow prisoner (عليه السلام) had such confidence in Joseph’s expertise that he gave the king a definite promise on his own behalf regarding the interpretation of the dream. «اَنا انبّئكم بتأویله»*

6- We should introduce the knowledgeable ones to society so that people may benefit from them. «فارسلون»

7- Some experts live in isolation; do not neglect them. «فارسلون»

8- We must go to the master (teacher), rather than summoning the master. «فارسلون»*

Ayah

يُوسُفُ أَيُّهَا ٱلصِّدِّيقُ أَفْتِنَا فِى سَبْعِ بَقَرَٰتٍ سِمَانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَبْعٌ عِجَافٌ وَسَبْعِ سُنۢبُلَـٰتٍ خُضْرٍ وَأُخَرَ يَابِسَـٰتٍ لَّعَلِّىٓ أَرْجِعُ إِلَى ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (٤٦)
‘Joseph,’ [he said], ‘O truthful one, give us your opinion concerning seven fat cows who are eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears and [seven] others dry, that I may return to the people so that they may know [the truth of the matter].’ (46)

Key Points

"Siddiq" (The Truthful/Veracious) is applied to someone whose speech, actions, and beliefs confirm one another. Yusuf's friend called him Siddiq because he had witnessed Yusuf's conduct and speech in prison, and furthermore, the interpretations of his own dream and his friend's dream had proven true. Allah called Ibrahim "Siddiq," (انّه كان صدّیقاً) (65) and made him His intimate friend (Khalil); (و اتّخذ اللّه ابراهیم خلیلاً) (66) He called Maryam (Mary) Siddiqah, (و امّه صدّیقة) (67) and chose her; (اِنّ اللّه اصطفاكِ) (68) He considered Yusuf Siddiq, (یوسف ایّها الصدّیق) (69) and granted him all kinds of authority; (و كذلك مكّنا لیوسف) (70) He called Idris Siddiq, (انّه كان صدّیقاً) (71) and considered him the possessor of a high rank. (رفعناه مكاناً علیاً) (72) And for those who are not at that degree, the path is to be in the company of the Siddiqin (the truthful). (فاؤلئك مع الّذین انعم اللّه علیهم من النّبیّین والصدّیقین...) (73) "Siddiq" is one of the titles that the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله bestowed upon Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. (74) The phrase (لعلّهم یعلمون) might mean that people become aware of Yusuf's valuable existence. That is, "I should return to the people so that they may know what a gem you are."

(65) Maryam, 41. (66) Nisa, 125. (67) Ma'idah, 75. (68) Aal-i-Imran, 42. (69) Yusuf, 46. (70) Yusuf, 56. (71) Maryam, 56. (72) Maryam, 57. (73) Nisa, 69. (74) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan and Kabir, regarding Ayah 28 of Surah Ghafir.

Messages

1- Before making a request, it is appropriate to honor the virtues of the person. (یوسف ایّها الصدیق)

2- We should ask our questions and problems from individuals with a good track record, who are Siddiq (truthful), honest in speech, and upright in action. (ایّها الصدّیق أفتنا...)

3- Governments are in need of the opinions of scholars and elites. (یوسف ایّها الصدّیق أفتنا...)

Ayah

قَالَ تَزْرَعُونَ سَبْعَ سِنِينَ دَأَبًا فَمَا حَصَدتُّمْ فَذَرُوهُ فِى سُنۢبُلِهِۦٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ (٤۷)
He said, ‘You will sow for seven consecutive years. Then leave in the ear whatever [grain] you harvest, except a little that you eat. (47)

Key Points

Joseph (عليه السلام), without complaint or grievance against his companion for forgetting him, and without setting any conditions for interpreting the king's dream, immediately proceeded to interpret the dream. This is because concealing knowledge, especially when the community is in need of it, is beneath the dignity of pure and virtuous people. Instead of merely interpreting the dream, Joseph articulated a clear plan for confronting the famine, demonstrating that in addition to the science of dream interpretation, he possessed the power of planning and management. The science of agriculture, the policy of stockpiling, and the necessity of economizing consumption are derived from this Ayah.

Messages

1- We should present our constructive plans and programs without expecting favor or reward. «قال تزرعون»*

2- To save society, one must overlook personal problems. (Joseph said nothing about his imprisonment, which was a personal matter; rather, He focused on solving the people's problem.) «قال تزرعون...»*

3- Crisis management and governing society under severe and difficult conditions are among the primary duties of the government. «قال تزرعون...»*

4- Governments must anticipate famine and drought and, through foresight, alleviate the pressure of problems during times of abundance. «تزرعون...»*

5 - Maximum utilization must be made when conditions are favorable. «تزرعون...»*

6- When a crisis is imminent, production and work capacity must be increased. «تزرعون...»*

7- Men of God must think about the welfare of the people and have long-term and short-term plans. «قال تزرعون سبع سنین»

8 - Time is a very important element in planning and management. «سبع سنین»*

9- Merely describing the situation is not enough; plans and programs must be provided. «تزرعون سبع سنین دأباً...»*

10- Planning in production «تزرعون سَبْعَ سِنِینَ» and economizing and stockpiling «فَذَرُوهُ» are necessities. (Whereas underdeveloped societies only consume without paying attention to these stages.)*

11- Government control over the process of production and distribution is essential during crisis conditions. «تزرعون... فذروه...»

12- If wheat remains in its ear (husk), its lifespan is extended. «فذروه فى سنبله»

13- Through planning, one can prepare to confront natural disasters such as famine, earthquake, and flood. «فذروه فى سنبله»

14- Planning and foresight for the future do not contradict reliance (Tawakkul) and submission to the command of Allah. «فذروه فى سنبله» (We should meet destiny with foresight.)

15- Plans must be practical. «فذروه فى سنبله» (The best practical method in that era, lacking silos and technology, was leaving the wheat in its ear.)

16- Not every bitterness is bad. This very famine became the prelude to Joseph's governance and also led to greater economizing and work among the people. «تزرعون، فذروه، الاّ قلیلا»

17- Farsightedness and long-term planning to confront the economic problems of society are prerequisites for national management. «تزرعون... فذروه... الاّ قلیلا»

18- Economizing today leads to self-sufficiency tomorrow, and extravagance today leads to need tomorrow. «قلیلاً ممّا تأكلون»

19- The dream of disbelievers can also express realities and contain instructions for preserving society.

20- Today, one must strive for a better tomorrow. «تزرعون... ثمّ یأتى من بعد ذلك»*

Ayahs

ثُمَّ يَأْتِى مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ سَبْعٌ شِدَادٌ يَأْكُلْنَ مَا قَدَّمْتُمْ لَهُنَّ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّمَّا تُحْصِنُونَ (٤۸)
Then after that there will come seven hard years which will eat up whatever you have set aside for them—all except a little which you preserve [for seed]. (48)
ثُمَّ يَأْتِى مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ عَامٌ فِيهِ يُغَاثُ ٱلنَّاسُ وَفِيهِ يَعْصِرُونَ (٤۹)
Then after that there will come a year wherein the people will be granted relief and provided with rains therein. (49)

Key Points

The phrase "یغاث الناس" (Yughāth al-Nās) is either derived from ghawth, meaning people are aided by Allah, or from ghayth, meaning rain falls and bitter events come to an end. (75) The seven fat and lean cows and the seven green and dry ears of grain that appeared in the dream were interpreted by Joseph (Yūsuf) as 14 years of bounty and famine. However, the fifteenth year, which was the year of rainfall and abundance, and which had not appeared in the king's dream, was knowledge of the unseen (ghayb) that Joseph presented from Allah, so that the groundwork for announcing his prophethood would be laid. "ثمّ یاتى من بعد ذلك عام..." (Thumma ya’tī min ba’di dhālika ‘ām...)

Conditions for effective management in society: 1. Public trust. "انّا لنراك من المحسنین" (Innā lanarāka min al-muḥsinīn) 2. Honesty. "یوسف ایّها الصدیق" (Yūsuf ayyuhā al-ṣiddīq) 3. Knowledge and wisdom. "علّمنى ربّى" (‘Allamanī Rabbī) 4. Correct foresight. "فذروه فى سنبله" (Fadharūhu fī sunbulih) 5. Public obedience. Because the people implemented Joseph's plan.

75) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

  1. We should inform people about future hardships and difficulties so that they are prepared to face them. "ثمّ یأتى من بعد ذلك سبع شداد" (Thumma ya’tī min ba’di dhālika sab‘un shidād)*

  2. Saving and planning for times of weakness is valuable. "سبع شداد یأكلن ما قدّمتم" (Sab‘un shidādun ya’kulna mā qaddamtum)

  3. Foresight and planning can guide a nation through the severe storms of events. "یأكلن ما قدّمتم" (Ya’kulna mā qaddamtum)*

  4. In consumption, save a portion for seed and capital. ("ممّا تحصنون" (Mimmā taḥṣunūn) means storing in a fortress or protected place.)

  5. During difficult conditions, the fundamental foundations and capital must be preserved. "ممّا تحصنون" (Mimmā taḥṣunūn)*

  6. We must learn methods of preserving and processing foodstuffs so that they are not destroyed. "ممّا تحصنون" (Mimmā taḥṣunūn)*

  7. After hardship comes ease. "ثمّ یأتى من بعد ذلك عام فیه یغاث الناس" (Thumma ya’tī min ba’di dhālika ‘āmun fīhi yughāthu al-nās)*

  8. We should give people hope for the future so that they can endure hardships. "ثمّ یأتى من بعد ذلك عام فیه یغاث" (Thumma ya’tī min ba’di dhālika ‘āmun fīhi yughāth)*

  9. Forecasting weather conditions and rainfall is useful for agricultural planning. "یأتى... عام فیه یغاث" (Ya’tī... ‘āmun fīhi yughāth)*

  10. Some dreams can recount future occurrences and serve as guiding keys for humanity. "یغاث النّاس و فیه یعصرون" (Yughāthu al-nās wa fīhi ya‘ṣirūn)

  11. We should take action to solve people's problems, even if they are not from our region or country. (Joseph's plan was not for his own region.)*

  12. Science and knowledge are the key to the progress and survival of governments, and to the security and welfare of society. (Joseph's plan and program for combating famine and drought attest to his knowledge.)*

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱئْتُونِى بِهِۦ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُ ٱلرَّسُولُ قَالَ ٱرْجِعْ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَسْـَٔلْهُ مَا بَالُ ٱلنِّسْوَةِ ٱلَّـٰتِى قَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى بِكَيْدِهِنَّ عَلِيمٌ (٥٠)
The king said, ‘Bring him to me!’ When the messenger came to him, he said, ‘Go back to your master, and ask him about the affair of women who cut their hands. My Lord is indeed well aware of their stratagems.’ (50)

Key Points

By interpreting the king's dream and presenting a well-considered plan, without any expectation or condition, Joseph proved that he was not an ordinary criminal or prisoner, but rather an extraordinary and knowledgeable man. When the king's messenger came to Joseph, he did not immediately welcome the news of freedom. Instead, he requested that his former case be reviewed, because he did not want to be included in a royal pardon; rather, he wanted his innocence and chastity to be proven, and to make the king understand the extent to which corruption and injustice had prevailed in his regime. We read in a Hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: I am astonished by Joseph's patience; when the 'Aziz (Minister) of Egypt needed the interpretation of the dream, he did not say, "I will not tell it until I am released from prison." However, when they wanted to release him, he did not come out until the accusation against him was cleared.(76) Perhaps Joseph, out of respect for the 'Aziz of Egypt, did not mention his wife by name but referred instead to the gathering of women. «قطّعن ایدیهنّ»

76) Tafsir Atib al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Minds that the country needs and who are imprisoned, if they have not committed a crime, must be released with the help of the government. «و قال الملك ائتونى به»

2- Delay is not permissible when utilizing intellectual minds (especially during crisis situations). «و قال الملك ائتونى به»*

3- Rulers are in need of thoughtful, prudent, and great individuals. «و قال الملك ائتونى به»*

4- If Allah wills, He makes the king dependent on the imprisoned slave. «قال الملك ائتونى به»*

5- Joseph عليه السلام did not consider the King of Egypt his lord. «ارجع الى ربّك»*

6- The respect of leaders and rulers, even if they are disbelievers, must be maintained before their subordinates. «ارجع الى ربّك»*

7- Freedom is not valuable at any cost. Proving innocence is more important than freedom. «ارجع الى ربّك فسئله»

8- A prisoner who, despite the offer of freedom, suggests reviewing the case, is pure. «فسئله»

9- Joseph first cleared the minds of the people, then accepted responsibility. «مابال النّسوة»

10- Defending one's honor and reputation is obligatory. «ما بال النّسوة»

11- Seeking justice from non-divine rulers is permissible. «فسئله ما بال النسوة...»*

12- In sensitive situations (such as Joseph's عليه السلام position before the king), truths should not be concealed merely to protect the honor of some individuals. «ما بال النسوة...»*

13- Joseph's reference to «قَطَّعنَ أیدیهنَّ» (they cut their hands) was perhaps because this event had a more public aspect and was undeniable. «ما بال النسوة اللاتى قَطَّعنَ أیدیهنَّ»*

14- Sins and moral issues should be mentioned indirectly and veiled. «انّ ربّى بكيدهنّ علیم»*

15- Although the enemy's plot is great, Allah is also aware of all plots and protects His friends from them. «انّ ربّى بكيدهنّ علیم»*

Ayah

قَالَ مَا خَطْبُكُنَّ إِذْ رَٰوَدتُّنَّ يُوسُفَ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۚ قُلْنَ حَـٰشَ لِلَّهِ مَا عَلِمْنَا عَلَيْهِ مِن سُوٓءٍ ۚ قَالَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ حَصْحَصَ ٱلْحَقُّ أَنَا۠ رَٰوَدتُّهُۥ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (٥۱)
The king said, ‘What was your business, women, when you solicited Joseph?’ They said, ‘Heaven be praised! We know of no evil in him.’ The prince’s wife said, ‘Now the truth has come to light! It was I who solicited him, and he is indeed telling the truth.’ (51)

Key Points

Khutb (خطب) refers to being summoned for an important matter. A Khatib (خطیب) is someone who invites people toward a great goal. Hashasa (حصحص) is derived from hissah (حصّه), meaning the separation of truth from falsehood. (77) In this incident, one of the Divine traditions was realized: that due to piety (taqwa) toward Allah, relief is created. «و مَن یتّق اللَّه یَجعل له مَخرجاً و یرزقه من حیث لایحتسب» (78) Whoever fears Allah, He will appoint for him a way out and provide for him from where He does not expect.

77) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan. 78) At-Talaq 2-3.

Messages

1- Sometimes, when a knot becomes tight, the highest official of the country must personally review the case and convene the court. «قال ما خطبكنّ»

2- Summon the accused individuals so they may defend themselves. «ما خطبكنّ» Even Zulaikha was present. «قالت امراة العزیز»

3- Sweetness follows bitterness. In contrast to «اراد باهلك سوءً» (he intended evil for your family), «ما علمنا علیه من سوء» (we know no evil of him) is stated.

4- The women of Egypt not only confessed that Yusuf was not the wrongdoer, but they also negated every kind of evil and deviation from him. (Min soo' (من سوء) means there is no fault or sin upon him).*

5- The truth does not remain hidden forever. «الآن حصحص الحقّ»

6- The clarification of some cases and truths requires time. «الآن حصحص الحقّ»*

7- Consciences will awaken one day and confess. «انا راودته» Just as the pressure of society and the environment compels the arrogant to confess. (As soon as the wife of the Aziz saw that all the women acknowledged Yusuf's chastity, she too submitted to confession.)

8- The Aziz of Egypt wanted Zulaikha's interaction with Yusuf عليه السلام to remain hidden, but Allah revealed it to all the worlds and forever, so that Yusuf's purity would be proven. «قالت... أنا راودتُه»*

9- When Allah wills, the enemy itself becomes the means of salvation and the removal of accusation. «انّه لمن الصّادقین»*

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ لِيَعْلَمَ أَنِّى لَمْ أَخُنْهُ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى كَيْدَ ٱلْخَآئِنِينَ (٥۲)
[Joseph said], [‘I initiated] this [inquiry], that he may know that I did not betray him in his absence, and that Allah does not further the schemes of the treacherous.’ (52)

Key Points

Regarding whether this Ayah is the speech of Joseph عليه السلام or a continuation of the speech of the wife of Al-Aziz, there are two views. Some commentaries (79) consider it a continuation of Joseph's speech, while others (80) consider it a continuation of the wife of Al-Aziz's speech. However, considering the content of the Ayah, the first view is correct, and it cannot be the speech of the wife of Al-Aziz. For what betrayal is greater than an innocent person remaining in prison for years? With this statement, Joseph عليه السلام presents the reason for his delay in leaving prison: the need for a re-examination of the case and the restoration of his reputation.

79) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Al-Mizan. 80) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- A noble person is not seeking revenge; he is seeking the restoration of reputation and the discovery of the truth. «ذلك لیعلم»

2- We should remove suspicions from minds. «ذلك لیعلم...»*

3- Malicious intent toward a man's wife is a betrayal of the man. «لم اَخنه»

4- Betrayal is a despicable act, even if committed against a disbeliever (Kafir). «لم اخنه»*

5- The sign of true faith is not committing betrayal in secrecy and concealment. «لم اخنه بالغیب»

6- The betrayer plots his deed by justifying his transgression. «كید الخائنین»

7- Joseph was trying to make the king realize that the Will of Allah and the Divine tradition (Sunnah) play a decisive role in events and occurrences. «انّ اللَّه لایهدى...»

8- The betrayer does not achieve success and does not have a good outcome. Yes! If we are pure; «لم اخنه بالغیب» Allah does not allow the impure to destroy our reputation. «انّ اللَّه لایهدى كید الخائنین»

9- Among the Divine traditions (Sunan Ilahi) is the failure of betrayers and their defeat and disgrace. «انّ اللّه لا یهدى كید الخائنین»*

Ayah

۞ وَمَآ أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلنَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌۢ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ إِلَّا مَا رَحِمَ رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٥۳)
‘Yet I do not absolve my [own carnal] soul, for the [carnal] soul indeed prompts [men] to evil, except inasmuch as my Lord has mercy. Indeed my Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (53)

Key Points

The Qur'an describes states for the Nafs (soul/self), including: 1. Nafs Ammārah (the commanding soul), which drives man toward evil deeds, and if not restrained by intellect and faith, man suddenly falls. 2. Nafs Lawwāmah (the self-reproaching soul), a state where the transgressing person blames and censures himself and proceeds to repent and apologize. It is mentioned in Sūrat al-Qiyāmah (Chapter of the Resurrection). 3. Nafs Muṭma'innah (the tranquil soul), a state possessed only by the Prophets, the Awliyā' (saints), and those truly trained by them. They emerge victoriously from every temptation and incident, devoted to Allah. Yūsuf (Joseph) عليه السلام attributes his lack of betrayal and his success in this trial to the grace and mercy of Allah, and as a human being possessing human nature, he does not absolve himself. In numerous narrations, the dangers of the Nafs have been discussed, and being satisfied with one's self is considered a sign of corrupted intellect and the greatest trap of Satan. (81) Imam Sajjad عليه السلام, in the supplication Munājāt ash-Shākkīn (The Whispered Prayer of the Complainers), enumerated fifteen dangers of the Nafs, paying attention to which is beneficial.

81) Ghurar al-Ḥikam.

Messages

1- Never praise ourselves for purity or absolve ourselves. «ما اُبرّء نفسى»

2- The condition of perfection is that even if all people consider one perfect, one should not consider oneself perfect. In the story of Prophet Yūsuf, his brothers, the wife of the Aziz of Egypt, the witness, the king, Satan, and the prisoners all testified to his perfection, yet he Himself says: «ما اُبَرّء نفسى»

3- The danger of carnal desire (hawā an-nafs) is serious; do not regard it lightly. «انّ النفس لامّارة بالسوء» Four types of emphasis are used in the Ayah: (Inna, the Lām of emphasis, the intensive form (ṣīghah mubālaghah), and the nominal sentence).

4- Although the Prophets are infallible (ma‘ṣūm), they possess human instincts. «انّ النفس لامّارة بالسوء»

5- One must both defend one's purity and chastity against accusations and confess to the evils of the Nafs and seek refuge in Allah. «انّ النّفس لامّارة بالسوء»

6- The Nafs repeats its desires until it traps man. «لامّارة»

7- The human Nafs, naturally and instinctively, has a negative inclination unless it remains within the orbit of Divine grace. «لامّارة بالسّوء»

8- Only His mercy is the source of salvation. If man is left to his own devices, he falls. «الاّ ما رحم»

9- Yūsuf is under the special training of Allah. The word «ربّى» (My Lord) is repeated.

10- The Trainer (Rabb) must possess mercy and forgiveness. «انّ ربّى غفور رحیم»

11- Forgiveness is the prerequisite for receiving Divine mercy. First, He says: «غفور» (Forgiving), then He says: «رحیم» (Merciful).

12- Despite all the dangers, we should not despair of His mercy. «غفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱئْتُونِى بِهِۦٓ أَسْتَخْلِصْهُ لِنَفْسِى ۖ فَلَمَّا كَلَّمَهُۥ قَالَ إِنَّكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ لَدَيْنَا مَكِينٌ أَمِينٌ (٥٤)
The king said, ‘Bring him to me, I will make him my favourite.’ Then, when he had spoken with him, he said, ‘Indeed today [onwards] you will be honoured and trustworthy with us.’ (54)

Key Points

It is stated in Lisan al-Arab: Whenever a person makes someone his confidant and involves him in his affairs, it is said: "استخلصه" (He chose him exclusively). When Joseph (Yusuf) عليه السلام was released from prison, he wrote several phrases on the prison door describing the nature of the prison: «هذا قبور الاحیاء، بیت الاحزان، تجربة الاصدقاء و شماتة الاعداء» (This is the graveyard of the living, the house of sorrows, the testing ground for friends, and the source of enemies' gloating). That is; the prison is the graveyard of the living, the house of sorrows, the place for testing friends, and the gloating of enemies. (82) When the king realized Joseph's honesty and trustworthiness and found no treachery in him, he chose him exclusively for himself. If Allah finds no treachery in a servant, what will He do!? Surely, He will choose him exclusively for Himself, as the Quran uses such expressions regarding the Prophets: «و أنا اخترتك فاستمع لما یوحى » (And I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed) (83), «واصطنعتك لنفسى» (And I have chosen you for Myself) (84). The king announced with "لدینا" (with us) that Joseph holds a position in our government, not just in my heart, so all officials must obey him. Having power (being makin) and being trustworthy (being amin) together is valuable, because if one is trustworthy but lacks resources, he does not have the power to accomplish anything, and if he is makin but not amin, he will waste public funds (Bayt al-Mal). Just as Hadrat Ali عليه السلام addressed Malik al-Ashtar, his governor, saying: After identifying and selecting employees, give them sufficient wages. «اَسبغ علیهم الارزاق» (Provide them with abundant provisions/salaries). (85)

82) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 83) Taha, 13. 84) Taha, 41. 85) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 53.

Messages

  1. If Allah wills, He makes the prisoner of yesterday the ruler of today. «قال الملك ائتونى به استخلصه لنفسى»
  2. One must react to the services rendered by individuals and appreciate them. (In response to the interpretation of the dream, he ordered him to be released.) «ائتونى به استخلصه لنفسى»*
  3. Polytheists and disbelievers also enjoy spiritual perfections. The innate desire for perfection exists in every human being. «استخلصه لنفسى»
  4. For holding and granting important and key positions, in addition to management and competence, popularity and acceptance are also very important. (Joseph gained special popularity due to his chastity, management, dream interpretation, and nobility, hence the Aziz of Egypt said he should be exclusively for me.) «استخلصه لنفسى»*
  5. Purity, trustworthiness, honesty, and chastity compel even kings to show humility. «استخلصه لنفسى»*
  6. Until a man speaks, his faults and virtues remain hidden. «فلمّا كلّمه قال»
  7. In selections (for positions), a face-to-face interview is also beneficial. «فلمّا كلّمه»
  8. For granting very important responsibilities, it is sometimes necessary for the head of the state himself to conduct the face-to-face interview. «فلمّا كلّمه»*
  9. Entrust major and key positions to deserving and tested individuals. «فلمّا كلّمه قال انّك الیوم...»*
  10. An honest and trustworthy person is accepted and respected even among disbelievers. ("لدینا" (with us) includes all officials.) «انّك الیوم لدینا مكین امین»*
  11. The women of Egypt saw Joseph's beauty, lost their hearts, and cut their hands; the king saw his trustworthiness and purity and placed the dominion of Egypt at his disposal. What would they have done if all of Joseph's perfections had been revealed to them?*
  12. When you become certain of someone's trustworthiness, competence, and honesty, do not hesitate to assign responsibility to him. «انّك الیوم لدینا مكین امین»*
  13. The special advisor to state officials must possess piety, management skills, planning ability, and trustworthiness. «استخلصه لنفسى... مكین امین» (Joseph was the embodiment of all these.)
  14. The power granted to Joseph عليه السلام was unconditional. ("مكینٌ" (established) without any condition or limitation.)*
  15. Being makin (established/powerful) and amin (trustworthy) are both necessary together. «مكین امین»

Ayah

قَالَ ٱجْعَلْنِى عَلَىٰ خَزَآئِنِ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ إِنِّى حَفِيظٌ عَلِيمٌ (٥٥)
He said, ‘Put me in charge of the country’s granaries. I am indeed fastidious [and]well-informed.’ (55)

Key Points

| Question: Why did Joseph propose granting responsibility to himself? Or, in other words, why did Joseph seek leadership? Answer: He sensed danger and harm for the people based on the dream of the King of Egypt, and He knew Himself to be qualified to prevent unfortunate economic events. Therefore, to prevent harm, He announced His readiness to accept such a responsibility. Question: Why did Joseph praise and commend himself, when the Quran states: «فلا تزكّوا انفسكم»(86) (Do not sanctify yourselves)? Answer: Joseph's commendation was a statement of His own capabilities and abilities necessary for carrying out the responsibility, which could prevent the negative effects of famine and drought, and was not for the sake of boasting or misuse. Question: Why did Joseph cooperate with a disbelieving government? Has the Quran not forbidden this? «و لا تركنوا الى الّذین ظلموا...»(87) Answer: Joseph did not accept this responsibility to support the oppressor, but rather undertook this task with the aim of saving the people from the pressures of the famine period. Joseph never even uttered a single word of flattery. Political figures usually abandon the people and flee when danger arises, but Joseph had to protect the people. Furthermore, if an oppressive regime cannot be overthrown or changed, one must prevent deviation and oppression as much as possible, take control of some affairs, and operate within them.(88) It is stated in Tafsir Namuneh: Observing the "rule of the more important and the important" (al-Ahamm wal-Muhimm) is a principle according to reason and Sharia. Participation in a polytheistic governmental system is not permissible, but saving a nation from famine is more important. For this reason, Joseph عليه السلام did not accept political responsibility, lest it aid the oppressor; He did not accept military responsibility, lest innocent blood be shed. He only took on economic responsibility, and that was for the sake of saving the people.(89) Imam Reza عليه السلام said: When necessity dictated that Joseph accept the supervision of the treasuries of Egypt, He himself made the proposal.(90) Ali ibn Yaqtin was also a minister in the Abbasid regime upon the recommendation of Imam Kadhim عليه السلام. The presence of such men of God can be a refuge for the oppressed. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «كفّارة عمل السلطان، قضاء حوائج الاخوان» (The expiation for working for the government is fulfilling the needs of the brethren in faith).(91) Imam Reza عليه السلام was asked: Why have you accepted the succession (Crown Princedom) under Ma'mun? In response, He said: Joseph, who was a Prophet, entered the system of a polytheist; I, who am the successor of the Prophet, have entered the system of a person who claims to be Muslim. Furthermore, my acceptance was compulsory, whereas Joseph عليه السلام accepted that responsibility voluntarily and due to the importance of the matter.(92) When Joseph attained status and position, He did not request to see His parents, but rather requested the responsibility of the treasury, because the visit was an emotional matter, while saving the people from famine was His social mission. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام addressed a group who claimed asceticism and invited people to make life difficult for themselves like they did... He said: What do you think about Joseph the Prophet, who told the King of Egypt: «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض» (Appoint me over the treasuries of the land), and Joseph's work reached the point where He took control of the entire country and its surroundings up to Yemen... Yet, we do not find anyone who criticized Him for this action.(93) In a narration from Imam Reza عليه السلام, it is stated: Joseph عليه السلام collected and stored the wheat during the first seven years, and during the second seven years, when the famine began, He gradually and carefully provided it to the people for their daily living expenses. With precision and trustworthiness, He saved the country of Egypt from misery. During the seven years of famine, Joseph never lived with a full stomach, lest He forget the hungry.(94) In Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Al-Mizan, Joseph's عليه السلام performance is recounted as follows: When the famine began; in the first year, Prophet Joseph traded wheat for gold and silver; in the second year, wheat in exchange for jewels and ornaments; in the third year, wheat for livestock; in the fourth year, wheat in exchange for slaves; in the fifth year, wheat for houses; in the sixth year, wheat for farmlands; and in the seventh year, He traded wheat in exchange for the people themselves becoming slaves. When the seventh year concluded, He told the King of Egypt: All the people and their assets are under my control, but I call God to witness, and you be a witness too, that I free all the people and return all their possessions, and I also return your palace, throne, and signet ring (seal and ring). Governance was a means for me to save the people, nothing else. You must treat them justly. Upon hearing these words, the King found himself so small and insignificant before Joseph's spiritual greatness that he suddenly opened his mouth in declaration, saying: «أشهد أن لااله الااللَّه و أنّك رسوله» (I bear witness that there is no god but Allah and that you are His Messenger). I, too, have believed, but you must be the ruler. «انّك الیوم لدینا مكین أمین» In selecting and choosing individuals, we must pay attention to the Quranic criteria. In addition to being «حفیظ» (guardian) and «علیم» (knowledgeable), other criteria are mentioned in the Quran, including: 1. Faith. «افمن كان مؤمناً كمن كان فاسقاً لایستوون»(95) 2. Precedence/History. «والسابقون السابقون . اولئك المقربون»(96) 3. Migration (Hijra). «والذین آمنوا و لم یهاجروا ما لكم من ولایتهم من شى ء»(97) 4. Physical and scientific ability. «و زاده بسطة فى العلم و الجسم»(98) 5. Family integrity. «ماكان ابوك امرء سوء»(99) 6. Jihad and struggle. «فضل اللَّه المجاهدین على القاعدین اجراً عظیما»(100)

Messages

  1. Wherever necessary, one must volunteer for sensitive responsibilities. «قال اجعلنى...»

  2. Prophethood is not separate from governance and politics, just as religion is not separate from politics. «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض»

  3. When employing qualified individuals, their personal interest (in the role) should be considered. «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض...»*

  4. First, establish competence, then request responsibility. «استخلصه... اجعلنى...»*

  5. When you are skilled in a task, have self-confidence and offer yourself. «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض»*

  6. In whatever position an individual holds, they must pay attention to the views of subordinates and trustworthy advisors. (The Aziz of Egypt paid attention to Joseph's suggestion, who said: «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض», and accepted it.)*

  7. Regional citizenship is not the principle. Joseph was not Egyptian, yet He entered the government of Egypt and took responsibility. (Nationalism is forbidden) «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض»

  8. In times of necessity, stating one's own competence and qualification does not contradict reliance on God (Tawakkul), asceticism (Zuhd), or sincerity (Ikhlas). «انّى حفیظ علیم»

  9. From the combination of the two descriptions the King stated about Joseph عليه السلام—«مكین» (established/powerful) and «امین» (trustworthy)—and the two attributes Joseph stated about Himself—«حفیظ» (guardian) and «علیم» (knowledgeable)—the characteristics of worthy executives are derived: power, trustworthiness, guardianship, and specialization.

  10. In planning and supervising consumption, the share of the future generation must be preserved and observed. «حفیظ علیم»

Ayahs

وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَكَّنَّا لِيُوسُفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَتَبَوَّأُ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ يَشَآءُ ۚ نُصِيبُ بِرَحْمَتِنَا مَن نَّشَآءُ ۖ وَلَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٥٦)
That is how We established Joseph in the land that he may settle in it wherever he wished. We confer Our mercy on whomever We wish, and We do not waste the reward of the virtuous. (56)
وَلَأَجْرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ (٥۷)
And the reward of the Hereafter is surely better for those who have faith and are Godwary. (57)

Key Points

In these two verses, Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) is praised as muhsin (good-doer), mu'min (believer), and muttaqi (pious/God-fearing). Throughout this Surah, the Will of Allah can be compared with the will and desire of the people. Yusuf's brothers willed to humiliate him by throwing him into the well and making him a slave, but the Aziz (Minister) of Egypt said concerning him: «اكرمى مثواه» (Treat him honorably). The wife of the Aziz intended to defile his chastity, but Allah kept him pure. Some wished to break his resistance and humiliate him by imprisoning Yusuf: «ليسجنن و ليكونن من الصّاغرين» (He will surely be imprisoned and will surely be among the humiliated). But in contrast, Allah willed to honor him and grant him the governance of Egypt: «مكّنا ليوسف...» (We established Yusuf...). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Yusuf was a free and noble man upon whom the envy of his brothers, captivity in the well, the lust of women, prison, slander, leadership, and power had no effect.(101) The rewards of the Hereafter are better than the rewards of this world, because the rewards of the Hereafter: A: Have no limits. «لهم ما يشاءون» (102) B: Are not perishable. «خالدين فيها» (103) C: Are not confined to one place. «نتبوّأ من الجنّة حيث نشاء» (104) D: Are beyond our calculation. «أجرهم بغير حساب» (105) E: Have no side effects, afflictions, or illnesses. «لا يصدّعون عنها» (106)

101) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 102) Zumar, 34. 103) Furqan, 76. 104) Zumar, 74. 105) Zumar, 10. 106) Waqi'ah, 19.

Messages

1- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to grant honor to chaste and pious individuals. «كذلك»

2- Although outwardly the King of Egypt told Yusuf: «إنّك اليوم لدينا مكين» (Indeed, you are today with us established), in reality, Allah granted Yusuf authority. «مكّنا»

3- The right of disposal (or administration) is among the prerogatives of governance. «مكّنا ليوسف فى الأرض يتبوّء...»*

4- Governments, during national crises, can restrict the freedom of people in disposing of their wealth and properties and direct them toward the public interest. «يتبوّء منها حيث يشاء»

5- The scope of Yusuf's authority was extensive. «حيث يشاء»

6- Power, if held by those worthy of it, is a mercy; otherwise, it will be harmful. «نصيب برحمتنا»

7- In the divine worldview, no deed remains without reward. «لا نضيع»

8- The wasting of people's rights stems either from ignorance, stinginess, inability, or other factors, none of which apply to Allah. «لا نضيع»

9- Attaining governance and power in this world does not contradict good-doing (ihsan), faith, and piety. «مكّنا ليوسف... أجر المحسنين»*

10- The Divine Will is orderly and governed by law. «نصيب برحمتنا... ولا نضيع أجر المحسنين»

11- Although everything depends on the Divine Will, Allah is Wise and does not grant power to anyone without reason. «و لا نضيع أجر المحسنين»

12- Good-doers, besides benefiting from rewards in this worldly life, will also enjoy superior rewards in the Hereafter. «لا نضيع أجر المحسنين و لأجر الآخرة خير»

13- If a good-doer does not achieve a reward or status in this world, he should not worry, as it will be compensated elsewhere. «لا نضيع... و لأجر الآخرة خير»*

14- Kingship and governance are also insignificant compared to the reward of the Hereafter. «لأجر الآخرة خير...»*

15- Material resources and apparent governance are not pleasurable for the men of God; what is desirable and beloved to them is the Hereafter. «و لأجر الآخرة خير»

16- If you pursue piety, We will also bestow Our Mercy upon you. «نصيب برحمتنا... للّذين... و كانوا يتّقون»

17- Faith accompanied by piety is the solution; otherwise, the fate of the sinful believer is uncertain. «آمنوا و كانوا يتّقون»

18- Piety that has become an enduring characteristic is valuable. «كانوا يتّقون»

19- Faith and adherence to piety are conditions for benefiting from the rewards of the Hereafter. «لأجر الآخرة خير للّذين آمنوا و كانوا يتّقون»

20- Piety (taqwa) is a higher stage and rank within faith. «آمنوا و كانوا يتّقون»*

Ayah

وَجَآءَ إِخْوَةُ يُوسُفَ فَدَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ (٥۸)
[After some years] the brothers of Joseph came and entered his presence. He recognized them, but they did not recognize him. (58)

Key Points

According to Joseph's prediction and prophecy, the people enjoyed seven years of abundance and rain, but after that, the second seven-year period arrived, and the people suffered famine and drought. The scope of the famine extended from Egypt to Palestine and Canaan. Jacob told his sons to travel towards Egypt to procure wheat. They entered Egypt and presented their request. Joseph saw his brothers among the applicants for grain, but the brothers did not recognize Joseph, and this was natural, because approximately twenty to thirty years had passed between the time Joseph was thrown into the well and his rule in the land of Egypt. When Joseph was taken out of the well, he was a youth, «يا بشرى هذا غلام» (O good news! This is a boy). He served as a servant in the house of the Aziz for several years, and he also spent years in prison. Seven years (the period of abundance and plentiful water) had also passed since his release from prison, and now that it was the years of famine, the brothers had come to Egypt.

Messages

1- During a famine, rationing is necessary, and everyone must apply in person to receive their quota so that others do not misuse their name. «اخوة» (Brothers) – even though they could have sent one representative, all the brothers came.

2- During a famine, if other regions ask you for help, provide assistance. «و جاء اخوة یوسف» (And Joseph's brothers came).

3- Do not remain passive or stagnant in times of need and poverty; even seek help from other countries. «و جاء اخوة یوسف»*

4- Sometimes the oppressor becomes dependent on the oppressed. «و جاء اخوة یوسف» (A mountain does not reach a mountain, but a person reaches a person.)*

5- Meeting Joseph, even for non-Egyptians, was easy and simple. «جاء اخوة... فدخلوا» (The heads of governments must adopt a policy that allows people to meet them easily.)

6- Childhood memories are imprinted on the mind and do not fade away. «فعرفهم و هم له منكرون» (The brothers had been associated with Joseph when they were older; but Joseph had seen them in his childhood, thus Joseph recognized them, but they did not recognize Joseph).

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُم بِجَهَازِهِمْ قَالَ ٱئْتُونِى بِأَخٍ لَّكُم مِّنْ أَبِيكُمْ ۚ أَلَا تَرَوْنَ أَنِّىٓ أُوفِى ٱلْكَيْلَ وَأَنَا۠ خَيْرُ ٱلْمُنزِلِينَ (٥۹)
When he had furnished them with their provision, he said, ‘Bring me a brother that you have through your father. Do you not see that I give the full measure and that I am the best of hosts? (59)

Key Points

Joseph said: «ائتونى باخٍ لكم» (Bring me a brother of yours), meaning a brother whom you share from your father, and He did not say: my brother. From this statement, it becomes clear that Joseph, in His disguised identity, opened the door for conversation with His brothers, and they reported, as mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir), that they were the sons of Jacob, the grandson of Abraham. Our father is an old man who has been weeping and secluded for years due to the grief and sorrow over his son, whom the wolf tore apart. We have also assigned one of our brothers to serve him. If possible, please give us the share of that father and brother as well, so that we may return happily. Joseph ordered that, in addition to the load for ten camels, two more loads—the shares of Jacob and the other brother—should be added. Joseph, in order to attract His brothers, said: «أنَا خیر المنزلین» (I am the best of hosts). And those brothers were attracted. But Allah uses many expressions to attract people: «خیر الرّازقین»(107) He is the best of providers. «خیر الغافرین»(108) He is the best of forgivers. «خیر الفاتحین»(109) He is the best of openers (or bestower of victory). «خیر الماكرین»(110) He is the best of planners (or schemers). «خیر الوارثین»(111) He is the best of inheritors. «خیر الحاكمین»(112) He is the best of judges. But a great many people are not attracted.

107) Al-Jumu'ah, 11. 108) Al-A'raf, 155. 109) Al-A'raf, 89. 110) Al-Anfal, 30. 111) Al-Anbiya, 89. 112) Yunus, 109.

Messages

1- Joseph had direct supervision over the distribution of the stored provisions of Egypt. «جهّزهم»

2- The sound management of Joseph عليه السلام ensured that during the time of famine, not only did the people of Egypt face no problems, but He was also able to help the surrounding regions. «جهّزهم بجهازهم»*

3- Both discretion (keeping secrets) and truthfulness are necessary. Joseph said: «اخ لكم» (a brother of yours) and did not say: my brother, so that truthfulness and discretion were both observed.

4- Individuals, institutions, or countries that provide economic aid can propose certain conditions for the purpose of development or other interests. «ائتونى باخ...»

5- Even during times of crisis and famine, injustice and short-changing (giving less than due) are forbidden. «اوفى الكیل»

6- In transactions, the quantity of the commodity must be specified. «الكیل»

7- We should respond to evil with good. (Joseph gave the brothers their full share and did not speak about the injustice they had committed against Him). «و جاء اخوة یوسف... اوفى الكیل»*

8- The short-changing or the justice of agents, workers, and assistants is credited to the main official and supervisor. «انّى اوفى الكیل»

9- The buying, selling, and distribution of grains in Egypt during the time of Joseph عليه السلام was done by measure (using a standard volume). «اوفى الكیل»*

10- Officials responsible for distributing public resources must be just. «اوفى الكیل»*

11- We should not use power as a means of revenge. «اوفى الكیل و انا خیر المنزلین»*

12- Hospitality is among the morals of the prophets. «خیر المنزلین»

13- Respect travelers and caravans entering your region, even during times of need and famine. «خیر المنزلین»

Ayah

فَإِن لَّمْ تَأْتُونِى بِهِۦ فَلَا كَيْلَ لَكُمْ عِندِى وَلَا تَقْرَبُونِ (٦٠)
But if you do not bring him to me, then there will be no rations for you with me, and don’t [ever] come near me.’ (60)

Messages

1- In management, both affection and threat are necessary. First, good tidings and kindness: «أنَا خیر المنزلین» (I am the best of hosts), then threat and ultimatum: «فان لم تاتونى» (But if you do not bring him to me).

2- We must eliminate opportunities for misuse. (If it becomes customary for those present to take the share of those absent, some will take advantage.) «فان لم تأتونى فلا كیل لكم»* (But if you do not bring him to me, then there shall be no measure for you.)

3- In the implementation of the law, there should be no discrimination between a brother, family, and others. «فلاكیل لكم» (then there shall be no measure for you.) (Every person had a specific share and had to receive it himself.)

4- When issuing a threat, it is not necessary for the manager to have a one hundred percent decision to execute it. «فلا كیل لكم» (then there shall be no measure for you.) (Because Joseph was not the kind of person who would allow his brothers to die of famine.)

5- Decisiveness in implementing programs is a condition of leadership. «فلاكیل لكم عندى و لاتقربون» (then there shall be no measure for you with me, nor shall you come near me.)

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ سَنُرَٰوِدُ عَنْهُ أَبَاهُ وَإِنَّا لَفَـٰعِلُونَ (٦۱)
They said, ‘We will solicit him from his father. [That] we will surely do.’ (61)

Key Points

Al-Murāwada (مراودة) refers to repeated recourse (to someone), accompanied by entreaty or deceit and trickery. The scent of envy is detected from the brothers' speech; instead of [saying] abānā (our father), they said abāh (his father). And at the beginning of the Surah, the brothers' conversation was this: «لیوسف و اخوه احبّ الى ابینا منّا» (Surely Joseph and his brother are dearer to our father than we are). The father belongs to us, but he loves Joseph and his brother more than us.

Messages

1- A son's journey must be with the permission and consent of his father. «سنراود عنه اباه»*

2- Joseph's brothers were aware of their father's negative reaction to the request to take Benjamin with them. (Sanurāwid (سنراود) means continuous conversation accompanied by planning and strategy).*

3- After the incident involving Joseph, Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) would not keep Benjamin far from himself, and separating him from his father was not an easy task. «سنراود عنه اباه»*

Ayah

وَقَالَ لِفِتْيَـٰنِهِ ٱجْعَلُوا۟ بِضَـٰعَتَهُمْ فِى رِحَالِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَعْرِفُونَهَآ إِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (٦۲)
He said to his servants, ‘Put their money [back] into their saddlebags. Maybe they will recognize it when they return to their folks, and maybe they will come back [again].’ (62)

Key Points

Prophet Yusuf (Joseph), who was mentioned in previous verses with titles such as; Sadiq (Truthful), Muhsin (Doer of Good), Mukhliṣ (Sincere), certainly would not give the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) to his father and brothers. It is possible that he gave the money for the grain from his personal share and property. He returned the money so that lack of funds would not prevent their second journey. «لعلّهم یرجعون» (Perhaps they will return). Furthermore, returning the money was a sign of sincerity and the absence of ill-intent behind his insistence on bringing the brother (Benjamin). Placing it secretly among the goods was a sign of giving without obligation (without minnah) and protecting it from theft. Yusuf, who was a slave and servant yesterday, today has servants and attendants. «لفتیانه» (His young men/servants). However, during the meeting with his brothers, he neither sought revenge nor complained, nor did he harbor resentment. Rather, by returning their capital, he showed them attention, indicating, "I love you."

Messages

1- A competent manager and leader must have innovative plans. «اجعلوا»

2- Compensate for bad deeds with good deeds. «اجعلوا بضاعتهم»

3- Maintaining kinship ties (Ṣilat al-Raḥim) means helping, not trading. «اجعلوا بضاعتهم»

4- Taking money from an elderly father and brothers during their time of need is incompatible with self-respect (karāmat al-nafs). «اِجعلوا بضاعتهم فى رحالهم»
