5- Yusuf entrusted the task of placing the brothers' merchandise to several people so as to block any grounds for accusation and prevent a servant from misusing the opportunity, taking the amount for himself and claiming, "I put it in the travelers' saddlebags." «اجعلوا بضاعتهم فى رحالهم»*

6- Provide help to others secretly, as far as possible. «فى رحالهم»*

7- Neither revenge nor resentment, but rather giving a gift for future connection. «اجعلوا بضاعتهم فى رحالهم... لعلّهم یرجعون»

8- To reclaim people, one must forgo money. «اجعلوا بضاعتهم... لعلّهم یرجعون»

9- Doing good is the best means of attracting people. «اجعلوا بضاعتهم... یرجعون»*

10- Officials must fully brief their agents regarding plans and projects in order to be successful managers. (Yusuf made everyone understand that the goal of this action was the return of this caravan). «لعلّهم یرجعون»*

11- In plans and programs, one hundred percent certainty of implementation is not necessary. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَجَعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَبِيهِمْ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا مُنِعَ مِنَّا ٱلْكَيْلُ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنَآ أَخَانَا نَكْتَلْ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَـٰفِظُونَ (٦۳)
So when they returned to their father, they said, ‘Father, the measure has been withheld from us, so let our brother go with us so that we may obtain the measure, and we will indeed take [good] care of him.’ (63)

Messages

1- Jacob had management and authority over his family and children. «یا أبانا مُنع...»

2- The father possesses the right to command and forbid his child. «فارسل»

3- Benjamin would not undertake a journey without his father's permission. «فارسل معنا»

4- To obtain something or to gain someone's trust, use emotions/affections. «اخانا»

5- Because the guilty person harbors anxiety within, his speech contains successive emphases. «انّا له لحافظون» («انّا», the letter «لام» (lām), and the nominal sentence are signs of emphasis.)

Ayah

قَالَ هَلْ ءَامَنُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا كَمَآ أَمِنتُكُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَخِيهِ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ فَٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌ حَـٰفِظًا ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (٦٤)
He said, ‘Should I not trust you with him just as I trusted you with his brother before? Yet Allah is the best of protectors, and He is the most merciful of merciful ones.’ (64)

Key Points

| Question: Considering the bad history the sons of Ya‘qūb (Jacob) had, why did their father entrust his other son to them again? Answer: Fakhr al-Rāzī (113) has presented several possibilities, each of which could be a justification for this agreement: Firstly: The brothers had not achieved the result they intended from their initial action (popularity/favor with their father). Secondly: The brothers' jealousy towards this brother was less than their jealousy towards Yūsuf (Joseph). Thirdly: Perhaps the famine and drought had created special circumstances that necessitated the renewed journey. Fourthly: Decades had passed since the first incident, and it was considered forgotten. Fifthly: Allah the Almighty had comforted him regarding the protection of his son. In Ayah 12, Ya‘qūb trusted his sons to be the protectors of Yūsuf, and he suffered the separation from Yūsuf and blindness. But regarding Benjamin, he relied on Allah and said: «فاللّه خیر حافظاً» (Allah is the best Guardian). He became capable (or strong), regained his sight, and the separation and parting ended.

113) Tafsir Kabir.

Messages

1- Quick trust in someone who has a history of transgression is not permissible. «هل آمنكم»

2- Remembering bitter past experiences safeguards a person against future incidents. «هل آمنكم على اخیه من قبل»

3- We should not withdraw ourselves after a single failure or bitter experience. «هل آمنكم علیه... فاللَّه خیر حافظاً» Ya‘qūb once again handed over the second son to the brothers, relying on Allah.

4- Yūsuf’s brothers considered themselves guardians, «انّا له لحافظون» but Prophet Ya‘qūb reminded them that Allah is the Guardian. «فاللّه خیر حافظاً...»*

5- We should not rely on apparent and material factors, no matter how numerous they are; we should rely solely on Allah. «فاللّه خیر حافظاً»*

6- Considering the unparalleled Divine Mercy and relying on Allah, we should face the incidents of life. «فاللّه خیر حافظاً وهو ارحم الرّاحمین»

7- The source of protection is Mercy. «فاللّه خیر حافظاً و هو ارحم الرّاحمین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا فَتَحُوا۟ مَتَـٰعَهُمْ وَجَدُوا۟ بِضَـٰعَتَهُمْ رُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا مَا نَبْغِى ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ بِضَـٰعَتُنَا رُدَّتْ إِلَيْنَا ۖ وَنَمِيرُ أَهْلَنَا وَنَحْفَظُ أَخَانَا وَنَزْدَادُ كَيْلَ بَعِيرٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ كَيْلٌ يَسِيرٌ (٦٥)
And when they opened their baggage, they found their money restored to them. They said, ‘Father, what [more] do we want?! This is our money, restored to us! We will get provisions for our family and take care of our brother, and add another camel-load of rations. These are meagre rations.’ (65)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is this: The amount we have taken is insufficient; if we go another time, we will take a larger load. "نمیر" (namīr) comes from "میر" (mīr), meaning foodstuffs, and "نمیر اهلنا" (namīru ahlanā) means we provide food for our family. It is understood from the phrase "نزداد كیل بعیرٍ" (nazdādu kayla ba'īrin) that the share for each person was one camel-load, which required the person to be present to receive it.

Messages

1- The sons of Ya'qub lived alongside their father and around him, striving to prepare provisions for the family. "فتحوا متاعهم... یا ابانا..."

2- The art of Yusuf (Joseph) is not merely being human, but building humanity. "وجدت بضاعتهم رُدّت الیهم" (He secretly gives a gift to his jealous and cruel brothers to prepare the ground for their return. The Quran states: "ادفع بالّتى هى احسن" Repel evil with that which is better. (114))

3- If the money or price of the goods is not taken initially, the buyer is humiliated. If the intention is a gift, the money should first be taken, but then returned wisely. "رُدّت الیهم"

4- If you wish to attract runaway pigeons, you must scatter some grain. (Yusuf returned the price of the grain to them to increase the incentive for their return.) "بضاعتهم رُدّت"

5- The man is responsible for feeding the family. "نمیر اهلنا"

6- Rationing foodstuffs during times of scarcity is a Yusufi (Joseph-like) action. "نزداد كیل بعیر" (114) Fussilat, 34.

Ayah

قَالَ لَنْ أُرْسِلَهُۥ مَعَكُمْ حَتَّىٰ تُؤْتُونِ مَوْثِقًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ لَتَأْتُنَّنِى بِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن يُحَاطَ بِكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّآ ءَاتَوْهُ مَوْثِقَهُمْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ (٦٦)
He said, ‘I will not let him go with you until you give me a [solemn] pledge by Allah that you will surely bring him back to me, unless you are made to perish.’ When they had given him their [solemn] pledge, he said, ‘Allah is witness over what we say.’ (66)

Key Points

The term Mūthaq (موثق) means that which provides trust and assurance in the word of the other party, which can be a covenant, an oath, or a vow. «موثقاً من اللَّه»

Messages

1- Do not suffice with mere kinship; strengthen the contracts. «لن ارسله معكم حتّى...»*

2- Faith in Allah, and an oath, vow, or covenant with Him, is and remains the strongest guarantee. «موثقاً من اللَّه»

3- Whenever you witness a breach of promise or misconduct from a person, strengthen the contract the next time. «موثقاً»

4- Do not easily send your child with others. «تؤتون موثقاً من اللّه»

5- In contracts, anticipate unforeseen events and those outside the scope of control. «الاّ ان یحاط بكم» (Obligation beyond capacity is forbidden.)

6- Legal and contractual safeguards should not make you neglect reliance upon Allah. «اللَّه على ما نقول وكیل»

Ayah

وَقَالَ يَـٰبَنِىَّ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بَابٍ وَٰحِدٍ وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ مِنْ أَبْوَٰبٍ مُّتَفَرِّقَةٍ ۖ وَمَآ أُغْنِى عَنكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنِ ٱلْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ ۖ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ۖ وَعَلَيْهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ (٦۷)
And he said, ‘My sons, do not enter by one gate, but enter by separate gates, though I cannot avail you anything against Allah. Sovereignty belongs only to Allah. In Him I have put my trust; and in Him let all the trusting put their trust.’ (67)

Key Points

Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) عليه السلام stated four points concerning Tawhid (monotheism) when his sons were traveling to Egypt: First: "ما اغنى عنكم" (I cannot avail you anything against Allah), meaning that even with this instruction to enter through separate gates, I cannot ward off anything decreed by Allah from you. Second: In the previous Ayah, He said: "الا ان یحاط بكم" (unless you are encompassed), meaning that all of you might be afflicted by an unforeseen incident. Third: In Ayah 64, He said: "فاللّه خیر حافظاً" (Allah is the best Guardian). Fourth: In Ayah 67, He said: "علیه توكّلت" (Upon Him I rely). Yes, the Messenger of God is entirely focused on Allah and Tawhid, but Ya'qub's sons, during their journey, merely said: "و انّا له لحافظون" (and indeed, we will be His guardians), meaning we ourselves will protect our brother. In these conversations, the portrait of the sincere monotheist is beautifully contrasted with that of ordinary people.*

Messages

1- Paternal affection does not vanish, even towards erring children. "یا بنّى" (O my sons).

2- Foresight and planning are necessary to maintain the safety of children. "یا بنىّ لا تدخلوا..." (O my sons, do not enter...).

3- When the advisor is knowledgeable and aware, the obedient party does not need to know the philosophy behind the command. (Ya'qub's sons obeyed the command "لا تدخلوا..." and did not ask for its philosophy or secret).*

4- One of the best times for admonition is on the eve of a journey. Ya'qub told his sons on the eve of their journey: "یا بنىّ لاتدخلوا من باب واحد" (O my sons, do not enter by one gate).

5- Prevent sensitivities, suspicions, and the evil eye. The entry of a group of young men into a foreign region is a cause for suspicion and slander. "لاتدخلوا من باب واحد" (Do not enter by one gate).

6- Do not assume Allah's grace and power are limited to one path; His Hand is open, and He can assist you through any means. "لاتدخلوا من باب واحد" (Do not enter by one gate).

7- Do not merely prohibit and restrain; rather, offer a solution as well. "لاتدخلوا... و ادخلوا" (Do not enter... but enter).*

8- To reach the goal, one must traverse various paths. "و ادخلوا من ابواب متفرّقه" (and enter through separate gates).*

9- A good manager must, in addition to planning, account for probabilities, because man is not independent in managing his affairs. That is, despite all calculations and precision, the Hand of Allah is still open, and there is no guarantee that our calculations will be 100% fulfilled. "وما اُغنى عنكم من اللَّه من شى ء" (I cannot avail you anything against Allah).

10- Against the definite decrees of Allah, there is no recourse but submission. "ما اغنى عنكم من اللَّه من شى ء" (I cannot avail you anything against Allah).

11- The absolute Ruler of existence is Allah. "ان الحكم الاّ للَّه" (The command is for none but Allah).

12- Tie the camel's knee with reliance (Tawakkul). "لا تدخلوا... علیه توكّلت" (Do not enter... Upon Him I rely).*

13- Both caution and calculation are necessary; "لاتدخلوا..." (Do not enter...), and reliance upon Allah is also essential. "علیه توكّلت" (Upon Him I rely).

14- Do not rely upon anyone other than Allah. "علیه توكّلت" (Upon Him I rely), because He alone suffices: "وكفى باللَّه وكیلا" (And sufficient is Allah as Disposer of affairs) (115), and He is the best Disposer of affairs. "نعم الوكیل" (The excellent Disposer of affairs) (116).

15- Ya'qub both relied upon Allah himself and encouraged others by commanding reliance. "توكّلت... فلیتوكّل..." (I rely... let them rely...). (115) An-Nisa, 81. (116) Al 'Imran, 173.

Ayah

وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَهُمْ أَبُوهُم مَّا كَانَ يُغْنِى عَنْهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ إِلَّا حَاجَةً فِى نَفْسِ يَعْقُوبَ قَضَىٰهَا ۚ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذُو عِلْمٍ لِّمَا عَلَّمْنَـٰهُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٦۸)
When they entered whence their father had bidden them, it did not avail them anything against Allah, but only fulfilled a wish in Jacob’s heart. Indeed, he had the knowledge of what We had taught him, but most people do not know. (68)

Key Points

Regarding what the inner desire and need of Ya'qub (Jacob) was that was fulfilled, there are several possibilities: 1. The reunion of Benjamin with Joseph (عليه السلام) and Joseph's release from loneliness, even if it was through the accusation of theft. 2. Accelerating the reunion of the father and son, which will be referred to later. 3. Performing the duty without guaranteeing the result. Ya'qub's need was that there should be no shortcoming in the preliminaries of the task and that they should not enter through a single gate, but what will happen is in the hands of Allah.

Messages

  1. Bitter experiences make a person well-mannered and accepting of the words of elders. «و لمّا دخلوا من حیث امرهم ابوهم»
  2. Obedience to the father is necessary and valuable. «دخلوا من حیث امرهم ابوهم»*
  3. If you speak of the ill-manners of individuals, speak also of their good manners. «دخلوا ... ابوهم» (If the brothers previously attributed error/misguidance to their father, today they submitted to his command.)
  4. Calculations, precision, and planning are effective only when the Divine Will exists; where He does not will it, they are ineffective. «ما كان یغنى عنهم من اللَّه من شى ء»
  5. Ya'qub was aware of matters and secrets that he did not deem expedient to disclose. «حاجة فى نفس یعقوب»
  6. The prayers and needs of the friends of Allah (Awliya') are answered. «حاجة فى نفس یعقوب قضاها»
  7. The knowledge of the Prophets is from Allah. «علّمناه»
  8. Most people do not recognize the true scholars. «علّمناه و لكنّ اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»*
  9. Most people focus on means and causes and are unaware of the sovereignty of Allah and the necessity of reliance upon Him (Tawakkul). «اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»
  10. The criterion for truth and falsehood is not the judgment of the majority. «اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»*

Ayah

وَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ ءَاوَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِ أَخَاهُ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَنَا۠ أَخُوكَ فَلَا تَبْتَئِسْ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (٦۹)
When they entered into the presence of Joseph, he set his brother close to himself, and said, ‘Indeed I am your brother, so do not sorrow for what they used to do.’ (69)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the commentaries (Tafasir) that when the sons of Ya'qub (Jacob) عليه السلام entered Egypt, Yusuf (Joseph) hosted them and allocated one platter of food for every two people. Binyamin (Benjamin) was left alone at the end. Yusuf seated him next to Himself, and then assigned a room for every two people, and again made Binyamin His own roommate. Binyamin spoke about the disloyalties of his brothers and the crime they had committed against Yusuf years earlier. It was here that Yusuf's patience ran out (his cup of patience overflowed), and He said: Do not worry, I am that Yusuf, and He said with such emphasis: «انّى انا اخوك» (Indeed, I am your brother), that it left no room for the possibility of saying, "I am in the place of your brother." Regarding the phrase «فلاتبتئس بما كانوا یعملون» (So do not grieve over what they used to do), two meanings are probable: Either, do not be saddened by the past actions of your brothers, or, do not worry about the plan the servants have for keeping you, which involves placing the measure (cup) in your saddlebag so that you remain with me.

Messages

1- The brothers who yesterday boasted of their strength; «نحن عصبة» (We are a strong group), must now humbly kiss the threshold of Yusuf to obtain provisions. «دخلوا على» (They entered upon him).

2- Words have classifications: confidential and public. Yusuf only told Binyamin confidentially: «إنّى أنا أخوك» (Indeed, I am your brother). (Every word has its place, and every point has its context.)

3- In some matters, only the select few (Khawass) should be informed. «فلا تبتئس» (So do not grieve).

4- Whenever you attain a blessing, forget the bitterness of the past. (Yusuf and Binyamin met each other, so previous worries must be forgotten.) «فلاتبتئس...»

5- Before executing a plan or scheme, the innocent party must be psychologically prepared and justified. (Binyamin was told: We will keep you under the pretext of being a thief; do not worry.) «فلاتبتئس»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُم بِجَهَازِهِمْ جَعَلَ ٱلسِّقَايَةَ فِى رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ ثُمَّ أَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌ أَيَّتُهَا ٱلْعِيرُ إِنَّكُمْ لَسَـٰرِقُونَ (۷٠)
When he had furnished them with their provision, he put the drinking-cup into his brother’s saddlebag. Then a herald shouted: ‘O [men of the] caravan! You are indeed thieves!’ (70)

Key Points

117) Mufradāt. 118) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Sometimes staging a scene to uncover an incident is permissible, and for the sake of greater interests, attributing the charge of theft to an innocent person who has been previously justified (or whose innocence is known beforehand) is not prohibited. (119) «انّكم لسارقون»

2- If there is one wrongdoer within a group, people call the entire group wrongdoers. «انّكم لسارقون» 119) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ وَأَقْبَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِم مَّاذَا تَفْقِدُونَ (۷۱)
They said, as they turned towards them, ‘What are you missing?’ (71)
قَالُوا۟ نَفْقِدُ صُوَاعَ ٱلْمَلِكِ وَلِمَن جَآءَ بِهِۦ حِمْلُ بَعِيرٍ وَأَنَا۠ بِهِۦ زَعِيمٌ (۷۲)
They said, ‘We miss the king’s goblet.’ ‘Whoever brings it shall have a camel-load [of grain],’ [said the steward], ‘I will guarantee that.’ (72)

Key Points

The terms «صواع» (Sawa') and «سقایة» (Siqayah) are used with the same meaning: a measuring cup used both for drinking water and for measuring (kil and measuring) wheat. This indicates thrift, efficiency, and multi-purpose use of a single tool. «صواع» is a vessel that holds one sa' (approximately 3 kilograms) of wheat. «حِمْل» (Him'l) refers to a load, and «حَمْل» (Ham'l) also means a load, but specifically a hidden load, such as rain within a cloud or a child within a mother's womb. (120) The phrase «لمن جاء به حمل بعیر» (whoever brings it [will have] a camel's load)—meaning, whoever does this, I will give him that reward—is called «جُعاله» (Ju'alah) in jurisprudential terminology, which has legal precedent and validity.

120) Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- The brothers, using the phrase «ماذا تَفقدون» (What is it you miss?), advised that no one should be accused of theft before the matter is proven.*

2- Setting a reward is an ancient method. «ولمن جاء به حِمل بعیر» (And whoever brings it [will have] a camel's load).

3- Rewards must be appropriate for the people and the time. During a famine, the best reward is a camel's load of grain. «حِمل بَعیرٍ»

4- The guarantor must be a specific and designated individual. «و انا به زعیم» (And I am responsible for it).*

5- The guarantor must be trustworthy/credible. «و انا به زعیم» (And I am responsible for it).*

6- Taking a guarantor to gain assurance has historical precedent. «انا به زعیمٌ»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ تَٱللَّهِ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتُم مَّا جِئْنَا لِنُفْسِدَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كُنَّا سَـٰرِقِينَ (۷۳)
They said, ‘By Allah! You certainly know that we did not come to make trouble in this country, and we are not thieves.’ (73)

Key Points

Joseph's brothers said: You know that we did not come to this region for theft and corruption. Regarding how they knew this, there are several possibilities: Perhaps it was by Joseph's indication that this group were not thieves. Perhaps they had been screened upon entering the region. Yes, care must be taken regarding the entry and exit of foreign delegations, especially during critical conditions, to ensure the objectives of the travelers.

Messages

1- A good record is a sign of innocence. «لقد علمتم»

2- The security apparatus of Egypt was strong and experienced, and it knew that this caravan had not come for corruption. «لقد علمتم»*

3- Theft and robbery are among the manifestations of corruption on earth. «ماجئنا لنفسد فى الارض وما كنّا سارقین »

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ فَمَا جَزَٰٓؤُهُۥٓ إِن كُنتُمْ كَـٰذِبِينَ (۷٤)
They said, ‘What shall be its requital if you [prove to] be lying?’ (74)

Key Points

It seems that the designer of this question was Prophet Yusuf (Joseph), because He knew that his brothers would express their opinion according to the regulations and law of the Canaan region and the view of Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob).

Messages

1- Call upon the conscience of the criminal to judge his punishment. «فما جزاؤه...»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ جَزَٰٓؤُهُۥ مَن وُجِدَ فِى رَحْلِهِۦ فَهُوَ جَزَٰٓؤُهُۥ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۷٥)
They said, ‘The requital for it shall be that he in whose saddlebag it is found shall give himself over as its requital. Thus do we requite the wrongdoers.’ (75)

Messages

1- The punishment for theft among some past nations was the enslavement of the thief. "فهو جزاؤه" (Then he is his recompense). According to Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, the punishment for theft was enslavement for a period of one year.

2- There is no exception or discrimination in the law; whoever was the thief shall be enslaved. "من وجد فى رحله" (He who is found in whose saddlebag).

3- By explaining the crime, stating the law, and its punishment, reduce the accused's objection to a minimum. "جزائه مَن وُجِد..."*

4- The evidence must be such that the accused himself easily accepts the accusation. "مَن وُجد..."*

5- The presence of one wrongdoer in a group is not proof that everyone is a wrongdoer. "مَن وُجِد فى رحله"*

6- The punishment for a wrongdoer in a foreign country can be according to his own law, not the host country's law. "كذلك نجزى الظالمین" (Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers).

7- Theft is a clear example of wrongdoing (oppression/injustice). (The use of ẓālim [wrongdoer] instead of sāriq [thief]). "نجزى الظالمین" (We recompense the wrongdoers).

Ayah

فَبَدَأَ بِأَوْعِيَتِهِمْ قَبْلَ وِعَآءِ أَخِيهِ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَخْرَجَهَا مِن وِعَآءِ أَخِيهِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كِدْنَا لِيُوسُفَ ۖ مَا كَانَ لِيَأْخُذَ أَخَاهُ فِى دِينِ ٱلْمَلِكِ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجَـٰتٍ مَّن نَّشَآءُ ۗ وَفَوْقَ كُلِّ ذِى عِلْمٍ عَلِيمٌ (۷٦)
Then he began with their sacks, before [opening] his brother’s sack. Then he took it out from his brother’s sack. Thus did We devise for Joseph’s sake. He could not have held his brother under the king’s law unless Allah willed [otherwise]. We raise in rank whomever We please, and above every man of knowledge is One who knows best. (76)

Key Points

The term Kayd (كَیْد) is not always used in a blameworthy sense; Kayd is also used to mean a plan or scheme. During the inspection (referred to by kiddna - كِدنا), Benjamin was at ease because he was aware of the plan and scheme. Therefore, no objection from him has been reported throughout this entire incident. To keep the plan hidden and prevent suspicion, they began the inspection with the loads of the others until it was Benjamin's turn. When it was found in his load, according to the previous arrangement, he had to remain in Egypt. This plan and scheme was Divine, because Joseph could not keep the alleged thief as a hostage under the current laws of Egypt.

Messages

1- Intelligence officers must act in a way that does not cause suspicion or doubt toward them. «فبدأ باوعیتهم» (During the inspection, they did not go immediately to Benjamin’s load, but started with the other brothers.)

2- The actions of subordinates are attributed to the officials in charge. «فبدأ» (Apparently, Joseph did not personally conduct the inspection, but the Quran states: He began the inspection.)

3- The inspection of the property of the accused by the ruler is permissible. «فبدأ باوعیتهم»

4- Thought, innovation, and finding solutions are forms of unseen Divine aid. «كِدنا»

5- Joseph’s schemes were Divine inspirations. «كِدنا لیوسف»

6- Benjamin’s presence with Joseph was beneficial for Joseph. «كذلك كِدنا لیوسف»

7- Respecting and observing laws is necessary, even in non-Divine systems. «ماكان لیاخذ اخاه فى دین الملك»

8- Leaders must also be loyal to the law and not violate it. (Joseph adhered to the law of Egypt, which is why he kept his brother.) «لیأخذ اخاه فى دین الملك»

9- Spiritual ranks have degrees and hierarchies. «نرفع درجات...»

10- Knowledge and awareness are sources of superiority. «نرفع درجات... وفوق كلّ ذى علم علیم»

11- Human knowledge is limited. «فوق كلّ ذى علم علیم»

Ayah

۞ قَالُوٓا۟ إِن يَسْرِقْ فَقَدْ سَرَقَ أَخٌ لَّهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ فَأَسَرَّهَا يُوسُفُ فِى نَفْسِهِۦ وَلَمْ يُبْدِهَا لَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ أَنتُمْ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا ۖ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَصِفُونَ (۷۷)
They said, ‘If he has stolen [there is no wonder]; a brother of his had stolen before.’ Thereupon Joseph kept the matter to himself and he did not disclose it to them. He said, ‘You are in a worse state! And Allah knows best what you allege.’ (77)

Messages

1- The accused either denies, saying: "We are not thieves"; «ما كنّا سارقین» or justifies his action, saying: There are many thieves. «فقد سرق اخ له من قبل»

2- The envious person strikes a blow even after decades. «فقد سرق اخ له من قبل»

3- The character of a brother affects his brother. «اخ له» (The character of the mother affects the child. Benjamin and Joseph were from the same mother.)

4- Where there is no sincerity, the accusation against individuals is quickly accepted. «ان یسرق فقد...» (The discovery of the cup in his load is not proof of theft, but because the brothers had no affection for Benjamin, they used the word 'theft' and considered the matter certain.)

5- Where there is no sincerity, a minor offense is treated as a general characteristic. (Using «یسرق» instead of «سرق», meaning he has always been a thief.)

6- To achieve a goal, one must endure insults. «سرق اخ له من قبل»

7- Sometimes, to save their own reputation, they accuse others. «سرق اخ له من قبل»

8- Generosity and broad-mindedness are the secrets of leadership. «فاسرّها یوسف»

9- We should not sacrifice secrets for emotions. (Joseph heard the accusation of theft from his brothers, but for the sake of expediency and preserving his secret, he said nothing.) «فاسرّها یوسف فى نفسه»

10- Revelation is not always a virtue. «و لم یبدها لهم»

11- Envy toward their brother, lying, and baseless accusation were among Joseph’s reasons for saying «شَرّ مَكاناً» (worse in position) to his brothers.

12- Joseph’s brothers, even assuming Benjamin stole the cup, were worse than him, because they had stolen their own brother. «انتم شرّ مكاناً»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْعَزِيزُ إِنَّ لَهُۥٓ أَبًا شَيْخًا كَبِيرًا فَخُذْ أَحَدَنَا مَكَانَهُۥٓ ۖ إِنَّا نَرَىٰكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۷۸)
They said, ‘O emir! Indeed, he has a father, a very old man; so take one of us in his place. Indeed we see that you are a virtuous man.’ (78)

Key Points

When Joseph's brothers realized that Benjamin's retention was certain, considering the pledge they had made to their father, the history they had concerning Joseph with their father, and sensing that returning without Benjamin would be extremely bitter, they presented their request with a specific psychological approach and began to plead. Using emotional and moving expressions—such as, "You are the Aziz (mighty and powerful)," "You are among the doers of good," "He has an old and aged father," and "Take any one of us you wish in his place"—they sought to gain his forgiveness.

Messages

  1. Divine decrees will one day bring every hard-hearted tyrant to the dust of humiliation. The tone of pleading is evident in the phrase: «یا ایّها العزیز....»

  2. Those who are pious, whom the pleasure-seekers humiliate, Allah will grant them honor. «یا ایّها العزیز»*

  3. Joseph, even during his time of power, displayed goodness (benevolence) in his behavior and character. «نراك من المحسنین»

  4. The ups and downs and various states of time—hardship and ease, weakness and power—do not change the condition of the benevolent (the Muhsinūn). (Joseph was described as a doer of good in all places and all circumstances.) «انّا نراك من المحسنین»*

Ayah

قَالَ مَعَاذَ ٱللَّهِ أَن نَّأْخُذَ إِلَّا مَن وَجَدْنَا مَتَـٰعَنَا عِندَهُۥٓ إِنَّآ إِذًا لَّظَـٰلِمُونَ (۷۹)
He said, ‘God forbid that we should detain anyone except him with whom we found our wares, for then we would indeed be wrongdoers.’ (79)

Key Points

Careful attention to the words of Joseph (عليه السلام) indicates that He does not want to introduce Benjamin as a thief. Hence, He does not say: «وجدناه سارقاً» (We found him stealing). Rather, He says: «وجدنا متاعنا عنده» (We found our goods with him). The goods were in his load, not that he is definitely a thief. If Joseph had kept another brother instead of Benjamin, the plan would have been ruined, and the brothers would have treated him as a thief and inflicted all kinds of harm and abuse upon him. Furthermore, the one who remained instead of Benjamin would have felt that he had been unjustly detained.

Messages

1- Observing regulations is necessary for everyone, and breaking the law is forbidden even for the 'Aziz (Minister) of Egypt. «معاذ اللَّه»

2- The righteous person does not break the law. «من المحسنین. قال معاذ اللّه...»*

3- A judge must not be influenced by emotions. «قال معاذ اللّه»*

4- Judgment is a sensitive task in which one must seek refuge in Allah. «معاذ اللّه»*

5- Joseph (عليه السلام) sought refuge in Allah in two places: one during the private encounter with Zulaikha, and one at the time of judgment and ruling. «قال معاذ اللّه»*

6- Breaking the law is oppression (or injustice). (Regulations must not be broken at the request of others.) «معاذ اللَّه ان ناخذ»

7- The innocent must not be punished instead of the guilty, even if he himself consents to this action. «معاذ اللَّه»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا ٱسْتَيْـَٔسُوا۟ مِنْهُ خَلَصُوا۟ نَجِيًّا ۖ قَالَ كَبِيرُهُمْ أَلَمْ تَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ أَبَاكُمْ قَدْ أَخَذَ عَلَيْكُم مَّوْثِقًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمِن قَبْلُ مَا فَرَّطتُمْ فِى يُوسُفَ ۖ فَلَنْ أَبْرَحَ ٱلْأَرْضَ حَتَّىٰ يَأْذَنَ لِىٓ أَبِىٓ أَوْ يَحْكُمَ ٱللَّهُ لِى ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ (۸٠)
When they had despaired of [moving] him, they withdrew to confer privately. The eldest of them said, ‘Don’t you know that your father has taken a [solemn] pledge from you by Allah, and earlier you have neglected your duty in regard to Joseph? So I will never leave this land until my father permits me, or Allah passes a judgement for me, and He is the best of judges. (80)

Key Points

«خَلَصوا» means they separated their group from others. «نجیّاً» means they engaged in secret counsel (whispering). Therefore, «خَلَصوا نجیّاً» means they formed a confidential council to decide what to do.

Messages

1- Supplications and pleas should not prevent you from implementing divine decrees and acting decisively. «فلمّا استیأسوا منه»

2- Sin causes others to distance themselves from the individual, seek acquittal, and even disregard kinship ties. «فلمّا استیأسوا منه خلصوا نجیّاً»

3- Once upon a time, these same brothers powerfully consulted on how to eliminate Yusuf (Joseph): «اقتلوا یوسف او اطرحوه ارضا... لا تقتلوا ... القوه...» Today, they hold the bowl of supplication in their hands, consulting and whispering about how to free Binyamin. «خلصوا نجیّاً»

4- In the absence of the father, the responsibility for the family members rests with the eldest brother. «قال كبیرهم»

5- Observing hierarchy and age status is necessary in the family and society. «قال كبیرهم»

6- In bitter and unfortunate events, the elders are more responsible and more ashamed. «قال كبیرهم»

7- Covenants and promises are mandatory to fulfill. «اخذ علیكم موثقاً»

8- Strict covenants and firm contracts block the path to misuse (or exploitation). «اخذ علیكم موثقا»

9- Treachery and crime torment sound consciences until the end of life. « و من قبل ما فرطتهم فى یوسف»

10- Sticking to a place (refusal to leave) is one of the ancient methods for achieving a goal. «فلن ابرح الارض»

11- Exile (or being away from home) is better than shame. «فلن ابرح الارض»

12- We should be optimistic about Allah. «هو خیر الحاكمین»

Ayah

ٱرْجِعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَبِيكُمْ فَقُولُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَآ إِنَّ ٱبْنَكَ سَرَقَ وَمَا شَهِدْنَآ إِلَّا بِمَا عَلِمْنَا وَمَا كُنَّا لِلْغَيْبِ حَـٰفِظِينَ (۸۱)
Go back to your father, and say, ‘‘Father! Your son has indeed committed theft, and we testified only to what we knew, and we could not have forestalled the unseen. (81)

Messages

1- Man is selfish. When they wanted to obtain more wheat, they said: «فارسل معنا اخانا» (121) (Send our brother with us), but today, when accusation is involved, they said: «ابنك» (Your son) committed theft, and they did not say: our brother.

2- Testimony and witnessing must be based on knowledge. «ما شهدنا الاّ بما علمنا»

3- In covenants and commitments, a clause must be opened for unforeseen events. «و ما كنّا للغیب...»

4- We should state our excuse with complete frankness. «و ما كنّا للغیب حافظین»

Ayah

وَسْـَٔلِ ٱلْقَرْيَةَ ٱلَّتِى كُنَّا فِيهَا وَٱلْعِيرَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَقْبَلْنَا فِيهَا ۖ وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ (۸۲)
Ask [the people of] the town we were in, and the caravan with which we came. We indeed speak the truth.’’ ’ (82)

Key Points

Qaryah (قریه) does not only mean village; rather, it refers to a place of gathering and residential areas, whether it is a city or a village. Furthermore, «واسئل القریه» (and ask the town) means to ask the people of the qaryah. ‘Ir (عیر) refers to a caravan that transports foodstuffs. Yusuf’s brothers, in their conversation with their father regarding the incident of Yusuf being killed by the wolf, had no proof. But here, they brought two proofs for their claim: one, asking the people of Egypt, and two, asking the caravan members with whom we were. Moreover, in the previous incident, they said: «لو كنّا صادقین» (if we were truthful), and law (لَو) is a sign of doubt, anxiety, and weakness. But in this scene, by using inna (انّا) and the letter lām (ل) in the phrase «انّا لصادقون» (indeed, we are truthful), they show that they are certainly telling the truth.

Messages

1- A history of bad conduct and lying creates doubt regarding the acceptance of a person’s words throughout their life. «واسئل القریة»

2- The testimony of eyewitnesses is a valid way to prove a claim. «واسئل القریة... والعیر...»

3- The possibility of a large group conspiring to lie is negated. «و اسئل القریة... انّا لصادقون»*

Ayah

قَالَ بَلْ سَوَّلَتْ لَكُمْ أَنفُسُكُمْ أَمْرًا ۖ فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ ۖ عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَأْتِيَنِى بِهِمْ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۸۳)
He said, ‘No, your souls have made a matter seem decorous to you. Yet patience is graceful. Maybe Allah will bring them all [back] to me. Indeed He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.’ (83)

Key Points

When the brothers brought the blood-stained shirt of Joseph (Yusuf) to their father with sad and weeping appearances and said: A wolf ate Joseph. Prophet Jacob (Ya'qub) said: «بل سوّلت لكم انفسكم» (Nay, but your souls have made up a story [for you]), meaning your soul has made this deed appear beautiful in your eyes, and I will observe sabr jamil (beautiful patience). Here too, when his two sons (Benjamin and the elder son) have been separated from Him, He repeats the same sentence. Perhaps this question arises: In the incident of Joseph, the brothers plotted and betrayed, but in the incident of Benjamin, these issues were absent. So why is Jacob’s statement in both cases the same tone and the same phrase? «بل سوّلت لكم انفسكم امراً فصبر جمیل» (Nay, but your souls have made up a story [for you]; so beautiful patience is [best]). Tafsir al-Mizan answers thus: Jacob intends to say that the separation of these two brothers is also a continuation of your previous action regarding Joseph. That is, all these scenes are a consequence of that same ugly deed of yours. It might be said that Jacob’s intention is that here too you imagine yourselves to be faultless and that your action was good, but you are guilty because: Firstly: Why did you consider your brother a thief upon seeing the measure-cup in his load? Perhaps someone else had placed this cup in the load? Secondly: Why did you return quickly? You should have investigated. Thirdly: Why did you make the punishment for the thief a hostage situation? (122) However, the statement of the author of Al-Mizan is more appropriate, because the discovery of the measure-cup in Benjamin’s load would lead the general public to conclude theft, and the elder brother’s stay in Egypt was for the purpose of following up on the matter or appealing to compassion and mercy, and the punishment mentioned was the penalty for a thief in their region. Therefore, in none of the three issues was there a manifestation of the soul’s beautification (of evil) such that it would be said: «بل سوّلت لكم انفسكم».

Patience (sabr) is sometimes out of necessity and helplessness, just as the people of Hell say: «سواء علینا أصَبرنا ام جزعنا» (It is the same for us whether we show patience or impatience); showing patience or impatience has no effect on our salvation. And sometimes patience is conscious, voluntary, and submission to the Will of Allah, the manifestation of which is presented with a different title in every context; Patience in the field of Jihad is bravery. Patience regarding the world is asceticism (zuhd). Patience against sin is piety (taqwa). Patience against lust is chastity (iffah). And patience against unlawful wealth is scrupulousness (wara').

The adornment and beautification of evil deeds is sometimes by Satan; «و اذ زیّن لهم الشیطان اعمالهم» (And [remember] when Satan made their deeds pleasing to them) (123), sometimes by the glitter of the world; «حتّى اذا اخذت الارض زخرفها وازّیّنت» (Until, when the earth has taken on its adornment and is beautified) (124), and sometimes by the human soul. «سوّلت لكم انفسكم»

121) Yusuf, 63. 122) Tafsir-e Namuneh. 123) Al-Anfal, 48. 124) Yunus, 24.

Messages

  1. The soul, in order to justify sin, makes ugly deeds appear beautiful in the eyes of man. «سوّلت لكم انفسكم»
  2. Patience (sabr) is the method of the men of God, and sabr jamil (beautiful patience) is patience in which no word is uttered contrary to submission and acceptance of the Will of Allah. (125) «فصبر جمیل»
  3. We should never despair of the power of Allah. «عسى اللَّه أن یاتینى بهم»
  4. Jacob was certain that his three sons (Joseph, Benjamin, and the elder brother) were alive and was hopeful of meeting them. «أن یاتینى بهم جمیعا»
  5. For the Divine Power, solving new and old problems is the same. Allah can gather Joseph of yesterday and your brother of today together in one place. «جمیعاً»
  6. The believer considers even bitter events to be part of Allah’s Wisdom. «الحكیم»
  7. Belief in and attention to the knowledgeable and wise nature of Divine actions compels man toward patience and forbearance during difficult events. «فصبر جمیل، انّه هو العلیم الحكیم» 125) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

وَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰٓأَسَفَىٰ عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ وَٱبْيَضَّتْ عَيْنَاهُ مِنَ ٱلْحُزْنِ فَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ (۸٤)
He turned away from them and said, ‘Alas for Joseph!’ His eyes had turned white with grief, and he choked with suppressed agony. (84)

Key Points

Asaf («اسف») means sorrow and grief accompanied by anger. Jacob had tears in his eyes, Yā Asafā (Oh, my sorrow!) on his tongue, and grief in His heart. In a narration, Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Ali ibn Al-Husayn علیهما السلام would weep on every occasion for twenty years after the incident of Karbala. They asked him why He wept so much. He replied: Jacob had eleven sons; one disappeared, and although he was alive, Jacob lost His sight from weeping. But I witnessed with my own eyes my father, my brothers, and seventeen members of the Prophet's Household martyred. How can I not weep? (126)

126) Bihar, Vol. 46, p. 108.

Messages

1- Envy brings a lifetime of humiliation. «و تولّى عنهم»

2- They wanted to become beloved by their father by eliminating Joseph; «یخل لكم وجه ابیكم» but envy brought the father's estrangement upon them. «تولّى عنهم»

3- Jacob knew that the injustice was not done to the other sons; the injustice was done to Joseph. «یا اسفا على یوسف»

4- Grief and weeping sometimes lead to blindness. «ابیضّت عیناه من الحزن»

5- Lamentation, weeping, passion, and love require recognition (knowledge). (Jacob knew Joseph, which is why he became blind from the intensity of His sorrow.)

6- The significance of calamities revolves around the personality of the individuals involved. (Injustice against Joseph differs from injustice against others; Joseph's name is mentioned, not the other two brothers.)

7- Weeping, grief, and sorrow over the separation from loved ones are permissible. «وابیضّت عیناه من الحزن»

8- Human endurance has a limit, and eventually, it overflows. «وابیضّت عیناه من الحزن»*

9- Suppressing anger (kadhmu al-ghayz) is a characteristic of divine men and a commendable action. «فهو كظیم»*

10- Weeping and grief do not contradict the suppression of anger (kadhmu al-ghayz) and patience. «فصبرٌ جمیل، یا اسفا، فهو كظیم»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ تَٱللَّهِ تَفْتَؤُا۟ تَذْكُرُ يُوسُفَ حَتَّىٰ تَكُونَ حَرَضًا أَوْ تَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْهَـٰلِكِينَ (۸٥)
They said, ‘By Allah! You will go on remembering Joseph until you wreck your health or perish.’ (85)

Key Points

Ḥaraḍ (حَرَض) refers to a person who has been worn out by love, sorrow, or grief.

Messages

1- Josephs (Yūsufs) must always be remembered. «تفتؤا تذكر یوسف» (The friends of Allah, in the Du'a al-Nudbah, call upon the Joseph of the Age and weep.)

2- If you want to see how much you love someone, observe how often you remember him. «تفتؤا تذكر یوسف»*

3- He who knows Joseph possesses a burning grief that ordinary people do not comprehend. «تذكر یوسف حتّى تكون حرضاً»

4- Spiritual and psychological issues affect the body. «حرضاً او تكون من الهالكین» (Separation can break or kill a person, let alone deep sorrow and calamity.)

5- The measure of paternal affection is separate from ordinary attachments. «تكون من الهالكین»

Ayah

قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أَشْكُوا۟ بَثِّى وَحُزْنِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۸٦)
He said, ‘I complain of my anguish and grief only to Allah. I know from Allah what you do not know.’ (86)

Key Points

Batth (بثّ) refers to intense grief, the severity of which seemingly prevents the bearer from expressing it. Prophet Adam lamented his action before Allah: «قالا ربّنا ظلمنا انفسنا» (127) And Prophet Ayyub (Job) lamented his illness: «انّى مسّنى الضرّ» (128) And Prophet Musa (Moses) lamented poverty and destitution: «ربّ انّى لما انزلت الىّ من خیر فقیر» (129) And Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) lamented the separation from his son: «انّما اشكوا بثّى و حزنى»

127) Al-A'raf, 23. 128) Al-Anbiya, 83. 129) Al-Qasas, 24.

Messages

1- What is blameworthy is either silence that pressures the heart and nerves, endangering human health, or wailing and crying out before people, which lowers one's standing. However, complaining (or expressing grief) to Allah is permissible. «انّما اشكوا... الى اللَّه»

2- A monotheist shares his inner pain only with Allah. «انّما اشكوا... الى اللَّه»

When you raise the hand of need, raise it to a Lord Who is Generous (Karim), Merciful (Rahim), Forgiving (Ghafur), and Loving (Wadud). His bounty is infinite, His generosity endless, No supplicant leaves this door without achieving his aim.

3- Superficial people easily pass by events, but profound individuals see the consequences and events extending until the Day of Judgment. «اعلم من اللَّه ...»

4- Ya'qub was aware of Yusuf's being alive, the end of their separation, and certain truths about Allah and His attributes that were hidden from others. «اعلم من اللَّه ما لاتعلمون»

5- Perhaps «ما لا تعلمون» (what you do not know) refers to the dream of Yusuf, which Ya'qub had interpreted at the beginning.*

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىَّ ٱذْهَبُوا۟ فَتَحَسَّسُوا۟ مِن يُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ وَلَا تَا۟يْـَٔسُوا۟ مِن رَّوْحِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يَا۟يْـَٔسُ مِن رَّوْحِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸۷)
‘Go, my sons, and look for Joseph and his brother, and do not despair of Allah’s mercy. Indeed no one despairs of Allah’s mercy except the faithless lot.’ (87)

Key Points

"Tahaṣṣus" (تحسّس) is the search for something through the senses. "Tajassus" (تجسّس) refers to searching for bad things, while "Tahaṣṣus" (تحسّس) refers to searching for good things. According to Raghib, rawḥ (رَوْح) and rūḥ (روح) both mean soul or spirit (jān), but rawḥ (رَوح) is used in contexts of relief (faraj) and mercy (raḥmah). It is as if a new life is breathed into a person with the untying of a knot or problem. It is stated in Tafsir al-Tibyan: Rūḥ (روح) comes from rīḥ (ریح - wind), just as a person feels comfort with the blowing of the wind, so too does a person become joyful through Divine mercy. Based on narrations, despairing of Allah's mercy is one of the major sins (kabā'ir).(130)

130) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Bāb Ma‘rifat al-Kabā’ir.

Messages

1- A father should not permanently sever ties with his children. «فتولّى عنهم... یابنىّ»

2- Knowledge requires movement. «اذهبوا فتحسّسوا»

3- Attaining Divine favor is incompatible with laziness. «اذهبوا، ولا تایْئسوا»

4- The friends of Allah (Awliyā') neither despair themselves nor prevent others from despairing. «لاتایئسوا»

5- Despair is a sign of disbelief (kufr). «لایایئس... الاّ القوم الكافرون» because the one who despairs says internally: Allah's power has ended.

Ayah

فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا ٱلضُّرُّ وَجِئْنَا بِبِضَـٰعَةٍ مُّزْجَىٰةٍ فَأَوْفِ لَنَا ٱلْكَيْلَ وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنَآ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَجْزِى ٱلْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ (۸۸)
Then, when they entered into his presence, they said, ‘O emir! Distress has befallen us and our family, and we have brought [just] a meagre sum. Yet grant us the full measure, and be charitable to us! Indeed Allah rewards the charitable.’ (88)

Key Points

Bida'ah (goods/merchandise) refers to wealth that takes the title of "price" (or value). Muzjāh (poorly valued) is derived from the root izjā' meaning to repel or drive away. Since sellers usually return low-value prices, it is called biḍā'atin muzjātin (poorly valued merchandise). Some commentators have said that the intent of taṣaddaqa ‘alaynā (be charitable to us) is the request for the return of Benjamin. It is narrated in traditions that Jacob wrote a letter to Joseph, the content of which was the glorification of Joseph, the description of the famine in Canaan, the request for Benjamin's release, and the exoneration of his sons from theft, and he sent it to Joseph along with his sons. When Joseph read the letter in front of his brothers, he kissed it, placed it on his eyes, and wept so much that drops of tears fell upon his clothes. The brothers, who still did not recognize Joseph, were astonished, wondering why he showed so much respect to their father. Gradually, a spark of hope lit up in their hearts. When they saw Joseph smile, they said to themselves, "Could he possibly be Joseph?" (131)

(131) Tafsir-e Nemooneh.

Messages

1- For Jacob, Joseph is the concern; «فتحسّسوا من یوسف» (search for Joseph), but for the sons, it is the wheat. «فأوف لنا الكیل» (give us full measure).

2- Those who humiliate others will one day be humiliated. The sons who arrogantly said: «نحن عصبة» (we are a strong group), "Our brother stole before," «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال» (indeed, our father is in manifest error), today humbly say: «مسنّا و اهلنا الضرّ» (distress has afflicted us and our family).

3- A specific etiquette is necessary when requesting help and assistance:

A: Glorifying the helper. «ایّها العزیز» (O mighty one).

B: Stating one's condition and need. «مسّنا و اهلنا الضّر» (distress has afflicted us and our family).

C: Lack of funds (financial poverty). «بضاعة مزجاة» (poorly valued merchandise).

D: Creating motivation in the helper. «فتصدّق علینا انّ اللَّه یجزى المتصدّقین» (so be charitable to us; indeed, Allah rewards the charitable).

4- Poverty and need humiliate a person. «مسنّا و اهلنا الضر»

That which turns lions into foxes Is need, is need, is need.

Ayah

قَالَ هَلْ عَلِمْتُم مَّا فَعَلْتُم بِيُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ إِذْ أَنتُمْ جَـٰهِلُونَ (۸۹)
He said, ‘Have you realized what you did to Joseph and his brother, when you were senseless?’ (89)

Key Points

A single question may conceal various objectives, whether positive and constructive, or negative and harmful. Yusuf’s question, when He asked, “Do you know what you did to Yusuf and his brother?”, might be intended to show that He knows the whole story. Or, the objective of the question might be to imply that they committed a bad deed and should repent. Or, His objective might be to comfort Benjamin, who is present in the gathering. And His objective might be reproach, rebuke, and blame, or showing off His own glory, or reproaching them by asking why they hope for charity despite all these crimes? Among the intentions mentioned, the first three are compatible with the status of Yusuf (Joseph), but the remaining cases are inconsistent with the generosity (futūwwah) and nobility that the subsequent verses report about Yusuf. Despite hearing the accusation of theft, He said nothing, and at the end of the affair, He told His brothers: «لاتثریب علیكم الیوم» (There shall be no reproach upon you this day). Ignorance (jahl) is not merely the meaning of not knowing; rather, the dominance of desires is a type of inattention. A sinful person, even if knowledgeable, is ignorant because he is inattentive and buys the fire of Hell for himself.

Messages

1- The file of errors and sins will one day be opened. «هل علمتم ما فعلتم»*

2- Generosity (futūwwah) lies in not mentioning the details of the transgression. «ما فعلتم»

3- When you attain power, do not forget the previously oppressed. «ما فعلتم بیوسف و اخیه»*

4- Generosity (futūwwah) lies in suggesting a path of excuse to the wrongdoer. «اذ انتم جاهلون»

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَءِنَّكَ لَأَنتَ يُوسُفُ ۖ قَالَ أَنَا۠ يُوسُفُ وَهَـٰذَآ أَخِى ۖ قَدْ مَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْنَآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَتَّقِ وَيَصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۹٠)
They said, ‘Are you really Joseph?!’ He said, ‘I am Joseph, and this is my brother. Certainly Allah has shown us favour. Indeed if one is Godwary and patient Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.’ (90)

Key Points

As time passed, the brothers became more bewildered as to why the Aziz (Governor) of Egypt wept upon receiving their father's letter! How does the Aziz know the story of Yusuf (Joseph)? Truly, his appearance bears a great resemblance to Yusuf! Could He be Yusuf? It would be best to ask Him directly. If He wasn't Yusuf, wouldn't they call us mad? And if He was Yusuf, what would we do out of shame? Excitement had seized the brothers entirely. Finally, they broke the spell of silence with the question, "Are you Yusuf?" What a scene unfolded here? And which painter can depict a portrait of shame, joy, weeping, and embracing? Only Allah knows. Conditions must be arranged such that people are motivated to ask questions, thereby raising their incentive for growth and education. The motivation for searching and questioning constantly increased among Yusuf's brothers. They said to themselves: Why did He insist that we bring Benjamin with us? Why was the measuring cup found in our baggage? Why did He return our money the first time? How does He know the story of Yusuf? What if He refuses to give us grain anymore? When these excitements peaked in their souls, they asked: Are you Yusuf? He said: Yes.... Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Indeed, in Al-Qa'im (the Awaited One) عليه السلام there is a tradition (Sunnah) of Yusuf... People will not recognize Him until Allah grants Him permission to introduce Himself.(132)

(132) Bihar, Vol. 12, p. 283.

Messages

1- The passage of time and bitter and sweet events change relationships and recognition. «ءانّك لانت یوسف»

2- The friends of Allah attribute all blessings to Him. «مَنّ اللَّه علینا»

3- The reward for patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) is given even in this world. «انا یوسف... مَن یّتق و یصبر...»*

4- The one worthy of leadership and governance is he who has been tested against calamities, jealousies, desires, humiliations, prisons, malicious propaganda, and so forth. «مَن یتّق و یصبر...»

5- Utilize the most sensitive times for propagation (Dawah). (When the brothers are ashamed of their actions and ready to accept Yusuf's words, He says: «مَن یتّق ویصبر...»)

6- The grace of Allah is wise and based on criteria and standards. «من یتّق و یصبر فانّ اللَّه...»

7- Patience and piety are the foundation of honor (Izzah). «مَن یتّق و یصبر فانّ اللَّه لایضیع...»

8- Piety and patience are among the characteristics of the doers of good (Muhsinin). «من یتّق و یصبر فانّ اللّه لا یضیع اجر المحسنین»*

9- One of the divine traditions (Sunan) is the governance of the righteous. «لایضیع اجر المحسنین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ تَٱللَّهِ لَقَدْ ءَاثَرَكَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَإِن كُنَّا لَخَـٰطِـِٔينَ (۹۱)
They said, ‘By Allah, Allah has certainly preferred you over us, and we have indeed been erring.’ (91)

Key Points

Īthār (selflessness/altruism) means giving preference to others over oneself. Yusuf's brothers, due to the mistaken notion of "نحن عصبة" (We are a strong group), committed a wrong act and said: Throw him into the bottom of the well; «القوه فى غیابت الجب». Allah put them in distress to the point where they had to beg just to feed themselves; «مسّنا و اهلنا الضّر» (Distress has afflicted us and our family). Then they confessed that their plan had failed; «كنّا لخاطئین» (We were certainly sinners). Then that mistaken notion turned into the acceptance of a reality: «لقد آثرك اللَّه علینا» (Allah has indeed preferred you over us).

Yusuf's brothers swore oaths several times using the formula "تاللَّه" (By Allah): 1. «تاللَّه لقد علمتم ما جئنا لنفسد فى الارض» By Allah, you yourselves know that we did not come to cause corruption or theft in your land. 2. «تاللَّه تفتوء تذكر یوسف» They said to their father: By Allah, you will never cease remembering Yusuf. 3. «تاللَّه انك لفى ضلالك القدیم» Father, by Allah, regarding your excessive affection for Yusuf, you are caught up in your old deviation. 4. «تاللَّه لقد اثرك اللَّه علینا» They said to Yusuf: By Allah, Allah has indeed preferred you over us.

Messages

1- If we do not confess to the perfections and superiority of others out of jealousy, we will be forced to acknowledge them under pressure and humiliation. «لقد آثرك اللَّه علینا»

2- One cannot resist the Will of Allah. «آثرك اللَّه علینا»

3- On the day of power, let us not act in a way that causes us shame in times of weakness. «و نحن عصبة... و ان كنّا لخاطئین»*

4- Confession of error paves the way for pardon and forgiveness. «إن كنّا لخاطئین»

Ayah

قَالَ لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ ۖ يَغْفِرُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (۹۲)
He said, ‘There shall be no reproach on you today. Allah will forgive you, and He is the most merciful of the merciful. (92)

Key Points

Tathrīb (تثریب) means severe reprimand, counting sins, and excessive blame. On the Day of the Conquest of Mecca, the polytheists had sought refuge in the Ka'bah. Umar said, "Today we will take revenge." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said, "Today is a day of mercy," and He asked the polytheists, "What do you think of me today?" They replied, "It is good; you are our noble brother." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said, "My words today are the same as the words of Joseph: «لا تثريب عليكم اليوم» (There shall be no blame upon you this day.)" Umar said, "I am ashamed of what I said." (133) Ali عليه السلام says: «اذا قدرتَ على عدوّك فاجعل العفو عنه شكراً للقدرة علیه» (When you gain power over your enemy, make forgiving him the gratitude for that power.) Whenever you gain victory over your enemy, make the forgiveness of the enemy the gratitude for it. (134) We read in a Hadith: The heart of a young person is more tender. Then He recited this Ayah (as evidence): Because Joseph was younger than his father and brothers, he immediately forgave his brothers. (135)

133) Tafsir Qurtubi. 134) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 11. 135) Bihar, Vol. 12, p. 280.

Messages

1- Broad-mindedness (tolerance) is the tool and means of leadership. «لا تثريب عليكم اليوم»

2- As soon as the wrongdoer confesses, accept it and do not shame him. «انّا كنّا خاطئین. قال لاتثريب عليكم»

3- Announce your forgiveness to everyone so that others do not reproach them either. «لاتثريب عليكم»

4- Joseph عليه السلام undertook the psychological and spiritual rehabilitation of his sinful brothers; we should do the same. «لاتثريب...»*

5- Forgiveness and pardon of people must be immediate. «الیوم »

6- Forgiveness at the peak of honor and power is the practice (Sīrah) of the friends of Allah. «لاتثريب عليكم اليوم»

7- Let us learn chivalry (generosity) from Joseph, who both forgave his own right and sought pardon and forgiveness from Allah. «لاتثريب ... يغفر اللَّه»

8- Where a servant forgives, what expectation do we have from Allah, Who is the Most Merciful of the merciful (Arham ar-Rāhimīn), except pardon? «يغفر اللَّه لكم»

9- Forgiving the ashamed is the Divine practice. (The verb yaghfir [يغفر] is used in the present tense/imperfect form.)

10- Allah's pardon includes even those who caused distress to two of His Prophets (Joseph and Jacob) for years. «و هو ارحم الرّاحمين»

11- The forgiveness of the oppressed toward the oppressors paves the way for the inclusion of Divine forgiveness, but their pardon is also contingent upon Allah's forgiveness and mercy. «لا تثريب عليكم... يغفر اللَّه لكم و هو ارحم الرّاحمين»

12- Describing Allah with forgiveness and mercy (such as Arham ar-Rāhimīn) is among the etiquette of supplication (Du'a) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfār).

Ayah

ٱذْهَبُوا۟ بِقَمِيصِى هَـٰذَا فَأَلْقُوهُ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِ أَبِى يَأْتِ بَصِيرًا وَأْتُونِى بِأَهْلِكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۹۳)
Take this shirt of mine, and cast it upon my father’s face; he will regain his sight, and bring me all your folks.’ (93)

Key Points

In the story of Prophet Joseph, his shirt is mentioned in several places: A: «و جاؤا على قمیصه بدم كذب» The brothers stained Joseph's shirt with false blood and brought it to their father, claiming the wolf had eaten Joseph. B: «قدّ قمیصه من دبر» The shirt was torn from the back, leading to the discovery of the crime and the criminal. C: «اذهبوا بقمیصى» The shirt causes the healing of the blind Jacob. If a shirt that was near Joseph can restore sight to the blind, then there is hope for healing through seeking blessing (tabarruk) from the shrine, courtyard, door, wall, cloth, and anything else that is near the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah).

Up to this point, the stages of recognizing Joseph, the brothers apologizing to Him, Joseph's pardon and forgiveness, and the request for divine forgiveness have passed. However, the father's blindness, which resulted from the brothers' crime, still remains. In this Ayah, the solution to this problem is presented.

Furthermore, it is narrated that Joseph said: Let the person who brought my blood-stained shirt to my father be the one to take this shirt to him, so that just as he grieved the father, he may now gladden his heart with this shirt.

It is narrated that Joseph would seat his brothers at His table every day and night, and they felt ashamed. They sent a message to Joseph asking that their table be separate, because His face made them ashamed! Joseph replied: But I am honored to be beside you and eat with you. There was a time when people saw me and said: «سبحان من بلّغ عبداً بیع بعشرین درهماً ما بلغ» (Glory be to Him Who raised a slave sold for twenty dirhams to such a rank.) But today, your presence is an honor for me. Now people know that I was not a slave, nor without lineage. I had brothers like you and a father like Jacob, but I had become a stranger.(136) (Allah is Greater than this chivalry and generosity)

It is narrated that the late Grand Ayatollah Hajj Sheikh Abdul Karim Ha'iri Yazdi, who had traveled from Arak towards Tehran for treatment, stayed one night in Qom. The people requested Him to move His seminary (Hawza Ilmiyya) from Arak to Qom, because Qom is the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام and the burial place of Lady Ma'suma علیها السلام. He performed Istikhara (seeking guidance from Allah) and this Ayah came: «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین» (And bring to me all your family.)

(136) Tafsir Kabir, quoted from Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

  1. Seeking blessing (Tabarruk) through objects related to the friends of Allah is permissible. «اذهبوا بقمیصى» (Joseph's shirt restores sight to the blind eye.)
  2. A person who fights against desires (hawa wa hawas), even his clothing becomes sacred. «بقمیصى»
  3. Merely seeing sacred objects is not enough for seeking blessing; one must touch it. «القوه على وجه ابى»* (Place it upon the face of my father.)
  4. Sorrow and joy affect eyesight. «و ابیضّت عیناه من الحزن ... یأت بصیراً» Perhaps this is why a good child is called "Qurrat al-'Ayn" (coolness of the eye). (Of course, if we do not analyze it from the perspective of a miracle.)
  5. Reunion revives the old heart and restores sight to the blind. «فالقوه على وجه ابى یأت بصیراً»*
  6. Age is not a condition for miracles and divine favors (karamat). (The son's shirt restores the father's eyes.)
  7. Joseph possessed knowledge of the unseen (Ilm al-Ghayb), otherwise, how would He know that the shirt would restore His father's sight? «یأت بصیراً»
  8. Allah made what was once the cause of Jacob's sorrow, the means of his joy and healing. (The shirt was once the cause of grief, and today it is the cause of joy.)*
  9. Affluent children should provide coverage for weak relatives, especially elderly parents. «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین»
  10. Social conditions affect the performance of duty. «واتونى باهلكم اجمعین» (Joseph's maintaining kinship ties (Silat al-Rahm) in those circumstances required the family to come to Egypt.)
  11. Attending to relatives is necessary while preserving the rights of other people. «اتونى باهلكم»
  12. Changing residence and migration has many effects, including transforming sad memories. «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین»
  13. Family members and close relatives should live near and beside each other if possible. «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین»*
  14. Joseph's علیه السلام chivalry (Javanmardi) was so great that although his brothers could not tolerate Him and threw Him into the well, Joseph علیه السلام invited all His brothers and their families. «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین»*
  15. Kinship ties should not be severed, even with relatives who have erred. «و اتونى باهلكم اجمعین»*
  16. Provision for welfare must be made for those who have suffered the pain of separation. «اجمعین» Jacob no longer had the strength for separation.
  17. The best kindness is that which includes everyone. «اجمعین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا فَصَلَتِ ٱلْعِيرُ قَالَ أَبُوهُمْ إِنِّى لَأَجِدُ رِيحَ يُوسُفَ ۖ لَوْلَآ أَن تُفَنِّدُونِ (۹٤)
As the caravan set off, their father said, ‘I sense the scent of Joseph, if you will not consider me a dotard.’ (94)

Key Points

Fasalt (فَصلت) means "it separated" or "it distanced itself." Fasalt al-'īr (فَصَلت العیر) means "the caravan distanced itself from Egypt." Tufannidūn (تُفنّدون) is derived from the root fanad (فَنَد), meaning intellectual weakness or foolishness. Jacob was concerned that those around him might attribute foolishness to him, so he said, "Lau lā an tufannidūn" (If you would not accuse me of senility). But, regrettably, those around the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) and some of his companions attributed this very thing to him when, on the verge of his passing, he said: "Bring a pen and paper so that I may dictate something to be recorded, which if you adhere to, you will never go astray!" One person said: "Inn ar-rajula layahjur" (137) (The man is raving/delirious), and they did not allow anything to be written.

Question: How is it that only Jacob perceives the scent of Joseph? "Innī la-ajidu rīha Yūsuf" (Indeed, I perceive the scent of Joseph).

Answer: There is no impediment to this, just as the Prophets perceive revelation while we do not perceive it. In other matters too, they may find something that we do not find. Was it not the case that on the eve of the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq), while digging the trench, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, upon seeing a flash of light caused by the pickaxe striking a stone, said: "In this flash, I saw the fall of empires"? However, some weak-faithed individuals said: "The Prophet is digging a trench around the city out of fear, yet with every strike and spark, He promises the downfall of a regime and government and His own victory!"

In the commentary on Nahj al-Balagha by the late Khū'ī, it is stated: For the infallible Imam, there is a pillar of light, and when Allah wills it, the Imam looks at it and sees the future, though sometimes He is like ordinary people.

You smelled the scent of His shirt from Egypt, Why did you not see Him in the well of Canaan? He replied: Our state is like the lightning of the world, Sometimes manifest, and the next moment, hidden. Sometimes we sit upon the highest dome, Sometimes we cannot see even behind our own feet.

Perhaps the meaning of the "scent of Joseph" is fresh news about Joseph. This issue is known today in the world of science as "telepathy," meaning the transfer of thought from distant points, and it has become an established scientific matter. That is, those who have a close bond or possess special spiritual power, when an event occurs to one person in one corner of the world, the other person perceives it in another corner of the world.

A person asked Imam Baqir عليه السلام: Sometimes, without reason, sadness overwhelms my heart in a way that those around me notice. The Imam said: Muslims are created from a single reality and nature (tīnah). When a bitter incident happens to one of them, the other becomes sad in another land or region. (138)

If we consider the perception of Joseph's scent to be related to the sense of smell, it must be accepted as an extraordinary event and a miracle that Jacob smelled a scent from afar.

During the time of Iraq's aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran, when people were present on the western and southern fronts by the command of Imam Khomeini (may his secret be sanctified), I was also serving with the Martyr Ayatollah Ashrafi Esfahani, who was about ninety years old, during the "Muslim ibn Aqil" operation. He repeatedly told me on the night of the attack: "I perceive the scent of Paradise," but no matter how much I smelled, I found nothing! Yes, a person who has spent ninety years in knowledge, piety, asceticism, and night vigil (tahajjud) can have a feeling that others do not possess. Just as his prophecy that he would be the fourth Martyr of the Mihrab came true! (139)

In any case, the intent behind the scent of Paradise might be a mystical scent, similar to the sweetness of supplication (munājāt), which is a spiritual taste. It might also be a natural scent, but not every sense of smell is worthy of perceiving it, similar to radio waves in the atmosphere, but not every radio can pick up all of them.

(137) Sawārim al-Muhriqah, p. 224. (138) Tafsir Namūneh. (139) Within a year or two, the hypocrites (munāfiqūn) dismembered Ayatollah Madani, Sadūqī, Dastgheib, and him (Ashrafi Esfahani) with grenades during Friday prayers or on the way to Friday prayers.

Messages

1- Man, through inner purity, can perceive spiritual realities. "Innī la-ajidu rīha Yūsuf." However, the perception of realities is limited; it is not the case that they can perceive everything at every time and place. Thus, he perceived the scent of the shirt only after the caravan had distanced itself. "Fasalt al-'īr."

2- If we do not perceive certain realities, we should not deny the status of others. "Lau lā an tufannidūn."

3- Not all people have the capacity to hear realities, and they attribute foolishness to the speaker. "Lau lā an tufannidūn."*

4- The life of scholars among the ignorant is painful and difficult. "Lau lā an tufannidūn."

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ تَٱللَّهِ إِنَّكَ لَفِى ضَلَـٰلِكَ ٱلْقَدِيمِ (۹٥)
They said, ‘By God, you persist in your inveterate error.’ (95)

Key Points

In Ayah 8 of this Surah, we read that the brothers said concerning their father: «انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین» (Indeed, our father is in manifest error). [They meant] Our father is in manifest error because of his undue affection for Joseph and his brother. Here (in this Ayah), they said: «ضلالك القدیم» (your ancient error), meaning he is still persisting in that previous mistake. Ordinary people should not judge the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) based on their own understanding, nor should they rule whether something is possible or impossible. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «النّاس اعداء ما جهلوا»(140) meaning, people oppose every knowledgeable person simply because they themselves do not know [that thing].

140) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 172.

Messages

1- Do not measure the deeds of the righteous by your own standards. «انّك لفى ضلالك» (Attributing error to the father stemmed from comparing and measuring his perception against their own understanding.)

2- Throughout the period of separation from Joseph, Jacob believed that he was alive and expressed this belief to those around him. «انّك لفى ضلالك القدیم»

Ayah

فَلَمَّآ أَن جَآءَ ٱلْبَشِيرُ أَلْقَىٰهُ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهِۦ فَٱرْتَدَّ بَصِيرًا ۖ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۹٦)
When the bearer of good news arrived, he cast it on his face, and he regained his sight. He said, ‘Did I not tell you, ‘‘I know from Allah what you do not know?’’ ’ (96)

Key Points

If the meaning of the eyes turning white, «وابیضت عیناه» (and his eyes turned white), is a reduction in light (vision), then «بصیراً» (seeing) means the restoration of full light (vision). This indicates that sorrow and joy affect human sight and visual power. However, if the meaning is absolute blindness, which is suggested by the apparent meaning of the verse and «فارتدّ بصیراً» (so he returned seeing), then it is a miracle and an act of tawassul (seeking intercession) which the Quran confirms. The world has its ups and downs; the brothers of Yusuf (Joseph) one day brought the news of Yusuf being killed by a wolf, and another day they brought the news of Yusuf becoming a ruler.

Messages

1- The source of the knowledge of the Prophets is Divine knowledge. «أعلم من اللَّه...»

2- The Divine Prophets are confident in the promises of Allah. «ألَم أقل...»

3- Ya'qub (Jacob), unlike his children, was confident in Yusuf's survival and the eventual end of their separation. «ألم أقل لكم»

4- An unrighteous child causes the father's blindness, and a righteous child causes his sight to be restored. «ابیضّت عیناه... فارتدّ»*

5- The Divine Will governs natural laws. «فارتدّ بصیراً»

6- The clothing and belongings of the friends of Allah (Awliya') can be a source of effect (blessing). «فارتدّ بصیراً»

7- How often is something considered undesirable (Makruh), yet it contains good for everyone. For instance, the famine was unpleasant, but it led to the freedom and rulership of an innocent person like Yusuf, the reunion of Ya'qub, and the control of the famine. «انّى اعلم من اللّه مالاتعلمون»*

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَبَانَا ٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا خَـٰطِـِٔينَ (۹۷)
They said, ‘Father! Plead [with Allah] for forgiveness of our sins! We have indeed been erring.’ (97)
قَالَ سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّىٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۹۸)
He said, ‘I shall plead with my Lord to forgive you; indeed He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.’ (98)

Key Points

Jacob's sons were monotheists and were aware of their father's high status; "یا ابانا استغفر لنا" (O our father, ask forgiveness for us). What they attributed to their father in previous verses as "ضلال" (misguidance) was not misguidance in belief, but rather misjudgment and excessive affection for Joseph.

For the oppressor, there are three days: the day of power, the day of respite, and the day of regret. For the oppressed, there are also three days: the day of sorrow (for having been wronged), the day of confusion (while seeking a solution), and the day of victory in this world or the Hereafter.

Those who mistakenly told their father, "انّ ابانا لفى ضلال مبین" (Indeed, our father is in manifest error), after realizing their mistake, say: "انّا كنّا خاطئین" (Indeed, we were sinners/mistaken).

It is mentioned in the Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Atyab al-Bayan that Prophet Jacob was waiting for the night of Friday (Jumu'ah) or dawn (Sahar) to pray for his sons.

Messages

1- Oppression leads to humiliation. The day the brothers threw Joseph into the well was a day of laughter for them and humiliation for Joseph, but today the reverse has occurred.

2- Seeking intercession (tawassul) through the friends of Allah for the forgiveness of sins is permissible. "یا ابانا استغفر لنا"

3- A father's prayer has a special effect. "یا ابانا استغفر لنا"*

4- It is never too late for repentance. "استغفر لنا"*

5- Confession of sin and error is the basis for forgiveness. "انّا كنّا خاطئین"*

6- Specific times have priority for prayer (Du'a). "قال سوف استغفر لكم"

7- A father's prayer on behalf of his children has a special effect. "سوف استغفر لكم"

8- A father should not be resentful or hold grudges against his children for their mistakes. "استغفر لكم"

9- Do not reproach the offender when he confesses. When they said, "انّا كنّا خاطئین" (Indeed, we were sinners), the father said, "سوف استغفر لكم" (I will ask forgiveness for you).

10- We should give the sinner hope in Divine forgiveness. "قال سوف استغفر لكم ربّى"*

11- We must be patient regarding the acceptance of prayers and seeking intercession through the friends of Allah. "سوف استغفر لكم"*

12- Prophet Jacob forgave his own right (against them) and promised to pray for his children regarding the right of Allah. "استغفر لكم ربّى"*

13- Allah's grace includes even the greatest sins and sinners. "هو الغفور الرّحیم" (He is the Forgiving, the Merciful). Despite the fact that two Divine Prophets were subjected to years of harm and distress, there is still hope for forgiveness from Him.

Ayah

فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ يُوسُفَ ءَاوَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِ أَبَوَيْهِ وَقَالَ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ مِصْرَ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ءَامِنِينَ (۹۹)
When they entered into the presence of Joseph, he set his parents close to himself, and said, ‘Welcome to Egypt, in safety, God willing!’ (99)

Key Points

I do not know how to write this part of the story! Yusuf (Joseph), in preparation for receiving his parents, had pitched a tent outside the city and was waiting to bring them into Egypt with honor and respect; «فلمّا دخلوا على یوسف... ادخلوا مصر» Naturally, when Yusuf’s father, mother, and brothers prepared themselves for the journey, there was excitement and commotion in Canaan. People saw how, after many years, upon receiving the good news of Yusuf’s well-being, Ya‘qub (Jacob), having regained his sight, eagerly intended to visit his son. They were also happy about the condition of this father and son, especially since Yusuf was the treasurer and ruler in Egypt and had supported them during the famine by sending grain. With what passion, excitement, and love can this story be written and concluded! From the word «اَبوَیه» (his parents), it is clear that Yusuf’s mother was also alive. However, a question that I myself have not found the answer to is why, throughout the entire story, there is no mention of his mother’s weeping, sorrow, or lamentations, and why this subject has remained silent? It is narrated in traditions that Ya‘qub insisted and swore, asking Yusuf to recount his story. When Yusuf began to tell how his brothers took him to the edge of the well and forcibly removed his shirt, Ya‘qub fainted. When he regained consciousness, he asked him to continue, but Yusuf said: Father, by the right of Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma‘il (Ishmael), and Ishaq (Isaac) علیهم السلام, excuse me from relating the story! Ya‘qub accepted. (141)

141) Tafsir Namuneh and Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Receiving guests outside the city is a virtuous act. «فلمّا دخلوا على یوسف» The reception ceremony for Ya‘qub was held outside the city, and Yusuf had pitched a tent there.

2- Rank and position should not make us neglect honoring our parents. «قال ادخلوا مصر...»

3- Even if the highest official of the country wishes to speak about the security of his land, he must pay attention to the grace of Allah. «ان شاء اللَّه» Because unless God wills it, there is no security, just as a group built homes out of mountain stones to be safe, but the wrath of Allah destroyed their security. «وكانوا ینحتون من الجبال بیوتاً آمنین فاخذتهم الصیحة مصبحین» (142)

4- In choosing a place of residence, one of the most important issues is security. «آمنین»

5- If people like Yusuf are rulers, security will be established. «آمنین» 142) Al-Hijr, 82-83.

Ayah

وَرَفَعَ أَبَوَيْهِ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ وَخَرُّوا۟ لَهُۥ سُجَّدًا ۖ وَقَالَ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ هَـٰذَا تَأْوِيلُ رُءْيَـٰىَ مِن قَبْلُ قَدْ جَعَلَهَا رَبِّى حَقًّا ۖ وَقَدْ أَحْسَنَ بِىٓ إِذْ أَخْرَجَنِى مِنَ ٱلسِّجْنِ وَجَآءَ بِكُم مِّنَ ٱلْبَدْوِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَن نَّزَغَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَ إِخْوَتِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى لَطِيفٌ لِّمَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱٠٠)
And he seated his parents high upon the throne, and they fell down prostrate before him. He said, ‘Father! This is the fulfillment of my dream of long ago, which my Lord has made come true. He was certainly gracious to me when He brought me out of the prison and brought you over from the desert after that Satan had incited ill feeling between me and my brothers. Indeed my Lord is all-attentive in bringing about what He wishes. Indeed He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.’ (100)

Key Points

‘Arsh’ (the Throne) refers to the seat upon which a sovereign sits. ‘Kharroo’ means to fall to the ground. ‘Badw’ means the desert or wilderness, and ‘Nazgh’ is defined as entering into an affair with the intention of corruption. ‘Latif’ (Subtle/Kind) is one of the Names of Allah, meaning His power penetrates even the most intricate and complex affairs. Its relevance to this Ayah is that there were seemingly insurmountable knots in the life of Yusuf (Joseph) that only the power of Allah could penetrate and untie. Yusuf became like the Ka'bah, and his father, mother, and brothers prostrated towards him, in honor of him, but for the sake of Allah. «خرّوا له سجداً» (They fell down prostrating to him). If this prostration had been for other than Allah and constituted shirk (polytheism), Ya'qub (Jacob) and Yusuf, who are two Prophets of Allah, would not have witnessed such a reprehensible act. Perhaps this prostration was a sign of humility, not worship, in which case the prostration was to Yusuf and there was no objection.

Messages

  1. Regardless of your status, consider your parents superior to yourself. «رفع ابویه» (He raised his parents). The one who has endured greater hardship should be held dearer.
  2. Prophets have also sat upon the throne of governance. «على العرش» (upon the throne).
  3. Respect for rightful rulers and humility before them is necessary. «خرّوا له سجداً» (They fell down prostrating to him).
  4. The prostration of the father and brothers to Yusuf interpreted his dream. «رأیتهم لى ساجدین - خرّوا له سجّداً» (I saw them prostrating to me - They fell down prostrating to him).
  5. Allah is Wise. Sometimes He brings about the fulfillment of a prayer or the interpretation of a dream after many long years. «هذا تأویل رؤیاى من قبل» (This is the interpretation of my dream of old).
