  6. Bringing plans to reality is the work of Allah. «قد جعلها ربّى حقّاً» (My Lord has made it true). Indeed, Yusuf does not speak of his own steadfastness or patience; He attributes everything to the work of Allah.
  7. The dreams of the friends of Allah are true. «جعلها ربّى حقّاً» (My Lord has made it true).
  8. When dealing with intermediaries, tools, and means, we must always consider Allah as the Origin and the Overseer. Although various causes and means came together in Yusuf's life leading Him to this high status, He still says: «قد احسن بى» (He has been good to me).
  9. When meeting one another, do not mention the bitterness of the past. «احسن بى اذ اخرجنى من السجن» (He was good to me when He brought me out of the prison). Yusuf's first words to his father were thanks to Allah, not a recounting of hardships.
  10. Be chivalrous and do not hurt the feelings of your guest. (In the noble Ayah, Yusuf عليه السلام mentions being brought out of prison, but He does not speak of being brought out of the well, lest His brothers be ashamed.) «اذ اخرجنى من السجن» (when He brought me out of the prison).
  11. Be generous and chivalrous, not prone to resentment and revenge. Yusuf says: «نزغ الشیطان» (Satan stirred up), implying that His brothers are not inherently bad, but Satan tempted them.
  12. The friends of Allah view entering and exiting prison within the sphere of Divine Unity (Tawhid) and Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّ السّجن احب» (The prison is dearer to me than what they invite me to) (previous Ayahs) and «احسن بى ربّى اذ اخرجنى من السجن» (My Lord was good to me when He brought me out of the prison).
  13. The end of hardships is relief. «اخرجنى من السّجن» (He brought me out of the prison).
  14. Dwelling in the wilderness is a necessity, not a virtue. «قد احسن بى ربّى اذ... جاء بكم من البدو» (My Lord was good to me when... He brought you from the desert).
  15. The life of parents alongside their child is a Divine favor. «احسن بى ربّى... جاء بكم» (My Lord was good to me... He brought you).
  16. During the time of Prophet Yusuf, Egypt possessed urban civilization, while Canaan was a tribal land. «جاء بكم من البدو» (He brought you from the desert).*
  17. To achieve progress and a better life, one must travel. «جاء بكم من البدو» (He brought you from the desert).*
  18. The sincere servant (Mukhlas) sees everything as coming from Allah and does not complain about events. (In this Ayah, the phrases «جعلها ربّى», «احسن بى», «اخرجنى» and «جاء بكم» speak of Divine favors).*
  19. Brothers and members of a family must know that Satan seeks to sow discord among them. «من بعد أن نزغ الشیطان بینى و بین اخوتى» (after Satan had stirred up trouble between me and my brothers).
  20. Do not consider yourself superior. «بینى وبین اخوتى» (between me and my brothers). Yusuf did not say Satan deceived them; He says Satan [stirred up trouble] between me and them... meaning He places Himself on one side of the conflict as well.
  21. Just as Prophet Ya'qub told Yusuf in his childhood at the beginning of the story: «اِنّ الشیطان للانسان عدو مبین» (Indeed, Satan is a manifest enemy to man) (Ayah 5), Yusuf also says at the end: «مِن بعد ان نَزغ الشَّیطان بَینى و بین اخوَتى» (after Satan had stirred up trouble between me and my brothers).*
  22. The actions of Allah are accompanied by gentleness, forbearance, and grace. «انّ ربّى لطیف» (Indeed, my Lord is Subtle/Kind).
  23. All events, bitter and sweet, occur based on Divine knowledge and wisdom. «العلیم الحكیم» (the All-Knowing, the Wise).
  24. After forgiving someone, do not make them ashamed. Since Yusuf had forgiven His brothers, He did not mention the well when recounting the events, so that they would not be embarrassed.
  25. Prophet Ya'qub told Yusuf at the beginning of the story: «انّ ربّك علیم حكیم» (Indeed, your Lord is All-Knowing, Wise) (Ayah 6), and at the end, Yusuf عليه السلام also said: «انه هو العلیم الحكیم» (Indeed, He is the All-Knowing, the Wise), which is noteworthy.*

Ayah

۞ رَبِّ قَدْ ءَاتَيْتَنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُلْكِ وَعَلَّمْتَنِى مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ ۚ فَاطِرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ أَنتَ وَلِىِّۦ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ تَوَفَّنِى مُسْلِمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِى بِٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱٠۱)
‘My Lord! You have granted me a share in the kingdom, and taught me the interpretation of dreams. Originator of the heavens and earth! You are my guardian in this world and the Hereafter! Let my death be in submission [to You], and unite me with the Righteous.’ (101)

Key Points

When the friends of Allah look at their own honor and power, they immediately remember Allah and say: O God, everything we have is from You. Joseph did the same; he turned the conversation away from his father and focused on God. Allah granted the rule of Egypt to two people: one was Pharaoh, who attributed it to himself and said, «أليس لى ملك مصر» (Is not the dominion of Egypt mine?), and the other was Joseph, who attributed it to God and said, «آتيتنى من الملك» (You have given me of the sovereignty).

The thought process of Abraham manifests in his progeny and descendants; Abraham said: «اَسلمت لربّ العالمین» (I have submitted to the Lord of the worlds) (143). Then his grandson Jacob advised his children to die while in faith: «لاتموتنّ الاّ و انتم مسلمون» (And do not die except while you are Muslims) (144). Here, Jacob's son (Joseph) also asks God for death while in submission: «توفّنى مسلماً» (Cause me to die in submission). In any case, Abraham عليه السلام is among the righteous: «انّه فى الآخرة لمن الصّالحین» (Indeed, in the Hereafter, he will be among the righteous) (145), and Joseph عليه السلام wishes to join him: «الحقنى بالصّالحین» (Join me with the righteous).

Joseph, whom Allah always protected, granted him knowledge, gave him sovereignty, and kept danger away from him, is still concerned about his ultimate end. Woe to those who have acquired their power, wealth, and knowledge through deceit; what end will they have!

Allah taught Adam the names: «و علّم آدم الاسماء» (And He taught Adam the names) (146); He taught David the knowledge of making coats of mail: «و علّمناه صنعة لبوس» (And We taught him the making of coats of mail) (147); He taught Solomon the knowledge of the speech of birds: «عُلّمنا منطق الطیر» (We have been taught the language of birds) (148); He taught Joseph the knowledge of interpreting dreams: «علّمتنى من تأويل الاحاديث» (You have taught me the interpretation of dreams); and He taught the Prophet of Islam the knowledge of the first and the last generations: «و علّمك ما لم تكن تعلم» (And He taught you what you did not know) (149).

143) Baqarah, 131. 144) Baqarah, 132. 145) Baqarah, 130. 146) Baqarah, 31. 147) Anbiya, 80. 148) Naml, 16. 149) Nisa, 113.

Messages

1- Granting sovereignty is among the affairs of Divine Lordship. «ربّ قد آتيتنى من الملك»

2- We should not consider sovereignty the result of our own thought, wealth, power, helpers, or planning; rather, the Will of Allah is the main factor. «آتيتنى»

3- Whatever Allah gives to the righteous or takes away from them is for their training and refinement. «ربّ قد آتيتنى, ربّ السجن احب»

4- Sovereignty is the right of the knowledgeable, not the ignorant. «آتيتنى... علمّتنى» Joseph's knowledge became the means of his rule.

5- In every situation and condition, entrust yourself to Allah. «انت ولىّ فى الدنيا والاخرة»

6- Power, sovereignty, and politics are grounds for deviation from religion, unless Allah shows His favor. «توفّنى مسلماً» (Joseph had one prayer in the well and another in prison, but as soon as he attained sovereignty, his prayer was: O God, cause me to die as a Muslim.)

7- The servants of Allah, at the peak of honor and power, remember death, the Resurrection, and their ultimate end. «توفّنى مسلماً و الحقنى بالصالحین» Just as Pharaoh's wife, while in Pharaoh's palace, was thinking of the Hereafter and said: «ربّ ابن لى عندك بيتاً فى الجنّة» (My Lord, build for me near You a house in Paradise) (150).

8- The greatness of Allah is not only due to the blessings He has bestowed upon us, but also because He is the Originator of all existence. «فاطر السموات والارض»

9- Joseph's pride is not that he rules over the people; his pride is that Allah rules over him. «انت ولىّ فى الدنيا و الاخرة»

10- A good ultimate end and perseverance in good deeds are more important than the beginning of the deed. The Prophets prayed for a good ultimate end: «توفّنى مسلماً», meaning, keep me steadfast in my submission until death (151).

11- In prayer, first mention the Divine blessings: «ربّ قد آتيتنى», then present your request. «توفّنى مسلماً»

12- When you attain power, do not forget intimate supplication with Allah. «ربّ قد آتيتنى...»

13- Prophet Joseph عليه السلام turns to Allah and supplicates to Him during the most inspiring moments. «ربّ قد آتيتنى...»*

14- Knowledge and learning play an important role in correct and just governance. «آتيتنى من الملك و علّمتنى»*

15- In prayers and supplications, do not only think about this world and material matters. «فى الدنيا والاخرة»

16- Human power is meager: «من الملك»; human knowledge is meager: «من تأويل الاحاديث...»; but the sovereignty of Allah is over all existence. «فاطر السموات والارض»

17- The God who is the Creator of the heavens and the earth can save someone from all hardships and raise him to the highest ranks. «ربّ قد آتيتنى من الملك... فاطر السموات...»*

18- We should not be arrogant about our current faith; preserving faith until the end is important. «توفّنى مسلماً»*

19- The ultimate degree of faith is submission to Allah. «توفّنى مسلماً»*

20- A good ultimate end is the best blessing that Allah grants to His servants. «توفّنى مسلماً»*

21- No king remains forever. «توفّنى مسلماً»*

22- Dying with faith and being included among the righteous is the aspiration of the pure. «توفّنى مسلماً و الحقنى بالصالحین»

23- Noble individuals desire sovereignty for service and righteousness. «الحقنى بالصالحین»*

24- The righteous possess the highest status in the Hereafter. (Joseph aspires to join them) «الحقنى بالصالحین»*

Portrait of Joseph (Characteristics of a Successful Leader)

At the conclusion of the story of Prophet Joseph, we review a portrait of him:

  1. Complete attention to Allah during hardships: «ربّ السجن احبّ ...» and during joys and sweetness: «ربّ قد آتيتنى من الملك»

  2. Abandoning every path of deviation from every group: «انّى تركت ملّة قوم لایؤمنون باللَّه و هم بالاخرة هم كافرون»

  3. Following the straight path of the predecessors: «واتبعت ملّة آبائى ابراهیم... و الحقنى بالصالحین»

  4. Steadfastness in the way of Allah's pleasure until the last breath: «توفّنى مسلماً»

  5. Dignity in the face of rivals: «احبّ الى ابینا منّا»

  6. Patience in the face of events and bitterness: «یجعلوه فى غیابت الجُبّ, اراد باهلك سوء»

  7. Chastity and preferring piety over comfort: «معاذ اللّه, ربّ السّجن احبّ الىّ ممّا یدعوننى»

  8. Concealment (of his true status) before strangers: «و شروه بثمن بخس»

  9. Abundant knowledge: «علّمتنى من تأويل الاحاديث - انّى حفيظ عليم...»

  10. Eloquent and beautiful expression: «فلمّا كلمّه قال انّك الیوم لدینا مكین»

  11. Family nobility: «آبائى ابراهیم و اسحاق ...»

  12. Tolerance toward ideological opponents: «یا صاحبى السّجن»

  13. Sincerity (Ikhlas): «كان من المخلصین»

  14. Zeal and desire for the guidance of others: «ءارباب متفرّقون خير ام اللّه الواحد القهّار»

  15. Power of planning and innovation: «جعل السقایة, ائتونى باخ لكم, فذروه فى سنبله,...»

  16. Humility and modesty: «رفع ابویه على العرش»

  17. Pardon and forgiveness: «لا تثريب عليكم»

  18. Generosity and chivalry: «نزغ الشّیطان بينى و بين اخوتى»

  19. Trustworthiness: «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض انى حفيظ عليم»

  20. Hospitality: «انا خير المنزلين»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ أَجْمَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَمْكُرُونَ (۱٠۲)
These are accounts from the Unseen, which We reveal to you, and you were not with them when they conspired together and schemed. (102)

Messages

1- Prophets become acquainted with the unseen through revelation. «ذلك من انباء الغیب...»

2- Prophets do not know all unseen news. «من انباء الغیب»

3- Where Allah does not will, neither the people's decision «امرهم», nor their consensus «اجمعوا», nor their plotting and conspiracy «یمكرون» has any effect.

4- In successive and related events, do not forget the main point and the starting point. The core of the story of Yusuf was the conspiracy to destroy Yusuf. «اَجمعوا أمرهم و هم یمكرون»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَكْثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠۳)
Yet most people will not have faith, however eager you should be. (103)

Key Points

«حرص» (Intense Desire) means intense interest in something and effort to achieve it.

Messages

  1. The majority of people have repeatedly been criticized by the Quran regarding their religious beliefs. «و ما اكثر الناس... بمؤمنین» (And most of mankind are not believers.)

  2. Prophets possess fervor, pain, and eagerness regarding the guidance of others. «حرصت»

  3. Not every intense desire (ḥirṣ) is blameworthy. (The Prophet was intensely eager for the people to believe.) «حرصتَ»*

  4. The misunderstanding and disbelief of the majority of people should not prevent the propagation of religion and the declaration of truth. «ما اكثر الناس... بمؤمنین»*

  5. The disbelief of the majority of people is not due to the shortcomings of the Prophets, but rather the result of the free will and liberty of the people themselves, who chose not to believe. «ما اكثر النّاس ولو حرصت بمؤمنین»

Ayah

وَمَا تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠٤)
You do not ask them any reward for it: it is just a reminder for all the nations. (104)

Key Points

The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, like other prophets, never requested a reward from the people in exchange for guiding them. This is because expecting something from the people makes accepting the invitation burdensome. In Surah At-Tur, Ayah 40, we read: «ام تسئلهم اجراً فهم من مغرمٍ مثقلون» (Or do you ask them for a reward, so they are burdened by heavy debt?) If, in another Ayah, we see that the reward for the message is defined as affection for the near kin, «الاّ المودّة فى القرُبى »(152) that is because following and obeying the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet) is beneficial for the people themselves, not for the Prophet. For in another place we read: «قل و ما سئلتكم من اجر فهو لكم»(153) Indeed, whoever loves the Ahl al-Bayt obeys them, and obedience to them is obedience to the Prophet and to Allah.

The Quran is a Dhikr (a reminder/mention), because: It is a reminder of Allah’s signs, blessings, and attributes. It is a reminder of man's past and future. It is a reminder of the factors leading to the decline and honor of societies. It is a reminder of the scenes of the Day of Judgment. It is a reminder of the greatness of existence. It is a reminder of history and the lives of history-making personalities.

The teachings and rulings of the Quran are truths that must be learned and constantly kept in mind. This is because Dhikr refers to knowledge and awareness that is present in the mind and is not neglected.

152) Ash-Shura, 23. 153) Saba, 47.

Messages

1- The preacher should not have expectations from the people, just as the prophets did not. «و ما تسئلهم علیه من اجر»

2- What is objectionable is requesting a reward, not receiving one. «تسئل»

3- The teachings of the Quran are innate (fitrī), and all people can benefit from them. (Dhikr applies where a person knew something internally but has forgotten it).*

4- The task of the prophets is to remind and awaken the innate nature (fitrah). «ذكر للعالمین»*

5- The mission of the Prophet of Islam is universal. «للعالمین»

6- The failure of a group of people, even the majority, to believe in a certain time and place should not discourage or disappoint religious preachers. If a group does not believe in one region of the earth, they should preach elsewhere. «للعالمین»

Ayah

وَكَأَيِّن مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ يَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهَا وَهُمْ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضُونَ (۱٠٥)
How many a sign there is in the heavens and the earth that they pass by while they are disregardful of it! (105)

Key Points

It seems this Ayah is for comforting the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and every rightful leader and Imam, so that if people disregard their commands and instructions, they should not worry. They constantly encounter the signs of Allah's power and wisdom in nature and creation, but they do not reflect for a moment. They see all these earthquakes, solar eclipses, lunar eclipses, lightning, the rotation of stars, galaxies—all of them—but they turn away from them. The phrase «یمّرون علیها» (they pass by them) has been interpreted in three ways: 1. The passing of humans over the divine signs means observing them. 2. The passing of humans over the signs refers to the movement of the Earth, because with the Earth's movement, man passes over celestial bodies.(154) 3. Passing over the celestial signs is a prophecy of man riding spatial vehicles and their movement in the heavens.(155) Turning away (اعراض) is more dangerous than heedlessness (غفلت). Although the number of signs is great («كایّن») and man constantly interacts with them («یمرّون»), he not only neglects them but sometimes deliberately turns away from them.

154) Tafsir al-Mizan. 155) Hajj Travelogue of Ayatollah Safi.

Messages

1- The entire existence is a sign and a key to knowing Allah. «آیةٍ»

2- If man is obstinate, he will not accept any sign. «و كأیّن من آیة... یمرّون علیها و هم عنها معرضون»

3- Superficial looking, without thought and contemplation, is not a basis for guidance and growth. «یمرّون... معرضون»*

4- Knowledge alone is not sufficient; accepting the truth is also necessary for faith to be achieved. «یمرّون علیها و هم عنها معرضون»*

5- The story being the best of narratives (Ahsan al-Qasas) is not sufficient alone; what is important is the readiness to apply and accept all these great lessons. «و هم عنها معرضون»*

Ayah

وَمَا يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُم بِٱللَّهِ إِلَّا وَهُم مُّشْرِكُونَ (۱٠٦)
And most of them do not believe in Allah without ascribing partners to Him. (106)

Key Points

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Shirk (association) in this Ayah does not mean disbelief (kufr) and idol worship, but rather the intention/attention directed toward other than Allah. (156) It is also narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: Shirk in man is more hidden than the movement of a black ant on a black stone on a dark night. (157) Imam Baqir عليه السلام also said: People are monotheists (muwahhid) in worship, but they fall into shirk in obedience to other than God. (158) And in other narrations, we read that the meaning of shirk in this Ayah is the shirk of blessings (or gratitude). For example, when a person says, "So-and-so sorted out my affairs," or "If so-and-so hadn't been there, I would have been ruined," and similar statements. (159)

156) Tafsir-e Namuneh. 157) Safinat al-Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 697. 158) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 292. 159) Tafsir-e Namuneh.

Messages

  1. Faith (Iman) has levels, and pure faith, which contains no shirk whatsoever, is rare. «و ما یؤمن... الا و هم مشركون»

Signs of the Sincere Believer

  1. In spending (charity): «لانرید منكم جزاء و لاشكوراً» (160) He does not expect reward or thanks from anyone.
  2. In worship: «و لا یشرك بعبادة ربّه احداً» (161) He worships no one but Allah.
  3. In propagation (Dawah): «إن اجرى الاّ على اللَّه» (162) He seeks reward from no one except Allah.
  4. In marriage: «ان یكونوا فقراء یغنهم اللَّه من فضله» (163) He does not fear poverty and marries relying on the promise of God.
  5. In dealing with people: «قل اللَّه ثم ذرهم» (164) He sets everything aside except His pleasure.
  6. In war and confronting the enemy: «و لایخشون احداً الا اللَّه» (165) He fears no one except Allah.
  7. In affection and love: «والّذین آمنوا اشدّ حبّاً للَّه» (166) He loves no one as much as Allah.
  8. In trade and business: «رجال لاتلهیهم تجارة و لابیع عن ذكراللَّه» (167) He is not distracted from the remembrance of Allah.

Signs of the Associating Believer

  1. He seeks honor from others: «أیبتغون عندهم العزّة» (168)
  2. In action: «خلطوا عملاً صالحاً وآخر سیّئاً» (169) He mixes righteous deeds with unrighteous ones.
  3. In dealing with others: «كلّ حزب بما لدیهم فرحون» (170) He falls into partisan and group fanaticism.
  4. In worship: «الّذین هم عن صلاتهم ساهون . الّذین هم یرائون» (171) He shows heedlessness and ostentation.
  5. In war and battle: «یخشون النّاس كخشیة اللَّه» (172) He fears people like the fear of Allah.
  6. In trade and worldly affairs: «اَلْهاكم التَّكاثُر» (173) Excessive accumulation distracts him.
  7. In choosing between religion and the world: «و اذا رأوا تجارة او لهواً انفضّوا الیها و تركوك قائماً» (174) They seize the world and leave the Prophet standing alone.

Ayah

أَفَأَمِنُوٓا۟ أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ غَـٰشِيَةٌ مِّنْ عَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْ تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱٠۷)
Do they feel secure from being overtaken by a blanket punishment from Allah, or being overtaken by the Hour, suddenly, while they are unaware? (107)

Key Points

"Ghāshiyah" (غاشیه) means a punishment that encompasses a society or an individual.

Messages

1- No one should consider himself safe (or guaranteed security). «أفآمنوا»

2- The possibility of Divine wrath is sufficient for man to move towards the path of truth; the problem is that some do not even entertain this possibility. «أفآمنوا»

3- The wrath of Allah is comprehensive, and escape is impossible. «غاشیة»

4- A portion of the torment is sufficient to afflict man. «غاشیة من عذاب»

5- Remembering the Resurrection (Qiyāmah) is a factor in spiritual development. «تاتیَهم السّاعة»

Ayah

قُلْ هَـٰذِهِۦ سَبِيلِىٓ أَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ عَلَىٰ بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا۠ وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَنِى ۖ وَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱٠۸)
Say, ‘This is my way. I summon to Allah with insight—I and he who follows me. Immaculate is Allah, and I am not one of the polytheists.’ (108)

Key Points

The caller to Tawhid (Monotheism) is different from the masses, just as we mentioned in the two previous verses that the faith of the masses is often tainted with polytheism: «و ما یؤمن اكثرهم باللَّه الاّ و هم مشركون» (And most of them do not believe in Allah except while they associate others with Him). But the heavenly messenger must be able to say: «و ما انا من المشركین» (And I am not of the polytheists).

Messages

1- The path of the Prophets is clear and exposed to the knowledge and sight of everyone. «هذه سبیلى»

2- Those who traverse the path of truth must state and declare their positions clearly and without fear. «هذه سبیلى»*

3- The leader's call must be towards Allah, not towards himself. «ادعوا الى اللَّه»

4- The leader must possess complete insight (Basirah). «على بصیرة»

5- People should not be encouraged to do something blindly and without awareness. «على بصیرة»*

6- The followers of the Prophet must each be a preacher (Muballigh) who invites people towards Allah with insight and awareness. «ادعوا الى اللَّه... اَنَا و من اتّبعنى»

7- The axis of propagation (Tabligh) is the purification (Tanzih) of Allah from every kind of polytheism (Shirk) and partner. «سبحان اللَّه»

8- Religious preachers must be sincere and purified individuals (Mukhlis). «ما أنا من المشركین»

9- Monotheism (Tawhid) and the negation of polytheism (Shirk) constitute the foundation of the religion of Islam. «ادعوا الى اللَّه، ما أنا من المشركین»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِىٓ إِلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ ۗ أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۗ وَلَدَارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۱٠۹)
We did not send [any apostles] before you except as men from among the people of the towns, to whom We revealed. Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? And the abode of the Hereafter is surely better for those who are Godwary. Do you not exercise your reason? (109)

Key Points

Opponents of the prophets often made excuses, asking why the prophets were human beings just like them. It seems that the people during the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله also had this thought and question, to which this Ayah both responds and warns.

Messages

1- All prophets were men. «و ما ارسلنا من قبلك الاّ رجالاً» Perhaps this is because the possibility for propagation, migration, and effort is greater for men.

2- The knowledge of the prophets was through revelation (Wahy) and, in technical terms, ladunni (divinely granted). «نوحى الیهم»

3- The prophets were of the same kind as the people and lived among them. (They were neither angels, nor recluses, nor people of luxury.) «من اهل القُرى»

4- Travel and journey must be purposeful. «أفلم یسیروا ... فینظروا»

5- Direct observation is one of the most effective methods for perceiving the truth. «أفلم یسیروا... فینظروا»*

6- Traveling the earth, gaining awareness of history, and taking lessons (of warning/admonition) are highly effective for guidance and training. «فینظروا»

7- Preserving ancient monuments is necessary for the admonition and visitation of future generations. «فینظروا»

8- The sending of prophets, the descent of revelation, and the destruction of their stubborn opponents are all among the Divine traditions (Sunan) in history. «كیف كان عاقبة الّذین من قبلهم»

9- The disbelievers gain nothing from opposing the prophets; in this world, they are afflicted by wrath and punishment. But the people of piety (Taqwa) attain the Hereafter, which is better than this world. «و لدار الاخرة خیرٌ»

10- Engaging human intellect and thought is among the goals of the mission of the prophets and the Quran. «أفلا تعقلون»*

11- Wisdom and reflection lead man toward the school of the prophets. «أفلا تعقلون»

Ayah

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا ٱسْتَيْـَٔسَ ٱلرُّسُلُ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كُذِبُوا۟ جَآءَهُمْ نَصْرُنَا فَنُجِّىَ مَن نَّشَآءُ ۖ وَلَا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُنَا عَنِ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱۱٠)
When the apostles lost hope and they thought that they had been told lies, Our help came to them, and We delivered whomever We wished, and Our punishment will not be averted from the guilty lot. (110)

Key Points

Throughout history, the Prophets were continuous and insistent in their call, until they despaired of guiding the people, just as the stubborn opponents did not cease their resistance. We read examples of this in the Quran:

A: Example of the Prophets' Despair: After Noah invited the people for many years, no one believed except a small group. Allah told him: «لن یؤمن من قومك الاّ من قد آمن»(175) (No one else of your people will believe except those who have already believed.) Noah, in his curse, which also indicates his despair, says: «و لایلدوا الاّ فاجراً كفّاراً»(176) (Meaning, none will be born from them except wicked disbelievers.) This despair regarding the belief of the disbelievers is also evident in the life stories and calls of Hud, Salih, Shu'ayb, Moses, and Jesus علیهم السلام.

B: Example of People's Suspicion towards the Prophets: The disbelievers considered the Prophets' threats empty and false. In Surah Hud, Ayah 27, we read: «بل نظنّكم كاذبین»(177) (We think you are liars.) Or when Pharaoh said to Moses علیه السلام: «انّى لاظنّك یا موسى مسحوراً»(178) (Indeed, I think you, O Moses, are bewitched.)

C: Example of Allah's Help: The Quran considers Divine help a right that Allah has made incumbent upon Himself: «و كان حقّاً علینا نصر المؤمنین»(179) (Meaning, helping the believers is incumbent upon Us.) Or in another place, He says: «نجّینا هوداً و الّذین آمنوا معه»(180) (We saved Hud and those who believed with him.) But regarding Allah's wrath, which is not turned back from the criminals, in Surah Ar-Ra'd, Ayah 11, He says: «اذا اراد اللَّه بقوم سوءً فلامردّ له» (When Allah intends ill for a people, there is no turning it back.)

175) Hud, 36. 176) Noah, 27. 177) Hud, 27. 178) Al-Isra, 101. 179) Ar-Rum, 47. 180) Hud, 58.

Messages

  1. Hard-heartedness and stubbornness in man escalate to the point that they cause even the patient Prophets to despair. «اذا اسْتیئَس الرسل»
  2. Optimism, good intention, and patience have limits. «حتّى»
  3. Do not expend your energy on impenetrable grounds. One must give up on some people. «استیئس الرُّسل»
  4. Granting respite to criminals and delaying their punishment is among the Divine traditions. «حتّى اذا استیئس» (Meaning, We granted the criminals respite until the Prophets despaired.)
  5. The delay of Divine punishment causes criminals to become emboldened and to deny the truth. «حتّى اذا... وظنّوا انّهم قد كذبوا»
  6. The despair of the Prophets regarding the guidance of the people is a condition for the descent of Allah's wrath. «اذا استیئس... لایرد بأسنا...»
  7. Divine aid for the Prophets also has a specific timing. «اذا استیئس... جاءهم»
  8. Divine wrath does not include the Prophets and true believers. «فنُجّى»
  9. Both wrath and punishment, and grace and aid, are in the hand of Allah. «نصرنا ... بأسنا»
  10. The destiny of man, whether wrath or salvation, is in his own hands. «من نشاء، مجرمین»
  11. Allah's will and desire are governed by law. «من نشاء ولایردّ بأسنا عن القوم المجرمین»
  12. The path of Allah has no dead ends. «اذا استیئس الرسل...جاءهم نصرنا» (Wherever people bring matters to a dead end, the power of Allah manifests itself.)
  13. No power can prevent Allah's wrath. «لایردّ بأسنا»
  14. The tradition of Allah is to support the Prophets and destroy the criminals. «جاءهم نصرنا ، لایُرّد بأسنا»

Ayah

لَقَدْ كَانَ فِى قَصَصِهِمْ عِبْرَةٌ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ ۗ مَا كَانَ حَدِيثًا يُفْتَرَىٰ وَلَـٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَتَفْصِيلَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۱۱)
There is certainly a moral in their accounts for those who possess intellect. This [Quran] is not a fabricated discourse; rather, it is a confirmation of what was [revealed] before it, and an elaboration of all things, and a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith. (111)

Key Points

‘Ibrah (عبرت) and Ta'bir (تعبیر) mean passage (or crossing), the passage from one scene to another. Ta'bir-e khwab (Interpretation of dreams) is the passage from dreams to realities. And ‘Ibrah means the passage from visible and audible things to invisible and inaudible things. "Qasasihim" (قصصهم - their stories) might refer to the stories of all the prophets, or perhaps the intention is the story of Joseph (Yusuf), Jacob (Ya'qub), the brothers, the Aziz of Egypt, and the bitter and sweet events that were narrated in this Surah.

Messages

1- The condition for the distinction of stories is their instructive nature. At the beginning of the Surah, He said: «نحن نقصّ علیك احسن القصص» (We relate to you the best of stories), and at the end, He said: «لقد كان فى قصصهم عبرة» (There was certainly a lesson in their stories).

2- Just as Prophet Joseph عليه السلام attained honor and power despite all the plots, difficulties, and obstacles, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله will also attain honor and power despite all the schemes and... «لقد كان فى قصصهم عبرة»*

3- Only the wise take admonition and lessons from the stories. «عبرة لاولى الالباب»

4- Taking lessons from the stories of the Quran is not restricted to one time period. «لاولى الالباب»

5- The stories of the Quran express objective and instructive realities. (They are found, not fabricated.) «ما كان حدیثاً یفترى»

6- True and factual speech has a profound effect. «عبرة... ما كان حدیثاً یفترى»

7- The Quran is consistent with other heavenly books. «تصدیق الّذى...»

8- The Quran addresses all human needs. «تفصیل كلّ شى ءٍ»

9- The Quran is pure guidance and is not mixed with any error. «هدىً»

10- Only the people of faith benefit from the guidance and mercy of the Quran. «هدىً و رحمةً لقوم یؤمنون»

11- Discernment and taking lessons require intellect; «عبرة لاولى الالباب» but receiving divine light and mercy also requires faith. «لقوم یؤمنون»

12- The story of Joseph is a sign for seekers of truth, «آیات للسائلین» a lesson for the wise, «عبرة لاولى الالباب» and a source of guidance and mercy for the people of faith. «هدىً و رحمةً لقوم یؤمنون»*
